[Subscription & billing](../collections/11438803-subscription-billing.html)

How are credits calculated?

# How are credits calculated?

Credits are calculated based on the amount and cost of the data you are
scraping, extracting, or monitoring.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 5 months ago

Table of contents

Each plan provides you with a specific number of monthly or annual credits.
Credits are calculated based on your usage and cost:

  * The amount of data you scrape, extract or monitor.

  * The cost of extracting data from that website.

# How are credits calculated?

## Standard sites

  * Minimum cost per task = 1 credit

  * 10 rows of data = 1 credit

  * 1 screenshot = 1 credit

  * 10 single captured text = 1 credit

## Premium sites

[Premium sites](10523683-what-are-premium-sites.html) cost more to extract,
scrape and monitor due to additional bot prevention tactics and site security.

  * Minimum cost per task = 2 to 10 credits

  * 10 rows of data = 2 to 10 credits

  * 1 screenshot = 2 to 10 credits

  * 10 single captured text = 2 to 10 credits 

# What is a task?

A task is a single execution of your robot, so every time your robot visits a
website to scrape, monitor extract data or capture screenshots - it is
executing a task.

Different types of tasks include:

  * Extracting data from a list page.

  * Capturing data from a detail page.

  * Taking screenshots of a webpage.

  * Checking for changes during a monitoring run.

  * Running a robot as part of a workflow.

# How does your pricing work?

We offer different [plans](https://www.browse.ai/pricing) based on the amount
of data and use case.

  * Free plan is great to get started and includes 2 websites and 50 credits monthly. 

  * Personal plans offer 5 websites and up to 24,000 credits annually. 

  * Professional plans include 10 websites and up to 360,000 credits annually. 

If you're looking to scrape, extract or monitor a large volume of data or from
a large number of websites our [Premium Plan](https://www.browse.ai/managed-
web-scraping) offers customized volume pricing.

# How do my usage costs scale?

As you scale with Browse AI your cost per credit decreases this means that in
our highest tiered plan, you'll be paying our lowest cost per credit.

# Can I add additional credits to my plan?

Yes - you can add additional credits to any plan. The cost is based on your
plan. For details please see our [pricing
page](https://www.browse.ai/pricing).

# Do my credits roll over?

No. Credits reflect your usage limit within your billing period (either
monthly or annual). One of the benefits of an annual plan is you get you get
all credits upfront, meaning that you have an annual limit of usage vs. having
to manage usage monthly.

# Credit usage examples

## Example 1: Extracting a list of products

If you extract a list of 50 products from a single page:

  * Credit calculation: 50 rows × 0.1 credit per row = 5 credits.

  * You'll use 5 credits for this task.

## Example 2: Extracting a small list

If you extract a list of 8 products:

  * Credit calculation: 8 rows × 0.1 credit per row = 0.8 credits.

  * Since this is below the minimum, you'll use 1 credit.

## Example 3: Extracting from detail pages

If you need data from 20 individual product pages:

  * Each page requires a separate task.

  * Credit calculation: 20 tasks × 1 credit minimum = 20 credits.

  * You'll use 20 credits for this extraction.

## Example 4: Deep scraping (lists + details)

If you extract a list of 100 product links and then visit each product page:

  * List extraction: 100 rows × 0.1 credit = 10 credits.

  * Detail page extraction: 100 pages × 1 credit = 100 credits.

  * Total: 110 credits.

## Example 5: Premium site extraction

If you extract 45 items from a Premium site (with a minimum cost of 5
credits):

  * Credit calculation: 45 rows × 0.1 credit = 4.5 credits.

  * Since the Premium minimum is higher, you'll use 5 credits.

## Example 6: Monitoring websites

If you monitor 10 product detail pages for changes every 3 days:

  * Each check runs as a separate task: 10 pages × 1 credit = 10 credits per check.

  * Monthly usage: 10 credits × (30 days ÷ 3 days) = 100 credits per month.

* * *

[Security & data](../collections/11438799-security-data.html)

What are your rights on data that you scrape?

# What are your rights on data that you scrape?

Web data ownership depends on source website terms - always check terms of use
before scraping to ensure legal compliance.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 12 months ago

Table of contents

In this article, we will explore the importance of data ownership and
copyright when using web scraping applications.  
​

# Understanding web data ownership

When you use Browse AI to extract data from websites, you're accessing the
same information you'd see in your web browser. However, this doesn't
automatically give you ownership rights to that data. The website's terms of
service, copyright laws, and other regulations determine what you can legally
do with the extracted information.

# Before you start scraping

Here are key steps to take before extracting data from any website:

  * Check the terms of service: look for specific clauses about automated access or data scraping.

  * Review the robots.txt file: this file indicates which parts of a site can be accessed by bots.

  * Consider rate limiting: implement reasonable delays between requests to avoid overwhelming the server.

  * Respect personal data regulations: be aware of GDPR, CCPA, and other privacy laws when collecting personal information.

  * Document your compliance efforts: keep records of your due diligence steps.

## Common permissible uses

While each website's terms vary, these uses are often allowed:

  * Extracting publicly available factual data that isn't protected by copyright.

  * Personal research and analysis.

  * Collecting pricing information for comparison purposes.

  * Monitoring your own content across websites.

## Uses that often require permission

These activities typically require explicit permission:

  * Republishing substantial portions of the website's content.

  * Using the data for commercial purposes that compete with the original website.

  * Creating derivative works based on copyrighted content.

  * Mass extraction that impacts the website's performance.

## Respecting website owners

Remember that websites invest significant resources to create and maintain
their content. Ethical scraping means:

  * Only collecting the data you genuinely need.

  * Identifying your scraper appropriately in request headers.

  * Following any cease and desist requests promptly.

  * Being transparent about your data collection practices.

# Why Compliance Matters

  1. Legal and Ethical Responsibility: respecting website policies is not only a legal requirement but also an ethical responsibility. It ensures fair and responsible data usage.

  2. Protecting Your Reputation: maintaining a trustworthy and professional image is crucial in business. Complying with website policies helps protect your reputation.

  3. Mitigating Risks: non-compliance can lead to legal consequences and damage to your business relationships. Staying on the right side of the law is essential.

# When to seek legal advice

Consider consulting with a legal professional if:

  * You're scraping at a large scale for commercial purposes.

  * The data includes personal or sensitive information.

  * You're uncertain about copyright implications.

  * The website has complex or ambiguous terms of service.

  * You're operating across multiple legal jurisdictions.

# How Browse AI helps

Browse AI is designed to be a responsible tool for data extraction. We provide
features like:

  * Intelligent rate limiting to prevent server overload.

  * Geolocation-based extraction that respects regional access rules.

  * Secure and compliant data handling processes.

However, as the user, you remain responsible for ensuring your specific use
case complies with all applicable laws and website policies.

While Browse AI empowers you to extract valuable data from websites, always
remember that website terms of service and relevant laws govern what you can
do with that data. By taking a thoughtful, ethical approach to web scraping,
you can benefit from publicly available information while respecting the
rights of content creators.

What are robots and what can they do?

# What are robots and what can they do?

Robots scrape, extract, and monitor website data with no coding required.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 2 months ago

Table of contents

You can train a robot to scrape, extract and monitor almost any content on a
website. Here's a full overview of what they are able to scrape and monitor.

# Scrape and extract data from any website

Robots can scrape and extract almost any content from almost any web page.

This includes:

  * Scraping a [list of items](10477709-capture-text-from-a-list-vs-just-text.html) on a page

  * Extracting and structuring [specific text from a page](10477709-capture-text-from-a-list-vs-just-text.html)

  * Take screenshots of entire pages or selected areas

  * Extract [data from behind login screens](10523650-can-i-extract-data-from-a-site-that-needs-login.html)

  * Scrape dynamic content on a web page

  * [Extract content that's triggered by a search or a form](10453929-how-to-set-up-and-use-input-parameters.html)

  * Extracting data across multiple connected pages with a [workflow](10518596-workflows-how-to-connect-robots-together-to-scrape-across-multiple-pages.html)

# Extracting dynamic content from a web page

You can train your robot to mimic human behavior to handle complex website
interactions including:

  * [Scrape data behind a login](10523650-can-i-extract-data-from-a-site-that-needs-login.html) using secure credentials

  * [Form filling](10497039-can-my-robot-fill-out-a-form-or-perform-an-action-before-extracting-data.html) to scrape output data (including search results)

  * Click buttons, toggles, and drop downs to scrape dynamic content

  * [Pagination](10509840-capture-text-how-to-set-up-pagination-when-extracting-data-from-a-list.html) and infinite scrolling

  * [Dynamic URL parameter handling](10453933-bulk-run-how-to-use-bulk-runs-to-extract-data-from-multiple-urls.html)

  * Basic [CAPTCHA](11090134-how-to-extract-data-on-a-page-that-has-captchas.html) solving

  * [Geographic](12271191-does-browse-ai-offer-location-based-extraction-for-region-dynamic-content.html) and/or regional based content

# Monitoring for website changes

You can train any robot to check for content changes on a custom schedule and
alert you to what has changed.

This includes the ability to:

  * [Keep your data up to date with scheduled monitoring](10453923-how-to-use-monitors-to-automate-data-extraction.html)

  * Monitor specific pages or data points

  * [Send notifications when changes occur](10524268-how-to-set-up-monitoring-notifications.html)

  * Track historical data changes

# Bulk automations and workflows

Once you train a robot to scrape or monitor data, you can easily scale by:

  * [Scrape up to 500,000 URLs at once](10477518-bulk-run-how-to-execute-bulk-tasks.html)

  * [Set up monitoring across thousands of pages](10534748-how-to-set-up-monitors-on-multiple-urls-at-once-bulk-monitor.html)

  * [Bulk scraping by uploading a list of pages to scrape](10453933-bulk-run-how-to-use-bulk-runs-to-extract-data-from-multiple-urls.html)

  * [Scrape pages and subpages automatically](10553465-how-to-extract-data-across-multiple-pages-deep-scraping.html)

  * [Creating workflows to scrape entire websites](10459476-workflows-how-can-i-create-a-workflow-connecting-two-robots.html)

  * [Scrape based on a list of search inputs](10518579-how-to-perform-a-custom-search-using-bulk-run.html)

# Reliability and scale

Browse AI's platform is designed to reliably scrape and monitor data at scale
with features designed to deliver accurate data you can rely on.

  * When websites change, robots are trained to adapt the code so that your data doesn't break.

  * Built in bot evasion in our core platform which includes IP address management, proxy management, rate limiting and more. 

  * Automated error recovery and retries with performance optimization. 

# Integrate or export your scraped data

## Export scraped data

  * [Download data directly as CSV files](10593816-how-to-export-data-from-tables.html)

  * [Auto-sync to Google Sheets](10535898-how-to-sync-scraped-data-with-google-sheets.html)

  * [Sync to an AWS S3 bucket](10715592-integrating-browse-ai-with-amazon-s3.html)

  * [Sync data to Airtable](10537018-how-to-enable-airtable-integration.html)

  * [Turn any website into an API](12683249-api-guide-getting-started.html)

  * [Download structured JSON files](10593816-how-to-export-data-from-tables.html)

  * [Set up webhooks for real-time updates](12683642-webhooks-set-up-guide.html)

## Integrate with 7,000+ apps and tools

  * [Zapier for automated workflows](10536328-how-to-connect-browse-ai-with-zapier.html)

  * [Pabbly Connect for seamless integrations](10537045-how-to-integrate-browse-ai-with-pabbly-connect.html)

  * [Make.com for complex automation scenarios](10573987-how-to-integrate-your-robot-with-make-com.html)

What websites work with Browse AI?

# What websites work with Browse AI?

Browse AI works with most websites, though some technical limitations may
affect functionality. Here's what you need to know about website
compatibility.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 12 months ago

Table of contents

# What websites work well

## Custom robots

Browse AI successfully extracts data from (almost) any websites, including:

  * E-commerce platforms

  * Business directories

  * Social media sites

  * News and content websites

  * Real estate listings

  * ...and billions more.

Our success rate for newly created custom robots is over 90%.

## Prebuilt robots

Some of our [prebuilt robots](https://www.browse.ai/prebuilt-robots) are
specifically designed to solve for limitations that our no code platform has
out of the box with popular sites.

## Managed services

While some sites can't be scraped out of the box, some require custom code,
configuration or data transformation. We offer all of these services as part
of our [managed services](https://www.browse.ai/premium) offering.

# Current limitations

## Custom CAPTCHAs

We support solving several types of standard CAPTCHA (e.g., ReCaptcha,
hCaptcha), but not all of them. For example, we currently do not solve custom
captchas.

## Expert bot detection

Some sites have _really_ strong bot detection mechanisms. While our platform
tries to mimic human behavior, and has bot evasion built-in - for some sites
that have high security measures this doesn't work.

This is especially true for high security sites that require a login to access
the web data.

Here's why:

  * Browse AI tries to mimic human behaviour and uses a browser session just like a regular user, with the same delays, pauses, and scrolling. 

  * We also rotate through IP addresses (usually located in the same country as the user who set up the robot) and almost never use the same IP address twice.

  * For most websites this is enough - but if your robot has to log into the website (either using your session cookies or your login credentials), the website can always detect two things:

    * This user is logging in from at least two different IP addresses (your local IP address and Browse AI's IPs)

    * If your robot is running a lot of tasks, it can seem suspicious.

## MFA (multi-factor authentication) and 2FA (two-factor authentication)

Note that neither of these are supported by Browse AI.

# Technical considerations

## Sites rendering virtual lists

Some websites use virtual lists that only render visible content during
scrolling. This is not currently supported by Browse AI.

## iFrames

Direct interaction with iFrames isn't currently supported. However, you can
often work around this by:

  1. Locating the iFrame's direct URL

  2. Creating a separate robot for that URL

  3. Extracting data from the direct source

## Tips for success

  * Test your robot thoroughly before scaling

  * Use the "Report" button if you encounter issues

  * Consider our managed services for complex extraction needs

  * Monitor your extraction frequency to avoid triggering security measures

* * *

configuration.html)

What is the 'Origin URL'?

# What is the 'Origin URL'?

The starting URL for robot training and a parameter for scaling robots across
multiple similar pages after they're created.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 4 months ago

Table of contents

The Origin URL serves two important purposes: it's the starting point when you
train a new robot, and it becomes a flexible parameter for scaling that robot
across similar pages.

## **Origin URL during robot training**

When you create a new robot, the Origin URL field is the first thing you'll
see in Robot Studio. This is where you tell Browse AI which webpage to use for
training.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1803911752/36471a44f362d20d7a6dd6f0a6dd/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image607f.png?expires=1771546500&signature=360769ea07e105406684a6bf0298ddd53bc21ca03cbd41297dca91300c273b93&req=dSgnFcB%2FnIZaW%2FMW1HO4zRtaETq9c1JEjGKnbZCcbhPU0oMd5%2FssDZOotDze%0A4A0ANzkNAyxJq%2BgpM8k%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1803911752/36471a44f362d20d7a6dd6f0a6dd/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image607f.png?expires=1771546500&signature=360769ea07e105406684a6bf0298ddd53bc21ca03cbd41297dca91300c273b93&req=dSgnFcB%2FnIZaW%2FMW1HO4zRtaETq9c1JEjGKnbZCcbhPU0oMd5%2FssDZOotDze%0A4A0ANzkNAyxJq%2BgpM8k%3D%0A)

**What to enter:** Enter the exact webpage URL where your target data is
currently displayed. Choose a page that gets you as close as possible to the
data you want to extract.

**Good Origin URL choices for training:**

  * A specific product page: `https://store.com/products/laptop-model-x`

  * An individual company profile: `https://directory.com/companies/airbnb`

  * A particular search results page: `https://jobs.com/search?role=engineer&location=nyc`

  * A detailed listing page: `https://marketplace.com/listings/vintage-guitar-123`

Starting close to your target data makes training faster and creates more
reliable robots.

## **Origin URL as an input parameter**

After your robot is trained (and approved), the Origin URL becomes an input
parameter that you can adjust to reuse the same robot across multiple similar
pages.

### **How to scale your robot**

  1. Open your trained robot's settings.

  2. Update the Origin URL parameter to point to a different page with the same layout.

  3. Run the robot on the new page.

Scaling examples: If you trained a robot on one company profile, you can
extract data from other company profiles by changing the Origin URL:

  * Original training URL: `https://directory.com/companies/airbnb`

  * Scale to: `https://directory.com/companies/uber`

  * Scale to: `https://directory.com/companies/stripe`

### **Ways to scale using Origin URL:**

  * [Change the Origin URL in robot settings for individual runs](10453731-how-to-set-up-and-run-tasks.html)

  * [Upload a CSV file with multiple Origin URLs using Bulk Run](10595234-import-csv-for-bulk-data-extraction.html)

  * [Create a workflow to generate a list of Origin URLs to scrape](10553465-how-to-extract-data-across-multiple-pages-deep-scraping.html)

  * [Use API calls to programmatically bulk scrape a list of Origin URLs](12683563-api-guide-bulk-operations-and-large-scale-data-extraction.html)

  
​

How to set up and run tasks

# How to set up and run tasks

Tasks are the core way your robots extract data.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 9 months ago

Table of contents

Each time a robot runs, it creates a task that collects data based on how
you've trained it. Think of tasks as individual data extraction jobs that your
robots complete.

# How tasks work

Every time you run a robot, it creates a new task. The results from each task
are automatically stored in your robot's History tab, making it easy to track
and manage your data extraction activities.

## Manual: How to create tasks manually

This is the simplest way to create a tasks. You can set them up individually,
or you can set up to 500,000 tasks at once.

### How to set up an individual task manually

  1. Select a robot from your dashboard

  2. Go to the Run Task tab

  3. Click to run the task

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1389446484/03a2c73abc51ee8ec9679382caf2/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image041f.png?expires=1771546500&signature=671979c91e37cee515b087fd0975a4afd6892b90a0da9f86c1f6e53f7022ab3d&req=dSMvH816m4VXXfMW1HO4zRAsQmR483ZkYxsDKxqOe6cUsHRCzXp8kUNWvA%2BL%0A31rUKpdvtLTdjPBp%2Fm0%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1389446484/03a2c73abc51ee8ec9679382caf2/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image041f.png?expires=1771546500&signature=671979c91e37cee515b087fd0975a4afd6892b90a0da9f86c1f6e53f7022ab3d&req=dSMvH816m4VXXfMW1HO4zRAsQmR483ZkYxsDKxqOe6cUsHRCzXp8kUNWvA%2BL%0A31rUKpdvtLTdjPBp%2Fm0%3D%0A)

### How to set up multiple tasks manually (in bulk)

For larger data extraction needs, you can use the Bulk Run feature to process
up to 500,000 tasks at once by uploading a CSV file.

![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1378045691/fea248433eaf9fb5ac6fa615c90b/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image1d0c.png?expires=1771632000&signature=defab3dd87fb6d5cfafef62dc101981f4b8dff3d5e5cba54fdaa69d18d325710&req=dSMgHsl6mIdWWPMW3Hu4gcS9XjkIcJcWDA%2F8osL5RHzKfUVOrSmHtx0gWNsD%0A5w%3D%3D%0A)
**See:**[What does the "Bulk Run" feature do?](https://intercom.help/browse-
ai-089fe61c5dc3/en/articles/10477518-what-does-the-bulk-run-feature-do)

## Automated: How to create tasks automatically (monitoring)

When you set up a robot to monitor a webpage, it automatically creates tasks
based on your schedule.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1389446856/1ac4d0a0bcda16160784960c882a/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imageb4d9.png?expires=1771546500&signature=2c31a58bb5b6514c1160df7bf89b21231bf215f845cef895b233859ce02d81d5&req=dSMvH816m4laX%2FMW1HO4zQoHbcMuj9LFuA5JfFhabgfe4V099wN60GB6LauV%0AqZG6MwUqOuxoZ%2Fj9Vus%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1389446856/1ac4d0a0bcda16160784960c882a/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imageb4d9.png?expires=1771546500&signature=2c31a58bb5b6514c1160df7bf89b21231bf215f845cef895b233859ce02d81d5&req=dSMvH816m4laX%2FMW1HO4zQoHbcMuj9LFuA5JfFhabgfe4V099wN60GB6LauV%0AqZG6MwUqOuxoZ%2Fj9Vus%3D%0A)

  
For example, if you configure daily monitoring, your robot will create and run
one task each day to check for changes.

## API: How to set up API triggered tasks

For advanced automation, you can create tasks programmatically using our API.

This allows you to integrate Browse AI with your existing tools and workflows.
Find detailed instructions in our API documentation.

![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1378045691/fea248433eaf9fb5ac6fa615c90b/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image1d0c.png?expires=1771632000&signature=defab3dd87fb6d5cfafef62dc101981f4b8dff3d5e5cba54fdaa69d18d325710&req=dSMgHsl6mIdWWPMW3Hu4gcS9XjkIcJcWDA%2F8osL5RHzKfUVOrSmHtx0gWNsD%0A5w%3D%3D%0A)
**See:**[API Documentation](https://www.browse.ai/docs/api/v2)

## Integrations: How to set up integration triggered tasks

If you've set up an [integration](10459396-integrations-how-to-send-extracted-
data-to-other-tools-and-apps.html) with your robot, depending on what tool
you've integrated with and if you've set up an automation that will trigger a
task.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1378049121/ac773041e774e9d808b5e439d770/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagef372.png?expires=1771546500&signature=3d35f7990d36b82e7a4356cf273162ebf5dcd4a01598efe7e5e06ff675084e02&req=dSMgHsl6lIBdWPMW1HO4zdGayWcB0cZzPrskjnUGQkaiRVFS5ZqmQalaxwAM%0AqLjOEBNDeis4wkHYISM%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1378049121/ac773041e774e9d808b5e439d770/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagef372.png?expires=1771546500&signature=3d35f7990d36b82e7a4356cf273162ebf5dcd4a01598efe7e5e06ff675084e02&req=dSMgHsl6lIBdWPMW1HO4zdGayWcB0cZzPrskjnUGQkaiRVFS5ZqmQalaxwAM%0AqLjOEBNDeis4wkHYISM%3D%0A)

![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1378047097/97bea69783e99435856fcb04dbc1/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imaged2f3.png?expires=1771632000&signature=687dbb414050377ed11a2e2b2c6616ada7ea8677206c2742394981d04de2d52a&req=dSMgHsl6moFWXvMW3Hu4gYLKsrv0Z7fyvQrCW0nPi0zKZJvCWxQyzz0AIuKx%0ASA%3D%3D%0A)
**What are tasks "run by system?"**  
​  
Browse AI occasionally creates maintenance tasks to:

  * Verify robot health

  * Optimize performance

  * Ensure reliability

These automated system tasks are clearly marked as "run by the system" in your
task history.

  
​

Type of robots: custom, prebuilt and managed

# Type of robots: custom, prebuilt and managed

Choose between creating your own robot, using prebuilt robots, or using our
managed services.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 3 months ago

Table of contents

# Custom robots

90%+ of all robots on our platform are custom. With a custom robot you can
scrape, extract, and monitor exactly what you need from any website giving you
complete control.

With a [custom robot](10496382-how-to-train-a-robot-to-scrape-or-monitor-
data.html) you can fully customize the structure, location, specifics of the
data you want to scrape, monitor, capture, or extract.

To create a custom robot all you need to know is:

  * The website URL you want to extract data from

  * Which data elements you want to capture

  * How you want the data structured and organized

  * When and how often to collect the data 

# Prebuilt robots

If you're looking to scrape data from popular websites, our library of 250+
prebuilt robots will help you extract data from some of the most popular
websites in a few clicks including TikTok, LinkedIn, YouTube, Medium, Amazon,
and more.

💻 **Want to use a prebuilt robot?** Browse our [Prebuilt Robot
Library](https://www.browse.ai/prebuilt-robots), select the robot you want to
use and follow the on-screen prompts. Once run, this robot will then be saved
to your account.

All of our prebuilt robots are:

  * Production-ready with proven reliability

  * Deployable in minutes

  * Built to handle complex data structures

  * No coding required (but fully customizable)

  * Can be connected together using workflows

  * Are built to monitor and extract data in just a few clicks 

# Managed robots

We offer managed services if the data you're looking to extract, scrape or
monitor is complex or high volume. This includes:

  * Robot set up - we will set up the robots for you based on your specifications and train you on how to manage and scale your account from there. 

  * Fully managed services - we manage the end to end data delivery for your business. 

  * Custom data delivery and management - we offer customize data post processing and delivery options based on your needs. 

☎️ **Interested in learning more?** If you're interested in these options,
[book a call with our sales team](https://www.browse.ai/service).

* * *

How to use monitors to automate data extraction

# How to use monitors to automate data extraction

Learn how to automate data extraction by setting up monitors that track
website changes and notify you when updates occur.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 12 months ago

Table of contents

# What are monitors?

Monitors are automated tracking systems that watch for changes on specific web
pages.

When you add a monitor to your robot, Browse AI automatically checks the page
according to your schedule and notifies you when changes occur. You can add a
monitor to any robot.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1378044153/8049b7a18d36960c3617c997bcad/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imageb6de.png?expires=1771547400&signature=80434b7fcdb8bf58a43f1b88e3dc63b3a6b0af883809dd2a40bc93a0dae4e3dd&req=dSMgHsl6mYBaWvMW1HO4zf1gjhTE1rQs7F2Vzvom%2BEnqRvNLoF1omcXyXNYM%0AlofjzwlBGeHqFEpiJF4%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1378044153/8049b7a18d36960c3617c997bcad/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imageb6de.png?expires=1771547400&signature=80434b7fcdb8bf58a43f1b88e3dc63b3a6b0af883809dd2a40bc93a0dae4e3dd&req=dSMgHsl6mYBaWvMW1HO4zf1gjhTE1rQs7F2Vzvom%2BEnqRvNLoF1omcXyXNYM%0AlofjzwlBGeHqFEpiJF4%3D%0A)

# Setting up monitors

Each robot can have multiple monitors configured.

Here's what you can monitor:

  * Individual product pages for price changes

  * Category pages for new items

  * Search results for new listings

  * Competitor pages for content updates

  * Directory pages for new entries

# Configuring monitor settings

When setting up a monitor, you can customize:

  * Monitoring frequency (hourly, daily, weekly, or custom intervals)

  * Notification preferences (email, webhook, or integration alerts)

  * Data handling (where to send extracted data when changes occur)

  * Change detection sensitivity (what constitutes a meaningful change)

# Using monitors with integrations

Connect your monitors directly to your workflow by:

  * Sending data to your preferred tools through our [7,000+ integrations](10536287-available-integrations-with-browse-ai.html)

  * Creating custom webhooks for direct data delivery

  * Setting up automated actions when changes are detected

  * Storing historical data for trend analysis

# Best practices for reliable monitoring

To ensure your monitors work effectively:

  * Test your robot thoroughly before setting up monitoring

  * Set up clear notification rules to avoid alert latency

  * Regularly review your monitors to ensure they're still needed

  * Keep your extraction patterns up to date

![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1378043155/14a91bb6a6ae33c71b243b9c3606/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagea177.png?expires=1771632000&signature=73176bba394eeb7ff1bb3849f8a45f8c15f333acde373e27a89902babddeb0b9&req=dSMgHsl6noBaXPMW3Hu4gYovb%2B7YWqKtP2kOqkiBlcgxpEC0G3FPMjl3dfJS%0AdA%3D%3D%0A)

changes?](https://intercom.help/browse-

How to set up and use input parameters

# How to set up and use input parameters

Input parameters let you adjust your robot's behavior without creating new
robots. You can modify URLs, search terms, and form fields to make your robots
more versatile.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 3 months ago

Table of contents

## What are input parameters?

Input parameters are variables you can change each time you run your robot.
They automatically appear when you:

  * Enter text in form fields during training

  * Use the [Origin URL](10453064-what-is-the-origin-url.html) field

  * Fill out search boxes

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1378040421/42fa4c3608dca0fde38468e76d80/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image4c45.png?expires=1771546500&signature=2884b685938d0b4a58da6421d3c9c0fa28d7a8de406c7e3b626ec2febe1ec8ee&req=dSMgHsl6nYVdWPMW1HO4zaHfH1JEOGtwoUrx4TZ9PorUe1w3Wn%2BcNkWhGNDR%0AM12LaSVCwRdHDwxQCT0%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1378040421/42fa4c3608dca0fde38468e76d80/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image4c45.png?expires=1771546500&signature=2884b685938d0b4a58da6421d3c9c0fa28d7a8de406c7e3b626ec2febe1ec8ee&req=dSMgHsl6nYVdWPMW1HO4zaHfH1JEOGtwoUrx4TZ9PorUe1w3Wn%2BcNkWhGNDR%0AM12LaSVCwRdHDwxQCT0%3D%0A)

## Origin URL

The Origin URL parameter tells your robot which page to start from. You can
change this to:

  * Extract data from different category pages

  * Monitor various product pages

  * Scan different sections of a website

## Search terms

When you use a search box during training, that term becomes a parameter. This
lets you:

  * Search for different products

  * Look up various keywords

  * Query multiple terms without creating new robots 

## Form fields

Any text you enter in forms during training becomes adjustable, allowing you
to:

  * Update date ranges

  * Change locations

  * Modify filter criteria

See: [Can my robot fill out a form or perform an action before extracting
data?](https://intercom.help/browse-ai-089fe61c5dc3/en/articles/10497039-can-
my-robot-fill-out-a-form-or-perform-an-action-before-extracting-data)

* * *

Bulk run: How to use bulk runs to extract data from multiple URLs

# Bulk run: How to use bulk runs to extract data from multiple URLs

Extract data from up to 500,000 URLs simultaneously by uploading a CSV file
through Browse AI's bulk run feature.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 3 months ago

Table of contents

![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1378050496/d8c837706d98f94a375b3fa170a4/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image776f.png?expires=1771632000&signature=245e652db81a27a1acad89a8acb074f6c9e5e7ef5ae4c987fe66c567a48226de&req=dSMgHsl7nYVWX%2FMW3Hu4gQkp%2Byx6Os4qliLIneub%2BP9iE%2BBp%2BlGB2mjaT8%2Fi%0AhA%3D%3D%0A)
Bulk runs can be started from a robot's Run Task tab, or in a robot's data
Table by using the Import CSV option.  
​  
In both cases, you can upload a CSV containing up to 500,000 different sets of
[Input Parameters](10453929-how-to-set-up-and-use-input-parameters.html) and
immediately create a [task](10453731-how-to-set-up-and-run-tasks.html) for
each one of them.

A bulk run allows you to extract data from multiple URLs in one go by
uploading a CSV file containing up to 500,000 different input parameters. This
feature helps you build comprehensive datasets efficiently across many pages
from the same website.

##

# Executing a bulk run via the Run Task tab

You can execute a bulk run directly from your robot's Run Task tab.

  1. Click the Bulk Run Tasks button.

  2. Download the Sample CSV Input if needed to format your CSV correctly.

  3. Upload the prepared CSV file to run the robot using the included input parameters.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1378055229/62e305499230e2c90de2ff7ceb8f/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image734f.png?expires=1771547400&signature=4ad6a8c68863f1f1c9229a004f4b644a321b9db84b0556524fa996c3dcaca426&req=dSMgHsl7mINdUPMW1HO4zad6u9YkZC60qwxwKlIuHsTAvjQzHlPwlTYdvMGK%0AUYCGfqQFz1LMw0kivwc%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1378055229/62e305499230e2c90de2ff7ceb8f/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image734f.png?expires=1771547400&signature=4ad6a8c68863f1f1c9229a004f4b644a321b9db84b0556524fa996c3dcaca426&req=dSMgHsl7mINdUPMW1HO4zad6u9YkZC60qwxwKlIuHsTAvjQzHlPwlTYdvMGK%0AUYCGfqQFz1LMw0kivwc%3D%0A)

# Executing a bulk run via the Table import CSV option

You can execute a bulk run from Tables via the import CSV option.

  1. Click the Import CSV button at the bottom of your table. 

  2. Download the Sample CSV Input if needed to format your CSV correctly.

  3. Select the Click to upload area, then choose your desired CSV.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1378065111/b31e91c800eaaf3ac7f79699d962/CleanShot%202025-02-12%20at%2017.17.39.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27CleanShot%25202025-02-12%2520at%252017.17.39c6d6.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=bc4e9604722b897019d5ab36b5be86f4ec57be2878bf73067ed31d34f31e648c&req=dSMgHsl4mIBeWPMW1HO4zZ%2FXlO5YfiQUn5XGvenzJwdTi3VEnegX%2Bd51e8vb%0A%2BFt3%2Fgkvkfj9%2Fh86hIY%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1378065111/b31e91c800eaaf3ac7f79699d962/CleanShot%202025-02-12%20at%2017.17.39.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27CleanShot%25202025-02-12%2520at%252017.17.39c6d6.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=bc4e9604722b897019d5ab36b5be86f4ec57be2878bf73067ed31d34f31e648c&req=dSMgHsl4mIBeWPMW1HO4zZ%2FXlO5YfiQUn5XGvenzJwdTi3VEnegX%2Bd51e8vb%0A%2BFt3%2Fgkvkfj9%2Fh86hIY%3D%0A)

# I've started my bulk run - what happens next?

Once you've executed your bulk run, the tasks will be processed in a queue.
You will get the fully extracted dataset once the bulk run has finished.

If you encounter failures during bulk run execution, it may indicate
insufficient credits in your Browse AI account. Verify and ensure you have
adequate credits to complete the task before starting. Visit your robot's
History tab to monitor the progress and adjust configurations as necessary.

# My bulk run has completed - where do I see the results?

You can have a holistic view on these results, as well as export the very same
results, via our **Tables.**

![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1378057460/1f317cb35c8a2b7801360f0cf2b9/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imageaa75.png?expires=1771632000&signature=af20504a3b893999867e9044fc174132b280c8850d22cfb28df68302b6bed8dd&req=dSMgHsl7moVZWfMW3Hu4gSVsZzu2Qya4ojw7ypOkztR3c1sghFw2woLgn86F%0AOw%3D%3D%0A)
**See:[How to use data Tables](https://intercom.help/browse-
ai-089fe61c5dc3/en/articles/10477537-how-to-use-data-tables)**

**Troubleshooting Bulk Run Issues** : One common issue is running out of
processing credits, which can halt tasks. Ensure your account has sufficient
credits and monitor tasks efficiently using logs.

# What are example use cases where a bulk run is useful?

Bulk runs are great for when you want to extract similarly structured data
across multiple web pages across the same site.

For example, let's say I wanted to create a company database using data from
LinkedIn. Bulk monitoring would allow me to upload a CSV containing links of
up to 500,000 company pages on LinkedIn, and then extract that data from all
of those pages into one dataset.

![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1378057590/c943c0930c79653d178047290a34/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagee8d4.png?expires=1771632000&signature=59c2511545758dc71edf895fdc7c3531eeeb6e1cc7aba847ad3e5cff3c1b6bfc&req=dSMgHsl7moRWWfMW3Hu4gdHg%2FyATAmozpoDk5mZHTTApPm0%2F1PO1ksR78pCI%0Asw%3D%3D%0A)
**See:**[What does the "Bulk Run" feature do?](https://intercom.help/browse-
ai-089fe61c5dc3/en/articles/10477518-what-does-the-bulk-run-feature-do)

# Best Practices for Successful Bulk Task Execution

  * **Plan Credit Usage:** Evaluate the estimated number of tasks and ensure sufficient credits are available before initiating the process.

  * **Standardize URLs:** Verify that all URLs match a consistent structure for compatibility with the same robot.

  * **Test Runs:** Conduct small-scale test runs before applying a bulk run task to the entire dataset.

  * **Monitor and Adjust:** Regularly check task logs to address any errors and fine-tune configurations for optimal results.

* * *

extraction](10595234-import-csv-for-bulk-data-extraction.html)[API Guide: Bulk
operations and large-scale data extraction](12683563-api-guide-bulk-

What is deep scraping?

# What is deep scraping?

Understand the core concepts and use cases for deep scraping (i.e. extracting
a list of items, then scrape info from each item's detail page).

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 12 months ago

Table of contents

![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1379635373/a1a52a83b95c8705d6b72e965815/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image638e.png?expires=1771632000&signature=701878ae5fb0b039f2676ec0ca4e79c6558c05b8bc689a8571b163ca45e2c1ee&req=dSMgH899mIJYWvMW3Hu4gWuDSkUq1vEt2tPi%2BIWMQhaFfinvaYG6Rrme06v3%0AdA%3D%3D%0A)
Deep scraping helps you gather detailed information by connecting two robots:
one that collects lists of items and another that extracts specific details
from each item's page.  
​  
This approach allows you to build comprehensive datasets across multiple pages
from the same website.

# How deep scraping works

Deep scraping involves two main steps:

  1. Your first robot scans a list or category page to collect basic information and URLs.

  2. Your second robot visits each URL to extract detailed information from individual pages.

You can learn how to set up deep scraping using [workflows here](10553465-how-
to-extract-data-across-multiple-pages-deep-scraping.html).

# Common use cases for deep scraping

## E-commerce product analysis: scraping a list of products and details about
each one

  1. Robot A collects product information from category pages, including names, prices, and URLs.

  2. Robot B visits each product's URL detail page to gather comprehensive information like specifications, reviews, stock levels, shipping options, etc.

![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1379640043/3c4a8ee57e672ff21ab8785fa5ad/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image18da.png?expires=1771632000&signature=cade6d05ad3091d33394e04c8e4ac90b95f255d78dd18c80905be313a56523ec&req=dSMgH896nYFbWvMW3Hu4gdsVC0UMwe53Udg%2BHGyS4C71S3Sn0xXBClaCHBWt%0ACA%3D%3D%0A)
This helps with competitive analysis and price monitoring across product
categories.

## Real estate market research: scraping multiple property listings and their
detailed information

  1. Robot A scans property listing pages for basic details like prices and locations.

  2. Robot B visits individual property pages to collect specifications, amenities, agent information, photos, and property history.

![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1379640043/3c4a8ee57e672ff21ab8785fa5ad/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image18da.png?expires=1771632000&signature=cade6d05ad3091d33394e04c8e4ac90b95f255d78dd18c80905be313a56523ec&req=dSMgH896nYFbWvMW3Hu4gdsVC0UMwe53Udg%2BHGyS4C71S3Sn0xXBClaCHBWt%0ACA%3D%3D%0A)
This creates comprehensive property databases for market analysis.

## Create a custom business directory database: extracting listings from a
directory as well as custom details

  1. Robot A processes directory pages to gather business listings (ex: Yellow Pages, professional associations, etc.).

  2. Robot B visits each business profile to extract contact details, business hours, services, reviews, and social media information.

![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1379640043/3c4a8ee57e672ff21ab8785fa5ad/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image18da.png?expires=1771632000&signature=cade6d05ad3091d33394e04c8e4ac90b95f255d78dd18c80905be313a56523ec&req=dSMgH896nYFbWvMW3Hu4gdsVC0UMwe53Udg%2BHGyS4C71S3Sn0xXBClaCHBWt%0ACA%3D%3D%0A)
This helps create detailed contact and/or lead databases for sales and
marketing teams.

# Using workflows to automate deep scraping

To make deep scraping more efficient, use our [Workflows](10459476-workflows-
how-can-i-create-a-workflow-connecting-two-robots.html) feature to connect
your robots. Workflows automatically pass data between robots, creating a
seamless extraction process that requires minimal oversight.

![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1379636205/9d29955f1f99bb922e43fb285bea/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imaged60a.png?expires=1771632000&signature=a64d7222aa83426569de6d4826e18248de00e5975eabe7f5e617dbd444d29667&req=dSMgH899m4NfXPMW3Hu4gRHvwL8jwAA%2BE64DkU9nVtMzDV4vlW4NiL9deaem%0A%2FQ%3D%3D%0A)
**See:**[How can I extract data from lists and their associated details pages?
(Deep scraping)](https://intercom.help/browse-
ai-089fe61c5dc3/en/articles/10553465-how-can-i-extract-data-from-lists-and-
their-associated-details-pages-deep-scraping) and****[How can I create a

Integrations: How to send extracted data to other tools and apps

# Integrations: How to send extracted data to other tools and apps

Scrape web data then connect to 7,000+ applications and tools.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 12 months ago

Table of contents

Connect your the data you extract from Browse AI into to your existing tools
to create automated data pipelines in minutes.

Browse AI offers native integrations with popular tools and connects with
thousands more through automation platforms.

![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1379829165/47286fcc8351d1714e3c0455f25a/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image0507.png?expires=1771632000&signature=2402b1b1f5920798ae694e7e27dd341190571311c02be60c34d1a68b7c9d2fae&req=dSMgH8F8lIBZXPMW3Hu4gTNAx8kD0ZE8rXBVvKMUbyNlPekholDdFBnYeaE%2F%0ANw%3D%3D%0A)
**See:**[Which integrations are available on Browse
AI?](https://intercom.help/browse-ai-089fe61c5dc3/en/articles/10536287-which-
integrations-are-available-on-browse-ai)

# Native integrations

##
![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1350995074/7a26edfac6f5a0d9950addf25080/CleanShot%202025-01-24%20at%2014.08.54%25402x.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27CleanShot%25202025-01-24%2520at%252014.08.54%25402xcb77.png?expires=1771632000&signature=2be323ecc891fc52c856241ba3c7ce0de86f061c4fe60563c398eb7725a865d5&req=dSMiFsB3mIFYXfMW3Hu4gavCxiyqPif9erB3MmvZVsiE0AiHwK8OtwuqqdFs%0Avg%3D%3D%0A)
Google Sheets and Airtable

Send extracted data directly to your spreadsheets and databases with our
native integrations. Once configured, your robot automatically updates your
sheets with fresh data after each extraction.

[Google Sheets Integration Guide](10535898-how-to-sync-scraped-data-with-
google-sheets.html)

[Airtable Integration Guide](10537018-how-to-enable-airtable-integration.html)

The Browse AI Google Sheets add-on provides additional functionality:

  * Run robots directly from your spreadsheet using Input Parameters

  * Automatically remove outdated data

  * Eliminate duplicate entries

  * Manage all your extracted data within Google Sheets

![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1379829165/47286fcc8351d1714e3c0455f25a/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image0507.png?expires=1771632000&signature=2402b1b1f5920798ae694e7e27dd341190571311c02be60c34d1a68b7c9d2fae&req=dSMgH8F8lIBZXPMW3Hu4gTNAx8kD0ZE8rXBVvKMUbyNlPekholDdFBnYeaE%2F%0ANw%3D%3D%0A)

on?](https://intercom.help/browse-ai-089fe61c5dc3/en/articles/10536316-how-
can-i-use-the-google-sheets-add-on)

#
![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1350994374/318dcb7c6ecddda750187fd3e0a7/CleanShot%202025-01-24%20at%2014.07.33%25402x.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27CleanShot%25202025-01-24%2520at%252014.07.33%25402x9e68.png?expires=1771632000&signature=ce284eef9e77a5fe1b0fc01edf03d4fce7c7e4c181de4ca46115cd52f4e9b946&req=dSMiFsB3mYJYXfMW3Hu4gUQp1lh4Ou5dqdNwx7ENGQL%2BShoaxUMDcOh9eHd0%0Asw%3D%3D%0A)

These native integrations allow you to connect Browse AI to 7,000+ other apps
and create custom workflows and automations.

## Zapier

Create automated workflows between Browse AI and thousands of applications.
Zapier offers a visual interface for building integrations without coding.

![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1379829165/47286fcc8351d1714e3c0455f25a/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image0507.png?expires=1771632000&signature=2402b1b1f5920798ae694e7e27dd341190571311c02be60c34d1a68b7c9d2fae&req=dSMgH8F8lIBZXPMW3Hu4gTNAx8kD0ZE8rXBVvKMUbyNlPekholDdFBnYeaE%2F%0ANw%3D%3D%0A)

ai-089fe61c5dc3/en/articles/10536328-how-to-connect-browse-ai-with-zapier)

## Make

Build complex automation workflows with Browse AI and your existing tools.
Make.com provides advanced features for customizing your data flows.

![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1379829165/47286fcc8351d1714e3c0455f25a/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image0507.png?expires=1771632000&signature=2402b1b1f5920798ae694e7e27dd341190571311c02be60c34d1a68b7c9d2fae&req=dSMgH8F8lIBZXPMW3Hu4gTNAx8kD0ZE8rXBVvKMUbyNlPekholDdFBnYeaE%2F%0ANw%3D%3D%0A)

Workflows: How can I create a workflow connecting two robots?

# Workflows: How can I create a workflow connecting two robots?

Perform deep scraping by using the Workflows feature.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 3 months ago

Table of contents

Browse AI's workflow feature allows you to connect two robots to work together
automatically. This feature solves a common data extraction challenge: getting
data from both list pages and detail pages efficiently.

We commonly refer to this as **deep scraping**.

![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1384496463/721f0d1af4ca779b699389d3f044/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagedf2b.png?expires=1771632000&signature=c191b9bdc8a0b835afab4675f81dda3c9ada608ff5638d5878dc717299537396&req=dSMvEs13m4VZWvMW3Hu4gecMCnZTGZC06lpl37EbGlv0d2kcjnrj3q0pHiMM%0A9g%3D%3D%0A)
**AT A GLANCE**

  * Robot A extracts a list of items, including a URL to its detail page (e.g. products, jobs, etc)

  * Robot B then extracts the data from each item (e.g. prices, descriptions, salaries, etc)

You simply set up your two robots and connect them in sequence. The system
handles the rest, making data extraction from multiple pages seamless and
efficient.

# Choose Your Learning Path

* * *

![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1351021753/5cb505510fcddb38503e369502b9/CleanShot%202025-01-24%20at%2014.48.27%25402x.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27CleanShot%25202025-01-24%2520at%252014.48.27%25402xb6c6.png?expires=1771632000&signature=becce552c9599a7f907fcde04f53fc8e732692f5c24d5e832e738a5211d8e59e&req=dSMiF8l8nIZaWvMW3Hu4gU%2Fy4PmrmiDjJl5i2iR%2Fky2%2BA%2FeRu2msIxrI2nVl%0ALw%3D%3D%0A)
**Watch& learn: **follow along with our video tutorial

![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1351024664/a36f532c25e38b38e8f36a0fb9a5/CleanShot%202025-01-24%20at%2014.52.59%25402x.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27CleanShot%25202025-01-24%2520at%252014.52.59%25402x26c5.png?expires=1771632000&signature=c45554aafafc88302d8b0200e369eaae4951144e62c720a6673fe8c5752fc108&req=dSMiF8l8mYdZXfMW3Hu4gXXB2fLYWMwsNNFxy6gDyIeIDQwgH3iMOHABdVqW%0AUw%3D%3D%0A)
**Quick start:** just the essential steps to get going

![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1351026267/3719044006ad192a11170eca94c0/CleanShot%202025-01-24%20at%2014.55.49%25402x.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27CleanShot%25202025-01-24%2520at%252014.55.49%25402xf0dc.png?expires=1771632000&signature=33bb73483a0a8db7255d793d64ccca91b0dca6e4fa6186b6a5545ea19524a486&req=dSMiF8l8m4NZXvMW3Hu4gaXSaSINQpMHWG%2FevFPowm0NdE3FPzMOVIb5UmrN%0AOg%3D%3D%0A)
**Full guide: **complete instructions with screenshots and examples

#
![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1351021753/5cb505510fcddb38503e369502b9/CleanShot%202025-01-24%20at%2014.48.27%25402x.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27CleanShot%25202025-01-24%2520at%252014.48.27%25402xb6c6.png?expires=1771632000&signature=becce552c9599a7f907fcde04f53fc8e732692f5c24d5e832e738a5211d8e59e&req=dSMiF8l8nIZaWvMW3Hu4gU%2Fy4PmrmiDjJl5i2iR%2Fky2%2BA%2FeRu2msIxrI2nVl%0ALw%3D%3D%0A)
Watch & learn

* * *

##  Using a Keyword in Robot B Instead of a URL

The more common use of the Workflow feature is typically to deep scrape on a
single website (get a list of items, "click into" each one, and get details
from the single pages).

But you can also send text from your first robot (like a company name) to an
input parameter in your second robot (like a keyword search).

See it in action below!

#
![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1351024664/a36f532c25e38b38e8f36a0fb9a5/CleanShot%202025-01-24%20at%2014.52.59%25402x.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27CleanShot%25202025-01-24%2520at%252014.52.59%25402x26c5.png?expires=1771632000&signature=c45554aafafc88302d8b0200e369eaae4951144e62c720a6673fe8c5752fc108&req=dSMiF8l8mYdZXfMW3Hu4gXXB2fLYWMwsNNFxy6gDyIeIDQwgH3iMOHABdVqW%0AUw%3D%3D%0A)
Quick start

* * *

Before creating the workflow, you’ll need two robots:

  * one robot that extracts a list of items, including a URL to its detail page

  * a second robot that extracts the data from those detail pages

**Example:** Robot A extracts a list of products from an eCommerce website
(title, price, link to product). Robot B extracts detailed information from
the product page (description, stock, SKU, etc).

When both robots are created:

  1. From your Dashboard, click on the _Workflows_ tab

  2. Click **Add New Workflow**

  3. Name the workflow something descriptive

  4. In the _Robot A_ tab

    1. From the dropdown, select the robot that extracts the list of items

    2. Click the **Next Step** button

  5. In the _Robot B_ tab

    1. From the dropdown, select the robot that extracts item details

    2. In the _Origin URL_ dropdown, select the item from Robot A that links to the detailed item page

    3. Click the **Next Step** button

  6. In the _Filter_ tab choose one of these options:

    1. Always

    2. Only if robot A finds changes while monitoring

    3. Only if robot A finds new items while monitoring

    4. Only if robot A finds new or changed items while monitoring

  7. Click the **Save and Enable Workflow** button

  8. On the _Overview_ tab of your workflow, you can:

    1. Click the **Run Task** button to trigger Robot A to run, which kick starts the workflow

    2. Click the **Create New Monitor** button to add a monitoring schedule to Robot A, so it runs automatically

    3. Edit Robot A, the Filter, or Robot B by clicking the **Edit** button on the right side of those items

    4. See Robot B’s extracted data by clicking the **View Data in Tables** button

    5. Add data integrations to Robot B by clicking the + icon then selecting **Add other integrations**

  9. On the _Settings_ tab of your workflow, you can:

    1. Rename the workflow by clicking into the box, changing the text, then clicking the **Save** button

    2. Delete the workflow by clicking the **Delete Workflow** button

#
![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1351026267/3719044006ad192a11170eca94c0/CleanShot%202025-01-24%20at%2014.55.49%25402x.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27CleanShot%25202025-01-24%2520at%252014.55.49%25402xf0dc.png?expires=1771632000&signature=33bb73483a0a8db7255d793d64ccca91b0dca6e4fa6186b6a5545ea19524a486&req=dSMiF8l8m4NZXvMW3Hu4gaXSaSINQpMHWG%2FevFPowm0NdE3FPzMOVIb5UmrN%0AOg%3D%3D%0A)
Full guide

* * *

Let’s connect two robots to create a workflow:

  * **Robot A:** extract a list of companies from Y Combinator’s _Companies_ page

  * **Robot B:** extract company details from a single company page on Y Combinator

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1384412774/9c60cdd8512ca3c37b4228015b94/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imageec65.png?expires=1771547400&signature=17ca2d8f11602bf4729d1a761c73a3efa07413fabdd09d1480c46a6695934a7d&req=dSMvEs1%2Fn4ZYXfMW1HO4zXEE1Rl6cIqT4kifqcpHRI2G4QuSIkmhfoVmIuU2%0ATYPcbYPFnr4HaxmGuio%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1384412774/9c60cdd8512ca3c37b4228015b94/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imageec65.png?expires=1771547400&signature=17ca2d8f11602bf4729d1a761c73a3efa07413fabdd09d1480c46a6695934a7d&req=dSMvEs1%2Fn4ZYXfMW1HO4zXEE1Rl6cIqT4kifqcpHRI2G4QuSIkmhfoVmIuU2%0ATYPcbYPFnr4HaxmGuio%3D%0A)

  
​From your Dashboard, (1) click on the _Workflows_ tab, then (2) Click **Add
New Workflow**

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1384413081/aba891f1a5e2784f03f21f63c127/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image3e13.png?expires=1771547400&signature=272aa67fd2a127d6d6373912af50cc40a5a2028c337b4ced1b9dd4fad886c17b&req=dSMvEs1%2FnoFXWPMW1HO4zREh3elWFtl4SjeU7csFT4EytEEC%2F8Tm3NKP6yO4%0Ajr%2Fq%2Bmii6W55aL5hiFo%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1384413081/aba891f1a5e2784f03f21f63c127/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image3e13.png?expires=1771547400&signature=272aa67fd2a127d6d6373912af50cc40a5a2028c337b4ced1b9dd4fad886c17b&req=dSMvEs1%2FnoFXWPMW1HO4zREh3elWFtl4SjeU7csFT4EytEEC%2F8Tm3NKP6yO4%0Ajr%2Fq%2Bmii6W55aL5hiFo%3D%0A)

Name the workflow something descriptive, like _Extract YC Company Details_.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1351019124/822bef09df65ebb3f96c29842c97/file-
ZGyVK1jMEw.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-
ZGyVK1jMEw831a.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=ab50c963a4c8e4817b252a197a139b3a61e271673927a26e736a9b176d4093ef&req=dSMiF8l%2FlIBdXfMW1HO4zZU7HugmlR3YcPwk8WqvQt6fhLo5nVtkmuin6Jzn%0AN6qS89TsV8UZ1GezxPs%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1351019124/822bef09df65ebb3f96c29842c97/file-
ZGyVK1jMEw.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-
ZGyVK1jMEw831a.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=ab50c963a4c8e4817b252a197a139b3a61e271673927a26e736a9b176d4093ef&req=dSMiF8l%2FlIBdXfMW1HO4zZU7HugmlR3YcPwk8WqvQt6fhLo5nVtkmuin6Jzn%0AN6qS89TsV8UZ1GezxPs%3D%0A)

**Robot A:** From the dropdown, select the robot that extracts the list of items. In this case it’s the robot called _Extract Companies | Y Combinator_. Then click the **Next Step** button.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1351019126/d59a585f5dc6236b2bcd55b3cd70/file-
Yz33PsPXcZ.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-
Yz33PsPXcZ430a.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=c68d6d8dc158cd67153ecc67af4de6b01d4fa8e3cd50922a4f67544546de05d8&req=dSMiF8l%2FlIBdX%2FMW1HO4zexewI%2FITJCG0l4IL4%2BumWlIoQJtwGw1WDpNIiuo%0Aby9SZry%2Fwi%2B8y2LxUBk%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1351019126/d59a585f5dc6236b2bcd55b3cd70/file-
Yz33PsPXcZ.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-
Yz33PsPXcZ430a.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=c68d6d8dc158cd67153ecc67af4de6b01d4fa8e3cd50922a4f67544546de05d8&req=dSMiF8l%2FlIBdX%2FMW1HO4zexewI%2FITJCG0l4IL4%2BumWlIoQJtwGw1WDpNIiuo%0Aby9SZry%2Fwi%2B8y2LxUBk%3D%0A)

**Robot B:** From the dropdown, select the robot that extracts item details. In this case it’s the robot called _Extract Company Details | Y Combinator_. From the _Origin URL_ dropdown, select the item from Robot A that links to the detailed item page (in this case, _Company URL_). Then click the **Next Step** button.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1351019123/2acd61fd3968f5ed4340c336749b/file-
do88hvgpjK.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-
do88hvgpjK621c.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=b6cecd64c2423ac9aba9e467a135db59c5ea0c8bcc3b3ddc56d8e2be79cfc38e&req=dSMiF8l%2FlIBdWvMW1HO4zQDWLP0CLr8%2F2msl8jUS5ZgYPlChkkkxw1G57vNy%0AN7GmaLcPXDvYteaM4yQ%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1351019123/2acd61fd3968f5ed4340c336749b/file-
do88hvgpjK.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-
do88hvgpjK621c.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=b6cecd64c2423ac9aba9e467a135db59c5ea0c8bcc3b3ddc56d8e2be79cfc38e&req=dSMiF8l%2FlIBdWvMW1HO4zQDWLP0CLr8%2F2msl8jUS5ZgYPlChkkkxw1G57vNy%0AN7GmaLcPXDvYteaM4yQ%3D%0A)

**Filter:** This step gives you precise control over when your workflow will
run. After Robot A completes its task, this step decides whether to proceed
with running Robot B based on the conditions you set here.

Choose one of the following options, to decide when the workflow will run:

  * Always (will run every time Robot A runs)

  * Only if robot A finds **changes** while monitoring

  * Only if robot A finds **new items** while monitoring

  * Only if robot A finds **new _or_ changed items** while monitoring

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1384417900/79800719a44dc9a861bb4500d1c9/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imageff8c.png?expires=1771547400&signature=1bf6b847710308751f939197b9b3d6c6d5ada89350150b8dc4041f5f0ae74731&req=dSMvEs1%2FmohfWfMW1HO4zXn5dMd62dhWVogzGZHrSJYmz4wIkDDLI0W9qy5a%0AeHpY%2FptacSUvem2yxPY%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1384417900/79800719a44dc9a861bb4500d1c9/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imageff8c.png?expires=1771547400&signature=1bf6b847710308751f939197b9b3d6c6d5ada89350150b8dc4041f5f0ae74731&req=dSMvEs1%2FmohfWfMW1HO4zXn5dMd62dhWVogzGZHrSJYmz4wIkDDLI0W9qy5a%0AeHpY%2FptacSUvem2yxPY%3D%0A)

If you choose, "Only if robot A finds changes while monitoring" or "Only if
robot A finds new or changed items while monitoring", a couple of new options
will appear:

  * **Run robot B when new data is found or when** :

    * _[Mapped column names]_ has changed

    * Any data from robot A has changed

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1635746717/dd6a1e295497ad1af548abb3c2d4/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imaged294.png?expires=1771547400&signature=0fdbaf4f82090eaf1df605936eb17b2831a990639be1f26efafd40d3e4416f0b&req=dSYkE856m4ZeXvMW1HO4zRgfp5b2np8MZB5z7YA7c7e3JrIlDKZUrvZHiVj2%0AW%2Fw7d0a1670rhHn1XDU%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1635746717/dd6a1e295497ad1af548abb3c2d4/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imaged294.png?expires=1771547400&signature=0fdbaf4f82090eaf1df605936eb17b2831a990639be1f26efafd40d3e4416f0b&req=dSYkE856m4ZeXvMW1HO4zRgfp5b2np8MZB5z7YA7c7e3JrIlDKZUrvZHiVj2%0AW%2Fw7d0a1670rhHn1XDU%3D%0A)

**Note:** If a task has no historical capture, it is automatically treated as
“Added.”

  
​**Save:** After you’ve chosen the workflow Filter, click the **Save and
Enable Workflow** button:

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1384418147/5fa19f7435bfd9765ec4dd909b0c/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image8a42.png?expires=1771547400&signature=b060909cfaec1c93f8868ecca5b5f64bc9b44545927d2a683442c2f6d769a008&req=dSMvEs1%2FlYBbXvMW1HO4za8fAyJieftj3YaEeF%2FNfiCPLeDlDF3ckoUYxtvO%0AnqjpT5iMJrG50w8uPEI%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1384418147/5fa19f7435bfd9765ec4dd909b0c/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image8a42.png?expires=1771547400&signature=b060909cfaec1c93f8868ecca5b5f64bc9b44545927d2a683442c2f6d769a008&req=dSMvEs1%2FlYBbXvMW1HO4za8fAyJieftj3YaEeF%2FNfiCPLeDlDF3ckoUYxtvO%0AnqjpT5iMJrG50w8uPEI%3D%0A)

**Overview:** On this tab you can do the following.

  1. Click the **Run Task** button to trigger Robot A to run, which kick starts the workflow.

  2. Click the **Create New Monitor** button to add a monitoring schedule to Robot A, so it runs automatically.

  3. Edit Robot A, the Filter, or Robot B by clicking the **Edit** button on the right side of those items.

  4. See Robot B’s extracted data by clicking the **View Data in Tables** button.

  5. Add data integrations to Robot B by clicking the + icon then selecting **Add other integrations.**

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1384415278/9944611b247fa9f3544ee4b767c2/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagedfbf.png?expires=1771547400&signature=05555cc3597ccef1be5bacfff8a92a8605b8b712caa7aba52f028b1e4c6e1886&req=dSMvEs1%2FmINYUfMW1HO4zftq4%2BzadJynR%2BayuPwQLEjw9FJhGVCh2soBVbN7%0AXm03MgqoNj%2BxovlAsYc%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1384415278/9944611b247fa9f3544ee4b767c2/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagedfbf.png?expires=1771547400&signature=05555cc3597ccef1be5bacfff8a92a8605b8b712caa7aba52f028b1e4c6e1886&req=dSMvEs1%2FmINYUfMW1HO4zftq4%2BzadJynR%2BayuPwQLEjw9FJhGVCh2soBVbN7%0AXm03MgqoNj%2BxovlAsYc%3D%0A)

  
​**Settings:** On this tab you can do the following.

  1. Rename the workflow by clicking into the box, changing the text, then clicking the S**ave** button.

  2. Delete the workflow by clicking the **Delete Workflow** button.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1384415547/86ce08d8f392b3648557d753fa3e/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image0640.png?expires=1771547400&signature=19a6c946069cf6fa1ef833b119b51ce34f22f81a4ff3ee8e65b865f3163c75d7&req=dSMvEs1%2FmIRbXvMW1HO4zafs8EBSjClUOLygQhuIe2NltCPBdF4SgdOz0D8A%0AXri2uOHatXMkHLatPZk%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1384415547/86ce08d8f392b3648557d753fa3e/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image0640.png?expires=1771547400&signature=19a6c946069cf6fa1ef833b119b51ce34f22f81a4ff3ee8e65b865f3163c75d7&req=dSMvEs1%2FmIRbXvMW1HO4zafs8EBSjClUOLygQhuIe2NltCPBdF4SgdOz0D8A%0AXri2uOHatXMkHLatPZk%3D%0A)

Bulk run: How to execute bulk tasks

# Bulk run: How to execute bulk tasks

Use Bulk Run to process multiple URLs simultaneously, saving time when
extracting data from numerous pages across websites.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 3 months ago

Table of contents

# What is Bulk Run?

Bulk Run allows you to execute multiple data extraction tasks simultaneously
by processing a list of URLs.

This feature is particularly useful when you need to extract data from many
similar pages, such as product details or business listings. You can extract
data from up to 500,000 URLs at once using the Bulk Run feature.

# Video

You can click the icon that looks like a list, to see the chapters available
in the video.

# Prerequisites

Before using Bulk Run, ensure you have:

  1. A robot trained to extract data from detail pages

  2. A CSV file containing the URLs you want to process

     * You can generate this list of URLs manually through your own internal process, or

     * You can generate this list of URLs by creating a robot to extract a list of URLs 

  3. Sufficient credits for your extraction needs

# How to create a Bulk Run

  1. Navigate to the Tables tab of the robot that scrapes the detail page

  2. Click the "Import CSV" button on the bottom left

  3. Upload your CSV file containing URLs

  4. Map your CSV columns to the robot's parameters

  5. Review and start the bulk run

# Working with integrations

When using Bulk Run with integrations:

  * Maintain consistent column headers in connected spreadsheets

  * Recalibrate integrations if column names need to change

  * Test your integration setup with a small batch first

# Managing Bulk Run tasks

You can manage your Bulk Run tasks from the History tab:

  * Monitor progress in real-time

  * Pause or stop runs as needed

  * Download results when complete

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1379836670/99d7c3d6bc86ab79a9bb67d37eb0/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image558e.png?expires=1771546500&signature=7dff8b7452cf396534c41be5e7412e730fcc4a01b7e34dd2600906f8c45a2166&req=dSMgH8F9m4dYWfMW1HO4zXfzMqYf%2Ba2nG0%2FxdiIgwsAzXkHLPOqkrV7DLK1H%0AvdrTmbX5HY0MlKlsiys%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1379836670/99d7c3d6bc86ab79a9bb67d37eb0/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image558e.png?expires=1771546500&signature=7dff8b7452cf396534c41be5e7412e730fcc4a01b7e34dd2600906f8c45a2166&req=dSMgH8F9m4dYWfMW1HO4zXfzMqYf%2Ba2nG0%2FxdiIgwsAzXkHLPOqkrV7DLK1H%0AvdrTmbX5HY0MlKlsiys%3D%0A)

# Troubleshooting

Common issues and solutions:

  * Mismatched URL formats: Ensure URLs match the robot's training format

  * Integration errors: Verify column mappings are correct

  * Failed runs: Check individual URL results for specific errors  
​
