Understanding monitoring result colors

# Understanding monitoring result colors

Understand how Browse AI uses color coding to show data changes: green for new
content, yellow for modifications, and red for removed information.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 5 months ago

Table of contents

# Quick color guide

Our monitoring results use three colors to help you quickly identify changes:

  * Green: New data added

  * Yellow: Existing data modified

  * Red: Data removed

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374346078/37ac7f25602f26d0ce5859469490/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image2516.png?expires=1771547400&signature=d13ee704d4d257a8712b4b4c60f48334bed2989037fdfa41d04c3c17db7a7fac&req=dSMgEsp6m4FYUfMW1HO4zchxqBC1Bh19Fyy3WSvXSjIc85kxh3TD5TSgHtng%0AynHYQTR5w4%2BvtrjLhCA%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374346078/37ac7f25602f26d0ce5859469490/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image2516.png?expires=1771547400&signature=d13ee704d4d257a8712b4b4c60f48334bed2989037fdfa41d04c3c17db7a7fac&req=dSMgEsp6m4FYUfMW1HO4zchxqBC1Bh19Fyy3WSvXSjIc85kxh3TD5TSgHtng%0AynHYQTR5w4%2BvtrjLhCA%3D%0A)

# Detailed breakdown

## Green: New content

  * Indicates newly added information

  * Content wasn't present in previous check

  * Common in product listings or news feeds

  * Example: New items added to a catalog

**Green Color:** When you see the green color, new data has been added to the
URL you're monitoring.

##  Yellow: Modified content

  * Shows changes to existing information

  * Could include text updates or price changes

  * Helps track item modifications

  * Example: Price updates on a product

**Yellow Color:** If you see the yellow color, it means that the existing data
on the URL has been changed in some way.

##  Red: Removed content

  * Highlights deleted or removed information

  * Previously monitored content no longer present

  * Useful for tracking availability

  * Example: Out-of-stock products

**Red Color:** if you see the red color, it means that previously present data
on the URL has been removed. This could indicate that the URL has been updated
and some information has been deleted.

## Position tracking

Numbers next to monitored items show position changes:

  * No number: Position unchanged

  * Positive number (+1): Moved down in list

  * Negative number (-1): Moved up in list

  * Example: A -2 means the item moved up 2 positions 

For example, in the image below, you can see data in position 3; the number
next to it is -1. This means the data was previously in position four but has
since been moved to position 3.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374348172/80392fc144e2c8086a482045d5fd/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image7e7a.png?expires=1771547400&signature=6c78e7cc441be7009fb3ee8af983b61f467a88366aef5448b8c66bdd262aaac3&req=dSMgEsp6lYBYW%2FMW1HO4zX34OBh8uirbfXavzKvPvEHa0glRd%2B5THrmPA4xq%0Adutyr0whMifhof9EPCI%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374348172/80392fc144e2c8086a482045d5fd/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image7e7a.png?expires=1771547400&signature=6c78e7cc441be7009fb3ee8af983b61f467a88366aef5448b8c66bdd262aaac3&req=dSMgEsp6lYBYW%2FMW1HO4zX34OBh8uirbfXavzKvPvEHa0glRd%2B5THrmPA4xq%0Adutyr0whMifhof9EPCI%3D%0A)

# Customizing your view

## Highlight changes

  * Toggle **Highlight changes since last check** to show/hide colors

  * Default view shows all changes highlighted

  * Switching off shows standard list view

## Best practices

  1. Regular check frequency

     * Set appropriate monitoring intervals

     * Balance between timeliness and credit usage

     * Consider website update patterns

  2. Change verification

     * Click highlighted items to see detailed changes

     * Compare with previous versions

     * Verify position changes

  3. Notification settings

     * Configure alerts based on change types

     * Set priority levels for different colors

     * Customize email notifications

# Troubleshooting

Common scenarios and solutions:

  1. Unexpected colors

     * Verify monitor configuration

     * Check website structure changes

     * Review extraction patterns

  2. Missing changes

     * Confirm monitoring frequency

     * Check website accessibility

     * Review robot configuration

  3. Position tracking issues

     * Verify list structure

     * Check sorting parameters

     * Review website layout changes

* * *

How to sync scraped data with Google Sheets

# How to sync scraped data with Google Sheets

Integrate your robot to Google Sheets to automatically sync extracted data to
a live Google Sheet.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 4 months ago

Table of contents

# Prerequisites

  1. An approved robot that has extracted data

  2. A Google account

  3. Optional: a Google sheet where you want the data to go (i.e. the target Google Sheet)

Note: you can sync the data you've captured to an existing Google Sheet or
create a new Google Sheet when you set up this integration.

# How to set up the Google Sheets integration

Select your Robot from the dashboard, then click on the **Integrate** tab.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374352607/b0d571c96085134ac8232787c59e/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image088b.png?expires=1771547400&signature=09aa56b34584d58b2213da12637d18e5b38381c3bf1d6158da701f9b20d3a1f0&req=dSMgEsp7n4dfXvMW1HO4zQRFbNg4aIEt9HxiKBtj4kiXAEfMXO94Idbea79i%0AiMtfSNlVBY2bIjraz9g%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374352607/b0d571c96085134ac8232787c59e/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image088b.png?expires=1771547400&signature=09aa56b34584d58b2213da12637d18e5b38381c3bf1d6158da701f9b20d3a1f0&req=dSMgEsp7n4dfXvMW1HO4zQRFbNg4aIEt9HxiKBtj4kiXAEfMXO94Idbea79i%0AiMtfSNlVBY2bIjraz9g%3D%0A)

Click on **Google Sheets**.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374353058/802e40dd329369799f126bce04ff/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image3e51.png?expires=1771547400&signature=1d6d19fd33a66a2287c3cf29d20d7f8a036125b7f4883cb0de3666c7bb160610&req=dSMgEsp7noFaUfMW1HO4zeNt180KTogsGkpAF96K4txtqHtHahh%2Buvx5rHDN%0ABjTNBjbyO9AwRuYueyA%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374353058/802e40dd329369799f126bce04ff/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image3e51.png?expires=1771547400&signature=1d6d19fd33a66a2287c3cf29d20d7f8a036125b7f4883cb0de3666c7bb160610&req=dSMgEsp7noFaUfMW1HO4zeNt180KTogsGkpAF96K4txtqHtHahh%2Buvx5rHDN%0ABjTNBjbyO9AwRuYueyA%3D%0A)

Click **Enable Syncing** **to Google Sheet**.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374353478/d09577dc5743904357eed3d72766/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image4967.png?expires=1771547400&signature=9069608e8ba5157f0fefa3f26e8abf7a588f671a9ea4a99358ff3820a62ed4d5&req=dSMgEsp7noVYUfMW1HO4zYmshtI5qn3IpnT%2BKOor%2B%2BOrPMYnU%2FhlwPMURVPn%0Axr3VzgdQ8T2J4wrTjGk%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374353478/d09577dc5743904357eed3d72766/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image4967.png?expires=1771547400&signature=9069608e8ba5157f0fefa3f26e8abf7a588f671a9ea4a99358ff3820a62ed4d5&req=dSMgEsp7noVYUfMW1HO4zYmshtI5qn3IpnT%2BKOor%2B%2BOrPMYnU%2FhlwPMURVPn%0Axr3VzgdQ8T2J4wrTjGk%3D%0A)

Under **Choose authorized account** , click on **Authorize new account** and
add the Google Account.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374354008/f7340ff71087e6b74821e7d5690a/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image5f08.png?expires=1771547400&signature=1ab24371db11dd2728c3a500bb3abc5e209632d74e829adba523e8cf04ceef2c&req=dSMgEsp7mYFfUfMW1HO4zc%2F%2Bnv9xPvxWx%2B%2BJ5Cv0CCTiqFBRxEh0t1shHWOD%0AWRdnHjI7pbgN8%2FYmMkM%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374354008/f7340ff71087e6b74821e7d5690a/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image5f08.png?expires=1771547400&signature=1ab24371db11dd2728c3a500bb3abc5e209632d74e829adba523e8cf04ceef2c&req=dSMgEsp7mYFfUfMW1HO4zc%2F%2Bnv9xPvxWx%2B%2BJ5Cv0CCTiqFBRxEh0t1shHWOD%0AWRdnHjI7pbgN8%2FYmMkM%3D%0A)

Select where you want the data to sync. You can either select:

  * An existing spreadsheet from your Google Drive or,

  * Create a new spreadsheet. 

Note: in order to sync to an existing Google Sheet you'll need to ensure the
account you selected has Edit access to the target Google Sheet.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374354438/b995fb2df666d30d3eb83199bab2/file-3XzPYDG5UP.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-3XzPYDG5UP21e4.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=316f9a54d3f6eb320a07e405ea92531d4e6c080cba418d558b37685d4ec632ea&req=dSMgEsp7mYVcUfMW1HO4zX5cRObYN6fOyIxnm3jeNn%2BL9%2FaOjskoQfDaYJB9%0A8E9kJcZgG7EVdmyXxVY%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374354438/b995fb2df666d30d3eb83199bab2/file-3XzPYDG5UP.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-3XzPYDG5UP21e4.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=316f9a54d3f6eb320a07e405ea92531d4e6c080cba418d558b37685d4ec632ea&req=dSMgEsp7mYVcUfMW1HO4zX5cRObYN6fOyIxnm3jeNn%2BL9%2FaOjskoQfDaYJB9%0A8E9kJcZgG7EVdmyXxVY%3D%0A)

Choose which data you'd like to sync to your Google sheet.

  * Changed or added data

  * Only changed data

  * Only added data

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1384602592/334967e0047d1cd674d629a89146/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image2568.png?expires=1771547400&signature=3af5770c7f66b26e1b95ae1b11034c0a2312f79cb1b4d2f4dc1b63eb80fb3329&req=dSMvEs9%2Bn4RWW%2FMW1HO4zbSz7%2BLjsA3Q73ijTvxevgY4%2BP2XdlD3hudrW2fZ%0AsBNojBt%2BP2KhM4KU%2Bfc%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1384602592/334967e0047d1cd674d629a89146/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image2568.png?expires=1771547400&signature=3af5770c7f66b26e1b95ae1b11034c0a2312f79cb1b4d2f4dc1b63eb80fb3329&req=dSMvEs9%2Bn4RWW%2FMW1HO4zbSz7%2BLjsA3Q73ijTvxevgY4%2BP2XdlD3hudrW2fZ%0AsBNojBt%2BP2KhM4KU%2Bfc%3D%0A)

Run your task on the **Overview** tab to start syncing. Make sure the
extracted texts appear in your Google Sheet in a new tab.

Every time you run your task or it runs to do a monitoring check, it will
append the newly captured data to your Google sheet.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374354815/7e8599341cfd642e76cab65a7b3c/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image6488.png?expires=1771547400&signature=cb5738b9d1016e67eebce7a80acb408babca98b559581b500ffe8c8a54298c6f&req=dSMgEsp7mYleXPMW1HO4zf50AuSk7kkYo1j9dk1jcTwiRq%2Fa%2Bcp24WZeFSlU%0A5jYyvyDkDYc%2Blyn0eqQ%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374354815/7e8599341cfd642e76cab65a7b3c/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image6488.png?expires=1771547400&signature=cb5738b9d1016e67eebce7a80acb408babca98b559581b500ffe8c8a54298c6f&req=dSMgEsp7mYleXPMW1HO4zf50AuSk7kkYo1j9dk1jcTwiRq%2Fa%2Bcp24WZeFSlU%0A5jYyvyDkDYc%2Blyn0eqQ%3D%0A)

**NOTE:** If you opt to create a new robot and select the existing integrated
Google sheet as the integration option for your new robot, the information for
your new robot will appear on a fresh worksheet in the same Google sheet.

# Combining data from multiple robots into a single worksheet

Want to see data from multiple robots in one worksheet? You can use Google
Sheets formulas to combine data from different tabs.

# I can't remember which Google Sheet I've connected with my robot.

You can quickly get to the Google Sheet that's connected to your robot, from
the **Integrate** tab.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374357469/7f668ab83da0998b7e2e5c76a6e3/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagef938.png?expires=1771547400&signature=b27589cf69c42a4cc1d7dd0e725872e4a48a72a95be63d8aea403c205933d0a2&req=dSMgEsp7moVZUPMW1HO4zZn9Wq7jv%2F41HbhMK%2B4G2XyIn3T4YpjDZzROuMi%2B%0AX7f7M8fg%2BMUNCl2EU48%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374357469/7f668ab83da0998b7e2e5c76a6e3/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagef938.png?expires=1771547400&signature=b27589cf69c42a4cc1d7dd0e725872e4a48a72a95be63d8aea403c205933d0a2&req=dSMgEsp7moVZUPMW1HO4zZn9Wq7jv%2F41HbhMK%2B4G2XyIn3T4YpjDZzROuMi%2B%0AX7f7M8fg%2BMUNCl2EU48%3D%0A)

# Why is data not appearing in Google Sheets after integrating with a robot?

If you have integrated a Browse AI robot with Google Sheets but notice that no
data is appearing in the sheet, this could be due to one of several reasons:

### Problem: Selecting the incorrect sheet or tab within the spreadsheet

### Solution: Verify the Selected Sheet or Tab

  * Open the Google Sheet linked to your robot integration.

  * Check the bottom of the spreadsheet and ensure the correct sheet or tab is selected. For instance, you may have multiple tabs within the spreadsheet, and the robot may be outputting data to a tab that is not currently visible or selected. By confirming that the appropriate tab is active, you can ensure a seamless data transfer in your integration setup. 

### Problem: The integration was added after the robot already ran the task

### Solution: Run the task _after_ the integration with Google Sheets

  * Make sure that if you've already run the task, that you re-run it after the Google Sheets integration has been established.

  * Re-check to see if the data has made it to the connected sheet.

How to send to Microsoft Excel using Zapier

# How to send to Microsoft Excel using Zapier

This guide shows you how to automatically send data scraped by your Browse AI
robots directly into Microsoft Excel using Zapier.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 11 months ago

Table of contents

# Prerequisites

  * A Browse AI account with at least one trained robot that extracts data

  * A Zapier account

  * Microsoft Excel (desktop application or online version with OneDrive)

# Video walkthrough

# Step-by-step instructions

## Part 1: Prepare your Excel spreadsheet

Before setting up the Zapier integration, you need to prepare your Excel
spreadsheet:

  1. **Create a new Excel spreadsheet** or open an existing one

  2. **Set up the header row** :

     * For best results, copy the column headers directly from your Browse AI robot's data table

     * Go to your Browse AI robot's results

     * Copy the column names you want to import

     * Paste them into the first row of your Excel spreadsheet

     * This ensures your column names match exactly with the data fields from Browse AI

## Part 2: Building the Zapier integration

  1. **Create a new Zap in Zapier**

     * Log in to your Zapier account

     * Click "Create Zap"

  2. **Set up the Browse AI trigger**

     * Search for and select "Browse AI" as the trigger app

     * For the trigger event, select "New Task" (this is the only option)

     * Click "Continue"

     * Connect your Browse AI account if not already connected

     * Click "Continue"

  3. **Configure the Browse AI trigger**

     * Select your workspace/team in Browse AI (important if you're a member of multiple workspaces)

     * Select your robot from the dropdown list

     * For Event Type, select "Task Execution Finish Successfully"

     * Click "Continue"

     * Click "Test Trigger" to pull in sample data from your robot

     * Review the sample data to ensure it looks correct

     * Click "Continue with selected record"

  4. **Add the Microsoft Excel action**

     * Search for and select "Microsoft Excel" as the action app

     * **Important:** Choose "Add Rows" (plural with line item support) as the action event

       * Do NOT select "Add Row" (singular) as this will put all your data into one row

     * Connect your Microsoft Excel account if not already connected

     * Click "Continue"

  5. **Configure the Excel action**

     * Select your OneDrive location (if applicable)

     * Select your spreadsheet from the dropdown list

       * If you don't see it, click "Load More Results"

     * Select the worksheet where you want the data to appear

     * Map the data fields from Browse AI to your Excel columns:

       * For each column field, type "/" and then "list" to filter your options

       * Select the corresponding field from your robot's data (e.g., position, name, description)

       * Make sure each column in your spreadsheet is mapped to the correct data field

     * Click "Continue"

     * Test the action to make sure data is sent correctly to Excel

  6. **Publish your Zap**

     * Review your Zap setup

     * Give your Zap a descriptive name

     * Click "Publish" or "Turn on Zap"

## Part 3: Testing the integration

  1. **Run your Browse AI robot**

     * Go to your Browse AI dashboard

     * Navigate to your robot

     * Run the robot on a different dataset if desired (for example, scrape a different category)

     * The Zap will trigger instantly when the robot finishes successfully

  2. **Check your Excel spreadsheet**

     * Open or refresh your Excel spreadsheet

     * Verify that the new data has been added correctly

     * Each item from your robot should appear as a separate row in Excel

# Troubleshooting

  * **If no data appears in Excel:**

    * Verify your Zap is turned on

    * Check that your Browse AI robot ran successfully

    * Look at Zap history for any errors

  * **If all data appears in a single row:**

    * Make sure you selected "Add Rows" (plural) and not "Add Row" (singular) in the Excel action

  * **If column data is mismatched:**

    * Double-check that your Excel header row exactly matches the field names in your mapping

    * Ensure you've selected the correct fields from the Browse AI data

  * **If you can 't find your spreadsheet:**

    * Click "Load More Results" in the spreadsheet selection dropdown

    * Make sure your spreadsheet is saved in the connected OneDrive account

# Additional tips

  * You can set up your robot to run on a schedule in Browse AI, and the data will automatically flow to Excel

  * This same workflow can be extended to send data to other destinations beyond Excel

  * For large datasets, be aware of any rate limits or row limitations in your Excel plan

* * *

How to manually run robots on demand using Zapier

# How to manually run robots on demand using Zapier

If you'd like more manual control over when your robots run.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 11 months ago

Table of contents

The video below starts at 3:07, where we show how to use Zapier's Chrome
extension as a trigger for a Zap that can run your robot.

# Prerequisites

  * A Browse AI account with a trained robot for data extraction

  * A Zapier account

  * The Zapier Chrome extension installed

  * Google Sheets (or another destination for your data)

# Step-by-Step Instructions

## 1\. Create a Zap in Zapier

  1. Log in to your Zapier account

  2. Click "Create Zap"

  3. Set up the trigger:

     * Select "Chrome Web Extension" as the trigger app

     * Choose "Push" as the trigger event (this will allow you to trigger your workflow from any web page)

     * Complete the trigger setup and test it

## 2\. Set Up Browse AI as the Action

  1. Add an action step to your Zap

  2. Select "Browse AI" as the app

  3. Choose "Run Task" as the action

  4. Connect your Browse AI account if not already connected

  5. Select the specific robot you want to trigger (the one trained to extract company details)

  6. Configure any necessary robot parameters or options

  7. Test the Browse AI action step

**NOTE:** The most important part of that Zap is where we use the Tab URL from
the trigger step and map that to the Origin URL field for Browse AI.  
​

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374366976/6a82a061e4933fc7479d252bc424/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image936b.png?expires=1771547400&signature=1bad8cbd1f30436d7c7cbc8c53828b8c7a07a3e88f318823b30d9b68598f90fa&req=dSMgEsp4m4hYX%2FMW1HO4zQ3EsG6NlXi9J%2BqN5MYfmVBmCt3H7lreXsU4Kf%2Bx%0ANGPSj0oSIkgV%2FCSQZgk%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374366976/6a82a061e4933fc7479d252bc424/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image936b.png?expires=1771547400&signature=1bad8cbd1f30436d7c7cbc8c53828b8c7a07a3e88f318823b30d9b68598f90fa&req=dSMgEsp4m4hYX%2FMW1HO4zQ3EsG6NlXi9J%2BqN5MYfmVBmCt3H7lreXsU4Kf%2Bx%0ANGPSj0oSIkgV%2FCSQZgk%3D%0A)

## 3\. Using the Workflow

  1. Navigate to a web page containing the data you want to extract (e.g., a company page on Y Combinator)

  2. Click the Zapier Chrome extension icon in your browser

  3. Select the Zap you created from the extension popup

  4. Trigger the Zap by clicking on it

  5. The Zap will:

     * Execute your Browse AI robot

     * Extract the data from the current page

     * Add the extracted information to your destination (e.g., Google Sheets if you've enabled that integration in Browse AI)

## 4\. Checking Your Results

  1. Go to your destination (this could be Tables in Browse AI or a third-party integration)

  2. Verify that the new data has been added correctly

  3. The data should include all fields your robot was trained to extract, such as:

     * Company name

     * Founding date

     * Team size

     * Company website

     * Available job positions

     * And any other configured data points

# Troubleshooting

  * If the data isn't appearing in your destination, check that all Zap steps were successfully completed

  * Ensure your Browse AI robot is correctly trained for the specific page structure

  * Verify that all necessary connections between Zapier, Browse AI, and your destination are working

# Advanced Usage

You can extend this workflow by:

  * Adding conditional logic in Zapier

  * Setting up multiple destinations for your data

  * Creating filters to only process certain types of data

  * Adding notification steps to alert you when new data is processed

* * *

microsoft-excel-using-zapier.html)[How to connect Browse AI with
Zapier](10536328-how-to-connect-browse-ai-with-zapier.html)[How to run your

[Google Sheets Add-on](../collections/16328940-google-sheets-add-on.html)

How to automatically remove duplicate rows of data in Google Sheets

# How to automatically remove duplicate rows of data in Google Sheets

Keep your data from Browse AI acurate and organized by automatically removing
duplicate entries in your synced Google Sheet.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 12 months ago

Table of contents

The Browse AI Google Sheets integration appends data to your spreadsheet by
default. This article walks you through how you can use Browse AI's Google
Sheets add-on to automatically delete duplicate rows to keep only the most up
to date data in your spreadsheet.

# Prerequisites

  * An approved robot where you've already enabled the Google Sheets integration

  * The Google Sheet that you'd like to remove duplicates

  * The [Browse AI for Sheets add-on](10536316-how-to-scrape-data-from-a-google-sheet-using-browse-ai-s-google-sheets-add-on.html) installed

# Quick set up guide

Open your Google Sheet, and click slick **Extensions** in the menu bar. Select
**Browse AI for Sheets** and **Login.** Login to your Browse AI account.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374369928/d78263d02e3d72d0f053a78b1baf/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imaged873.png?expires=1771547400&signature=7a54d5971d7172388b420932f70ee244b575f4d972714fcceeba71f98f0ed44e&req=dSMgEsp4lIhdUfMW1HO4zebssUBl4M%2FfO3GiHbXYjV8rjLmxmmfarkfMQWJ%2F%0AfGAw3d1CYLzBmm%2FQbw4%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374369928/d78263d02e3d72d0f053a78b1baf/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imaged873.png?expires=1771547400&signature=7a54d5971d7172388b420932f70ee244b575f4d972714fcceeba71f98f0ed44e&req=dSMgEsp4lIhdUfMW1HO4zebssUBl4M%2FfO3GiHbXYjV8rjLmxmmfarkfMQWJ%2F%0AfGAw3d1CYLzBmm%2FQbw4%3D%0A)

Once logged in, go back to your Google Sheet, click slick **Extensions** in
the menu bar. Select **Browse AI for Sheets** and **Experimental features.**

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374370269/b7797081a6a5abc4b12dddeefceb/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image20f2.png?expires=1771547400&signature=34c2698a476fef20e79483554911a4f7235a17893b611a4b3c4b5c2e22fa6421&req=dSMgEsp5nYNZUPMW1HO4zfQURo3dyhLJM01N6tY%2BwdpUqmY5x4NTGNxqpcT8%0Ay%2FImGLw%2B3YTIaJ10Ozg%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374370269/b7797081a6a5abc4b12dddeefceb/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image20f2.png?expires=1771547400&signature=34c2698a476fef20e79483554911a4f7235a17893b611a4b3c4b5c2e22fa6421&req=dSMgEsp5nYNZUPMW1HO4zfQURo3dyhLJM01N6tY%2BwdpUqmY5x4NTGNxqpcT8%0Ay%2FImGLw%2B3YTIaJ10Ozg%3D%0A)

In the opened sidebar, click on the **Auto-delete duplicate rows when I open
this spreadsheet** checkbox to enable the feature and enable the two inputs
below.

  * Comparison columns: enter the column headers you want to compare (e.g., title, description)

  * Sheet name: enter the exact name of your current sheet

When you've done select **Save changes**.

Note: only specify which columns it to compare to detect duplicate rows. In
this example below, if the title and description in any rows are identical, it
will delete the newly added rows which are duplicates.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374370660/950b6c052517af692032d77dd964/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image618c.png?expires=1771547400&signature=f01973dc140fb1e2de94f6919586f0852c20d98a00edc1104ad925b6d87ffee5&req=dSMgEsp5nYdZWfMW1HO4zUpYXCiKZmYBQuJ%2F6%2FesE19z7lBHPI%2BbSUikLg0t%0AxNRRCDEYahCSJ%2FKHOaw%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374370660/950b6c052517af692032d77dd964/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image618c.png?expires=1771547400&signature=f01973dc140fb1e2de94f6919586f0852c20d98a00edc1104ad925b6d87ffee5&req=dSMgEsp5nYdZWfMW1HO4zUpYXCiKZmYBQuJ%2F6%2FesE19z7lBHPI%2BbSUikLg0t%0AxNRRCDEYahCSJ%2FKHOaw%3D%0A)

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374371304/fcea431d87ad85a1018ff4e0ec67/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image6ec2.png?expires=1771547400&signature=e2af86b6e691eab122c2e212c9ffba519b42e5efc1c49b22afd337ab815e05e7&req=dSMgEsp5nIJfXfMW1HO4zctyHAojB0WkytX3zf9uU7pE1VjWudHQguWVwxsq%0Ah%2B7TU6xDQIssfx6Ru1g%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374371304/fcea431d87ad85a1018ff4e0ec67/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image6ec2.png?expires=1771547400&signature=e2af86b6e691eab122c2e212c9ffba519b42e5efc1c49b22afd337ab815e05e7&req=dSMgEsp5nIJfXfMW1HO4zctyHAojB0WkytX3zf9uU7pE1VjWudHQguWVwxsq%0Ah%2B7TU6xDQIssfx6Ru1g%3D%0A)

# How it works

The add-on compares rows based on your selected columns, ignoring data that
naturally varies (like dates or timestamps). When duplicates are found, the
newer entries are removed, keeping your original data intact.

##

# Managing settings

You can modify or disable this feature anytime:

  1. Go to Extensions > Browse AI for Sheets > Experimental features

  2. Adjust your column selections or uncheck the auto-delete option  
​

Available integrations with Browse AI

# Available integrations with Browse AI

Browse AI connects with popular tools and platforms to help you automate your
data workflows.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 3 months ago

Table of contents

# How do I view all available integrations from my dashboard?

Select any approved robot and select the **Integrate** tab. From there you
will see a full list. To integrate any robot to the integrations below, simply
select the desired integration from this screen.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1498708382/2a2aaf716de4b871461ef032f889/CleanShot%202025-04-28%20at%2015.12.09%25402x.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27CleanShot%25202025-04-28%2520at%252015.12.09%25402x5de3.png?expires=1771547400&signature=e3630a5f1fc3722c2a4b3b3e41ecbeb2e0579c8cf2f9f353b39ce9818e0345a5&req=dSQuHs5%2BlYJXW%2FMW1HO4zc1pue0N2RctolQnanmkPXdkDmIzmlCypVPyfpUk%0AAbwffujGcb4IuEmhnn4%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1498708382/2a2aaf716de4b871461ef032f889/CleanShot%202025-04-28%20at%2015.12.09%25402x.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27CleanShot%25202025-04-28%2520at%252015.12.09%25402x5de3.png?expires=1771547400&signature=e3630a5f1fc3722c2a4b3b3e41ecbeb2e0579c8cf2f9f353b39ce9818e0345a5&req=dSQuHs5%2BlYJXW%2FMW1HO4zc1pue0N2RctolQnanmkPXdkDmIzmlCypVPyfpUk%0AAbwffujGcb4IuEmhnn4%3D%0A)

# What native integrations do you offer, and what do they do?

## Google Sheets Integration

* * *

The [Google Sheets integration](10535898-how-to-sync-scraped-data-with-google-
sheets.html) enables you to sync your data from a robots into a Googe Sheet.
Connect your robot's data to new or existing spreadsheets with flexible sync
options:

  * Sync all changed or added data

  * Sync only changed data

  * Sync only newly added data

Integrating your data with Google Sheets allows you to manipulate, analyze,
combine, and transform the data based on your use case. This includes:

  * Analyze the data you've extracted

  * Transform the data you've extracted

  * [Combine extracted data from multiple robots](10536304-how-to-track-failed-tasks-and-combine-data-in-google-sheets.html)

  * Build live dashboards on top of live data-sets

  * Feed data you've extracted into LLM models

## Google Sheets Add-on

* * *

The [Google Sheets add-
on](https://workspace.google.com/marketplace/app/browse_ai_for_sheets/604105196134)
allows you scrape web data in bulk (up to 500,000 rows) directly from a Google
Sheet. [Read more about that here](10536316-how-to-scrape-data-from-a-google-
sheet-using-browse-ai-s-google-sheets-add-on.html).

Note that this add-on is not available via the Integrations tab and needs to
be set up separately.

#

## Zapier

* * *

Automate your workflows by connecting Browse AI with thousands of other apps
through Zapier. Our Zapier integration supports both triggers and actions,
letting you create powerful automation workflows.

Read our full [Zapier Integration Guide](10536328-how-to-connect-browse-ai-
with-zapier.html) to get started.

With our Zapier integration you can:

  * [Automatically update an Excel spreadsheet with live data](https://exc/)

  * [Run robots on demand using trigger automations](10535944-how-to-manually-run-robots-on-demand-using-zapier.html)

  * Create automations when new data is extracted, when a monitor detects a change, when a task is completed, and more.

  * Connect the data you extract with over 7,000+ tools and apps.

## Webhooks

* * *

Send your extracted data to any endpoint using webhooks. Configure
notifications for:

  * Successful monitoring checks with detected changes

  * Task completion (with or without errors)

  * Task completion is successful (no errors)

  * Task completion with errors 

Webhook triggers:

  * Task completion (success or error)

  * Data change detection

  * Monitoring alerts

Configuration options:

  * Custom endpoint URLs

  * Event filtering

  * Automatic retries

  * Secure delivery

You can view the full documentation for our Webhooks
[here](https://www.browse.ai/docs/api/v2#tag/webhooks).

## REST API

* * *

Build custom integrations using our comprehensive REST API:

  * Create and manage robots

  * Run tasks programmatically

  * Monitor extraction results

You can view our API documentation
[here](https://www.browse.ai/docs/api/v2#tag/webhooks).

## Airtable

* * *

Streamline your data management by connecting Browse AI directly to Airtable.
You can automatically sync data from a robot, and customize based on adding
only:

  * Changed or added data

  * Only changed data

  * Only added data

You can access the full [Airtable Integration Guide](10537018-how-to-enable-
airtable-integration.html) here.

## Pabbly Connect

* * *

The Pabbly Connect integration enables you to create automate workflows with
the data you've extracted. Connect your robots to other applications through
official triggers and actions:

  * Execute robot tasks based on triggers

  * Run bulk operations

  * Transform extracted data automatically

  * Send data to other connected apps

Key automation options:

  * Monitor for data changes

  * React to task completion

  * Process successful extractions

  * Handle failed tasks

  * Transform data for downstream use

You can access our full [Pabbly Integration Guide](10537045-how-to-integrate-
browse-ai-with-pabbly-connect.html) here.

## Make.com

* * *

The Make.com integration enables you to create visual workflows and automate
data processing from Browse AI without coding.

Connect your robots to other applications through our official integration:

  * Watch for task execution events

  * Process extracted data automatically

  * Create custom automation scenarios

  * Connect with thousands of apps

Key automation options:

  * Monitor for data changes

  * Process completed tasks

  * Handle successful extractions

  * Manage failed tasks

  * Transform data through visual workflows

You can access the full [Make.com Integration Guide](10573987-how-to-
integrate-your-robot-with-make-com.html) here.

## Browse AI Workflows

* * *

The Workflows feature enables you to connect two robots together to extract
data from both list pages and detail pages. Browse AI allows for sequential
automation by chaining workflows together. For example, one workflow can use a
robot to identify links, and another can use the output links to retrieve
required data. Additionally, dynamic workflows are available where output from
one robot propagates automatically to the next, ensuring real-time data-driven
automation.

  * Chain two robots together

  * Extract data from detail pages automatically

  * Schedule automated monitoring

  * Execute bulk runs

  * Filter based on data changes

  * Add integrations to workflow outputs

  * Set up sequential task robots, including chaining workflows for three or more robots

  * Enable dynamic robot interactions where outputs automatically feed into the next robot

You can access our full [Workflows](10459476-workflows-how-can-i-create-a-
workflow-connecting-two-robots.html) guide here.

## AWS (beta)

* * *

The Amazon S3 integration allows you to securely store your extracted data in
your own AWS S3 bucket. This integration gives you full control over your data
storage while maintaining security best practices.

**Set up your S3 bucket to receive data with:**

  * Secure CloudFormation template deployment 

  * IAM role-based access control 

  * Optional folder-specific permissions 

**Key features:**

  * Store extracted data directly in your own infrastructure 

  * Maintain complete ownership of your data 

  * Configure access at bucket or folder level 

  * Secure authentication using external IDs 

You can access our full [S3 Integration Guide](10715592-integrating-browse-ai-
with-amazon-s3.html) to set up this connection using AWS CloudFormation
templates without any coding required.

* * *

pages](10518596-workflows-how-to-connect-robots-together-to-scrape-across-

[Google Sheets](../collections/11625310-google-sheets.html)

How to track failed tasks and combine data in Google Sheets

# How to track failed tasks and combine data in Google Sheets

Learn how to identify which tasks failed during a Bulk Run and how to merge
data from multiple robots into a single Google Sheet.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 12 months ago

Table of contents

# Prerequisites

  * Approved robots where the [Google Sheets integration is already enabled](10535898-how-to-sync-scraped-data-with-google-sheets.html)

  * Basic familiarity with Google Sheets formulas

# How to identify failed tasks from a bulk run

  1. Open your Google Sheet containing the bulk run results

  2. Add a new column in robot B's sheet

  3. Add the formula below to track failed tasks (you'll need to adjust the formula for your data)

  4. Copy the formula down to all rows in the new column

    
    
    =IFERROR(IF(INDEX(FILTER('robotA'!$A$2:$D, SEARCH(C2,'robotA'!$D$2:$D)), 1, 4) = C2, "Scraped", "Not Scraped"), "Not Scraped")

The formula will show "Scraped" for successful tasks and "Not Scraped" for
failed ones.

# How to combine data from two robots

  1. Open the Google Sheet containing robot A's data

  2. Add a new column where you want the combined data

  3. Add the formula below to bring in data from robot B (you'll need to adjust the formula for your data)

  4. Copy the formula down to all rows in the new column

    
    
    =INDEX(FILTER('robotB'!$A$2:$D, SEARCH(D2,'robotB'!$C$2:$C)), 1, 4)

The formula will pull matching data from robot B into robot A's sheet.

## Tips for success

  * Double check your sheet names match exactly in the formulas

  * Adjust column references (like $A$2:$D) based on your sheet layout

  * Test the formulas on a few rows before copying to all rows

# Tutorial - Identifying failed tasks and combining data from ycombinator.com

In this example:

  * We've set up a bulk run on ycombinator.com to extract a list of companies (robot A), and the company detail (robot B).

  * After the bulk run is complete, the data was stored in separate sheets in Google Sheets via the Google Sheets integration—one for the list data (robot A -ycombinator A) and another for the detail data (robot B - ycombinator B). 

## Determining which tasks failed

After the integration, in the new column of the sheet for Robot B, utilize the
following formula for the new column:

    
    
    =IFERROR(IF(INDEX(FILTER('ycombinator A'!$A$2:$D, SEARCH(C2,'ycombinator A'!$D$2:$D)), 1, 4) = C2, "Scraped", "Not Scraped"), "Not Scraped")

### How does the formula work?

  * Utilizes the filter function to extract data from robot A (ycombinator A) within columns A to D. 

  * It searches for the corresponding data in robot B (ycombinator B) column C2. 

  * If the data is successfully scraped, it displays "Scraped"; otherwise, it shows "Not Scraped."

  * The formula also handles any errors that may occur during the process.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374560662/12080807b2e665bb7ad1531a4aac/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imageded6.png?expires=1771547400&signature=f9bd8b1f7681de0a0846703ad185adb943a150a72e12246a5dc85f2220c10a9f&req=dSMgEsx4nYdZW%2FMW1HO4zaN%2Fh%2F%2BQxGj6TRZ9IYN1X%2BDeeNi5NpTJMBpx85Iq%0A%2B01Zd995mG6YLXc0EyI%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374560662/12080807b2e665bb7ad1531a4aac/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imageded6.png?expires=1771547400&signature=f9bd8b1f7681de0a0846703ad185adb943a150a72e12246a5dc85f2220c10a9f&req=dSMgEsx4nYdZW%2FMW1HO4zaN%2Fh%2F%2BQxGj6TRZ9IYN1X%2BDeeNi5NpTJMBpx85Iq%0A%2B01Zd995mG6YLXc0EyI%3D%0A)

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374560879/27d667cc5d0809d37343376533f3/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagefe19.png?expires=1771547400&signature=b9bc3e3150914c957bc47ecd90b9f0cbf9899d1ac7093f5a26a413940568a789&req=dSMgEsx4nYlYUPMW1HO4zRxOsjvqPfD3439ZEu5wpXE7qTkJtj%2B6jV4JuCTd%0AtuTDwvytc7VEzq3wL6M%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374560879/27d667cc5d0809d37343376533f3/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagefe19.png?expires=1771547400&signature=b9bc3e3150914c957bc47ecd90b9f0cbf9899d1ac7093f5a26a413940568a789&req=dSMgEsx4nYlYUPMW1HO4zRxOsjvqPfD3439ZEu5wpXE7qTkJtj%2B6jV4JuCTd%0AtuTDwvytc7VEzq3wL6M%3D%0A)

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374561045/3250e0a8686867dcb17f6df47e75/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imaged93f.png?expires=1771547400&signature=95b067037d7d340ee248912b714ccb89145508ef98c4f843f03f4711375f1ca7&req=dSMgEsx4nIFbXPMW1HO4zdJLAf2jIkmPK7rbfpFnxcQk%2BEVbuvMSTRv7fMYA%0AETrKY92JLNf6pS08Xrc%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374561045/3250e0a8686867dcb17f6df47e75/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imaged93f.png?expires=1771547400&signature=95b067037d7d340ee248912b714ccb89145508ef98c4f843f03f4711375f1ca7&req=dSMgEsx4nIFbXPMW1HO4zdJLAf2jIkmPK7rbfpFnxcQk%2BEVbuvMSTRv7fMYA%0AETrKY92JLNf6pS08Xrc%3D%0A)

## Bring the data from both robots into one Google Sheet

To bring the detailed data from robot B into the sheet of robot A, we can
utilize the following formula:

    
    
    =INDEX(FILTER('ycombinator B'!$A$2:$D, SEARCH(D2,'ycombinator B'!$C$2:$C)), 1, 4)

## How does the formula work?

  * Filters the data from "_ycombinator B "_ based on the search term in column D of "y _combinator A "_

  * The index function extracts the desired details and brings them into "_ycombinator A_ ".

  * You can repeat this process for other data points you want to consolidate, allowing you to access all the necessary information in one sheet.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374561261/126c2e7047238c238922c05c95b6/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image720a.png?expires=1771547400&signature=330d2117b428bc61caf9596d351bc89c708f824a7c69794584e7f371e5a12742&req=dSMgEsx4nINZWPMW1HO4zWwgnOg9lxn32ENNXcGoCzhFdxqQgJK0OLV9B0VE%0ARxOBKRuOkrY%2F7DJ8rvU%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374561261/126c2e7047238c238922c05c95b6/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image720a.png?expires=1771547400&signature=330d2117b428bc61caf9596d351bc89c708f824a7c69794584e7f371e5a12742&req=dSMgEsx4nINZWPMW1HO4zWwgnOg9lxn32ENNXcGoCzhFdxqQgJK0OLV9B0VE%0ARxOBKRuOkrY%2F7DJ8rvU%3D%0A)

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374561450/bfde4115cb121fbb281a13c895bc/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imageb7f0.png?expires=1771547400&signature=bc6ceefe549e8728bc6fa6dcd6a5e59139be24a6bc4a5eeaa52f0157bdce858b&req=dSMgEsx4nIVaWfMW1HO4zYwzrf5%2BELOhCLCC6VEDMDCpNNw00dS9gcFdDAPM%0A22P%2FU9stBpJ71YvjpsI%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374561450/bfde4115cb121fbb281a13c895bc/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imageb7f0.png?expires=1771547400&signature=bc6ceefe549e8728bc6fa6dcd6a5e59139be24a6bc4a5eeaa52f0157bdce858b&req=dSMgEsx4nIVaWfMW1HO4zYwzrf5%2BELOhCLCC6VEDMDCpNNw00dS9gcFdDAPM%0A22P%2FU9stBpJ71YvjpsI%3D%0A)

How to scrape data from a Google Sheet using Browse AI's Google Sheets add-on

# How to scrape data from a Google Sheet using Browse AI's Google Sheets add-
on

Learn how to automate data extraction tasks in Google Sheets by running your
Browse AI robots directly from spreadsheet rows.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 9 months ago

Table of contents

# What is Browse AI's for Google Sheets add-on?

Browse AI for Google Sheets is an add-on that lets you run your robots
directly from your google sheet.

You can automate up to 500,000 extraction tasks by using spreadsheet rows as
inputs, making it perfect for bulk data extraction without needing API
knowledge.

The add-on also offers additional features including:

  * [Auto-deleting duplicate rows](https://intercom.help/browse-ai-089fe61c5dc3/en/articles/10535955-how-can-i-have-duplicate-rows-deleted-automatically-from-google-sheets-integration)

  * [Auto-deleting old rows](https://intercom.help/browse-ai-089fe61c5dc3/en/articles/10537011-how-can-i-only-keep-new-rows-in-my-google-sheet-and-have-older-rows-deleted-automatically)

## Key benefits

  * Run multiple extraction tasks at once using spreadsheet data

  * Extract data from detail pages associated with list items

  * Automatically remove duplicate or outdated rows

  * Keep all your inputs and outputs in one organized spreadsheet

  * No coding or API knowledge required

## Example Use Case

Imagine that you want to scape the newest and shiniest software products from
ProductHunt. Using Browse AI, you would create a robot. Your robot takes
“Search Keyword” and “Max Products” as input and extracts products related to
that specific search keyword.

With the Google Sheets add-on, you can create a Google Sheet with 2 columns
for your robot input parameters ("Search Keyword" and "Max Products") and run
tasks to scrape all of them at once.

# Getting started

## Install the add-on

  1. Visit [Google Workspace Marketplace](https://workspace.google.com/marketplace)

  2. Search for [Browse AI for Sheets](https://workspace.google.com/marketplace/app/browse_ai_for_sheets/604105196134)

  3. Click Install

  4. Open any Google Sheet to begin 

## Connect your Browse AI account

  1. Click **Extensions** in the top menu

  2. Select **Browse AI for Sheets**

  3. Click **Login** and authorize the connection

  4. **Accept** the permissions request 

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1390897611/94b03acc509c2338e02921a1e381/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagea45a.png?expires=1771547400&signature=49acc3b674996e45d07f0a220caae42e2e9d214c3d773bd940ea1badbad3f063&req=dSMuFsF3modeWPMW1HO4zdtsk4zrghZKBQ45MUkvGEorvWTsWAzR87MeJSbE%0A27AsdKiUUeOr9zXARk0%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1390897611/94b03acc509c2338e02921a1e381/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagea45a.png?expires=1771547400&signature=49acc3b674996e45d07f0a220caae42e2e9d214c3d773bd940ea1badbad3f063&req=dSMuFsF3modeWPMW1HO4zdtsk4zrghZKBQ45MUkvGEorvWTsWAzR87MeJSbE%0A27AsdKiUUeOr9zXARk0%3D%0A)

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1390897613/e2b3f37a6ff31edbb325abe8d9dd/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image6be3.png?expires=1771547400&signature=fe8135b40ecdeb003116c06f0e48b5f8da8d670a9307a3f8e0e73fecea9e1afc&req=dSMuFsF3modeWvMW1HO4zX%2FF5KBL5io%2Fc%2BgFhfy6Tr1fhUUBLQ0MI3g47GFd%0Aru5ZtUTJqKBlqaw3s9k%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1390897613/e2b3f37a6ff31edbb325abe8d9dd/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image6be3.png?expires=1771547400&signature=fe8135b40ecdeb003116c06f0e48b5f8da8d670a9307a3f8e0e73fecea9e1afc&req=dSMuFsF3modeWvMW1HO4zX%2FF5KBL5io%2Fc%2BgFhfy6Tr1fhUUBLQ0MI3g47GFd%0Aru5ZtUTJqKBlqaw3s9k%3D%0A)

## Set up your spreadsheet

  1. Open your robot in [Browse AI dashboard](https://dashboard.browse.ai/)

  2. Note the input parameter names from the **Run** tab

  3. Create columns in your spreadsheet matching these parameter names

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1390897612/4253936b2056b323df17e7e982cf/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagef275.png?expires=1771547400&signature=3d8bf4a205452af6a1c440129b4e003f60765f409cc45139a0b22bcc797fabaa&req=dSMuFsF3modeW%2FMW1HO4zTXr0iLWmIseRq2bqH6aH1lHurxAJHkMlva48p8g%0AFDYbDt5duvKAHPsBQPU%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1390897612/4253936b2056b323df17e7e982cf/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagef275.png?expires=1771547400&signature=3d8bf4a205452af6a1c440129b4e003f60765f409cc45139a0b22bcc797fabaa&req=dSMuFsF3modeW%2FMW1HO4zTXr0iLWmIseRq2bqH6aH1lHurxAJHkMlva48p8g%0AFDYbDt5duvKAHPsBQPU%3D%0A)

Your Google Sheet should look like this:

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1390897618/ae9bebd8970d80f831d9a2e4ad1b/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image0de4.png?expires=1771547400&signature=c0225abe6c5cd16dcb61af7035da1d5826f1f4a37b37217d04e7a23486ffab3f&req=dSMuFsF3modeUfMW1HO4zfHjfEOPQmWqASmaQHwJNh7F0XMd0jmIAu1c0Dmd%0AER62BFsUsLZJ6R%2F85KU%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1390897618/ae9bebd8970d80f831d9a2e4ad1b/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image0de4.png?expires=1771547400&signature=c0225abe6c5cd16dcb61af7035da1d5826f1f4a37b37217d04e7a23486ffab3f&req=dSMuFsF3modeUfMW1HO4zfHjfEOPQmWqASmaQHwJNh7F0XMd0jmIAu1c0Dmd%0AER62BFsUsLZJ6R%2F85KU%3D%0A)

## Run your robot

  1. Enter your desired inputs as rows under your columns

  2. Select the rows you want to process 

  3. Click **Extensions > Browse AI for Sheets**

  4. Select your robot from the menu

  5. Wait for the success message confirming your tasks are running

  6. Check your Robot's History tab to monitor progress

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1390897615/f250bdd5ccf82735e0d060c42629/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image0853.png?expires=1771547400&signature=9fc7ca7d7c308b3dd9fa586ed2fda67538e7a5053dac6b6b133abf5ff9afba93&req=dSMuFsF3modeXPMW1HO4zcKtPMj0qRXvZ8nBtyjCwCIxsTYFgF%2Fp2ZKorFkz%0AvaSEMqPUxE1IwPWeUBo%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1390897615/f250bdd5ccf82735e0d060c42629/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image0853.png?expires=1771547400&signature=9fc7ca7d7c308b3dd9fa586ed2fda67538e7a5053dac6b6b133abf5ff9afba93&req=dSMuFsF3modeXPMW1HO4zcKtPMj0qRXvZ8nBtyjCwCIxsTYFgF%2Fp2ZKorFkz%0AvaSEMqPUxE1IwPWeUBo%3D%0A)

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1390897608/55ae420fe03b2fef3fdcae5d2200/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image31e4.png?expires=1771547400&signature=4589036abda190c99d1e6a7a7aa0453618d3ec711ffcbf3e46d15e29f21107ff&req=dSMuFsF3modfUfMW1HO4zWxzlOCexAT04dkknsv9u%2B80y%2BivqscZuZQpPi1s%0ACrELjy7h7%2FBQW6LTnFA%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1390897608/55ae420fe03b2fef3fdcae5d2200/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image31e4.png?expires=1771547400&signature=4589036abda190c99d1e6a7a7aa0453618d3ec711ffcbf3e46d15e29f21107ff&req=dSMuFsF3modfUfMW1HO4zWxzlOCexAT04dkknsv9u%2B80y%2BivqscZuZQpPi1s%0ACrELjy7h7%2FBQW6LTnFA%3D%0A)

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1390897610/a82939d55627f161b9098f223690/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image0f66.png?expires=1771547400&signature=0998198b38c60eead55af99fedff172ae1e610a8ebf9feced6f19638316f7812&req=dSMuFsF3modeWfMW1HO4zcJ3erTxzcS6FJXjXV0UEwONoxrWwKXlBrIEbCqm%0AP4G2EPo%2BoKFVaYkDb9c%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1390897610/a82939d55627f161b9098f223690/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image0f66.png?expires=1771547400&signature=0998198b38c60eead55af99fedff172ae1e610a8ebf9feced6f19638316f7812&req=dSMuFsF3modeWfMW1HO4zcJ3erTxzcS6FJXjXV0UEwONoxrWwKXlBrIEbCqm%0AP4G2EPo%2BoKFVaYkDb9c%3D%0A)

## Optional: Create a complete workflow

Last, an optional step you can take is to integrate your robot with the same
Google Sheet so that whenever it extracts data, it adds the data to another
tab in the same Google Sheet.

  1. Go to your robot's Integrate tab

  2. Select Google Sheets

  3. Choose your current spreadsheet

  4. New extracted data will automatically appear in a new tab

[Zapier](../collections/16329079-zapier.html)

How to connect Browse AI with Zapier

# How to connect Browse AI with Zapier

Learn how to automate your web data workflows by connecting Browse AI with
thousands of apps through Zapier's powerful integration platform.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 11 months ago

Table of contents

# Prerequisites

To connect Browse AI with Zapier, you'll need:

  * A Browse AI account with at least one approved robot

  * A Zapier account (free or paid plan)

  * The app or service you want to connect with Browse AI 

# What is Zapier?

Zapier is an automation platform that connects different apps and services
together, allowing you to create powerful workflows without coding.

With Zapier, you can send Browse AI's extracted data to over [7,000
apps](https://zapier.com/apps) or trigger Browse AI robots based on events in
other apps.

# Setting up your first Zap

You can create a Zap (automated workflow) in two ways:

## Run a Zap every time a Browse AI Robot executes a task

There are two ways you can start this process.

### Option 1: Start from Browse AI

  1. Go to your Browse AI dashboard

  2. Select your robot

  3. Click the "Integrate" tab

  4. Select the Zapier tile

  5. Choose from suggested templates or search for your desired app

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374569993/7bd9f85948346f2cbd6d4a1d6bc5/file-
FmE39iRoTN.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-
FmE39iRoTNe561.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=60969287ee92290d02dfa6d02a88f531aa2e7ed9283a973bc5213c3495d212bf&req=dSMgEsx4lIhWWvMW1HO4zc%2FgwcjkURjHS74XGR4vfCfJ%2BbTXZresu%2FNgH2J2%0AEL734sGImaGImSOHN2Q%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374569993/7bd9f85948346f2cbd6d4a1d6bc5/file-

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374570011/cd0617cd207f0246e35a157122c4/file-E4Zb6JRji1.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-E4Zb6JRji1e518.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=498e2fc045cd1ea78a7776a96fda9ee7f8f91b2e38ae336533c3c1c82c1f7d82&req=dSMgEsx5nYFeWPMW1HO4zch32VSqKaTwA4ii7w0bs1XmKyOGLmC9rYWEHuhQ%0AcsFSaq8134LMsYQsO5s%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374570011/cd0617cd207f0246e35a157122c4/file-E4Zb6JRji1.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-E4Zb6JRji1e518.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=498e2fc045cd1ea78a7776a96fda9ee7f8f91b2e38ae336533c3c1c82c1f7d82&req=dSMgEsx5nYFeWPMW1HO4zch32VSqKaTwA4ii7w0bs1XmKyOGLmC9rYWEHuhQ%0AcsFSaq8134LMsYQsO5s%3D%0A)

### Option 2: Start from Zapier

  1. Visit the [Browse AI and Zapier integration page](https://zapier.com/apps/browse-ai/integrations)

  2. Search for your desired app or choose from suggested templates

  3. Follow Zapier's guided setup process

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1390927813/de9037716650039d3dd7191899a6/file-

# Configuring your trigger

Now that you have selected the app, you must set up the trigger.

  1. Choose your robot from the dropdown menu

  2. Select your trigger event (note that not all trigger events are available for every integration):

     * Monitor detected changes

     * Task completion (success or error)

     * Successful task completion only

     * Failed task completion only

  3. Test your trigger to ensure it works

  4. Connect to your desired app and map the data fields

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1390929689/a4244c0255f960fa8c1577f83b50/file-9us7SL1ZE8.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-9us7SL1ZE86bc2.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=600a80127e8658a97d2e05c3dd6c638f6dbc1a9b8afb790801256ce3c23aef4f&req=dSMuFsB8lIdXUPMW1HO4zdLzbr0eygVxt5BwA7lmd%2FT6RHEqwhOmIOttfBuP%0Au5GLPQjfNTKmd21yqJw%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1390929689/a4244c0255f960fa8c1577f83b50/file-9us7SL1ZE8.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-9us7SL1ZE86bc2.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=600a80127e8658a97d2e05c3dd6c638f6dbc1a9b8afb790801256ce3c23aef4f&req=dSMuFsB8lIdXUPMW1HO4zdLzbr0eygVxt5BwA7lmd%2FT6RHEqwhOmIOttfBuP%0Au5GLPQjfNTKmd21yqJw%3D%0A)

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1390929688/b59028bdc0a0c51e90f559a7217b/file-
Lw2c18vVQm.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-
Lw2c18vVQmaaa2.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=3968d78d897c4271ffc4eaa7111167a928f4e884c204143197d1a1fd2b3d4312&req=dSMuFsB8lIdXUfMW1HO4zUx9Wk13vbGkJgt%2FMGrx7UwiwA3SNJbXZZFschTI%0AH3QQie9j4ttPU1qkEqU%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1390929688/b59028bdc0a0c51e90f559a7217b/file-
Lw2c18vVQm.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-
Lw2c18vVQmaaa2.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=3968d78d897c4271ffc4eaa7111167a928f4e884c204143197d1a1fd2b3d4312&req=dSMuFsB8lIdXUfMW1HO4zUx9Wk13vbGkJgt%2FMGrx7UwiwA3SNJbXZZFschTI%0AH3QQie9j4ttPU1qkEqU%3D%0A)

# Popular automation examples

Here are some common ways to use Browse AI with Zapier:

  * Send scraped leads directly to your CRM

  * Update spreadsheets with monitored price changes

  * Notify your team on Slack when specific data changes

  * Trigger data extraction when new entries appear in your systems

# Advanced usage: Deep scraping automation

You can connect multiple Browse AI robots through Zapier to create powerful
deep scraping workflows. For example:

  1. Robot A extracts product URLs from category pages

  2. Zapier sends each URL to Robot B

  3. Robot B extracts detailed product information

  4. Zapier sends the complete data to your desired destination

Note: Avoid creating infinite loops by ensuring robots don't trigger
themselves.

# Troubleshooting tips

  * Verify your Browse AI account is properly connected in Zapier

  * Ensure your robot has run successfully at least once

  * Check that all required fields are mapped correctly

  * Monitor your Zapier task history for any errors

* * *

How to remove old rows to keep your Google Sheets data up to date and accurate

# How to remove old rows to keep your Google Sheets data up to date and
accurate

Learn how to automatically remove old data rows from your Google Sheets using
Browse AI's add-on to keep only your most recent web scraping results.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 12 months ago

Table of contents

Browse AI Google Sheets integration only appends data to your spreadsheet by
default. This means that over time if you're extracting data regularly to
Google Sheets, your spreadsheet can quickly become crowded with historical
data.

The Browse AI for Sheets add-on includes an auto-delete feature that helps
keep your spreadsheet clean by removing older rows automatically.

# Prerequisites

  * [Browse AI for Sheets](https://intercom.help/browse-ai-089fe61c5dc3/en/articles/10536316-how-can-i-use-the-google-sheets-add-on) add-on installed

  * A Google Sheet that's already [connected to an approved robot](10535898-how-to-sync-scraped-data-with-google-sheets.html)

#  Setting up auto-delete

  1. Open your Google Sheet and click **Extensions** in the menu bar

  2. Select **Browse AI for Sheets > Experimental features**

  3. In the sidebar, enable auto-delete by checking **Auto-delete old rows when I open the spreadsheet**

  4. Configure your settings:

     * Delete rows older than (in days): choose how many days of data to keep

     * Date column name: enter the header name of your date column (the add-on will use this date column to find out how old each row is)

     * Sheet name: should be already set to the name of the current sheet in which you want to delete old rows automatically.

IMPORTANT: Make sure that your sheet has a column for the date. Otherwise, the
auto-delete feature will not work correctly.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374637435/2ad7e64c1da5b5a13ad6fe7590cc/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagec5b7.png?expires=1771547400&signature=6d62e291b46aa2201d949f909ccd0a2d73fa644f924542736e26d227df1bfda8&req=dSMgEs99moVcXPMW1HO4zbQdVMZAB9Sdqor54v3lZNlyhjn4Cu0J%2B7DNiU2S%0AForgNVgxwGZyvAIbdH4%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374637435/2ad7e64c1da5b5a13ad6fe7590cc/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagec5b7.png?expires=1771547400&signature=6d62e291b46aa2201d949f909ccd0a2d73fa644f924542736e26d227df1bfda8&req=dSMgEs99moVcXPMW1HO4zbQdVMZAB9Sdqor54v3lZNlyhjn4Cu0J%2B7DNiU2S%0AForgNVgxwGZyvAIbdH4%3D%0A)

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374637643/0000068b94ff1d24f437a7684686/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image5990.png?expires=1771547400&signature=5d681912e55c55aa35251d4920ee6fac56a8077944286c73f3fe4a2ea283a6cf&req=dSMgEs99modbWvMW1HO4zWxPp8MxfzTWyAtZV3I05YfxoS0ouUAxVXGoufpv%0AD8DVzVpu9ncG8g1FkBk%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374637643/0000068b94ff1d24f437a7684686/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image5990.png?expires=1771547400&signature=5d681912e55c55aa35251d4920ee6fac56a8077944286c73f3fe4a2ea283a6cf&req=dSMgEs99modbWvMW1HO4zWxPp8MxfzTWyAtZV3I05YfxoS0ouUAxVXGoufpv%0AD8DVzVpu9ncG8g1FkBk%3D%0A)

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374637813/20b3ab698b9f6c05270e5d0c5b60/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagebb8f.png?expires=1771547400&signature=f7a0681f1680624f5d6d326e2c43b4196f17bb0735a95241a5ca947ab0c5a550&req=dSMgEs99moleWvMW1HO4zdj61Km9OizjM4a7vc9fDl%2FWJwijAzkQ8ane0NyD%0AoKeneljF7BYZZBkhTcQ%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374637813/20b3ab698b9f6c05270e5d0c5b60/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagebb8f.png?expires=1771547400&signature=f7a0681f1680624f5d6d326e2c43b4196f17bb0735a95241a5ca947ab0c5a550&req=dSMgEs99moleWvMW1HO4zdj61Km9OizjM4a7vc9fDl%2FWJwijAzkQ8ane0NyD%0AoKeneljF7BYZZBkhTcQ%3D%0A)

# Important considerations

  * Every time you open this sheet, the Browse AI for Sheets add-on will remove captured data older than 10 days automatically

  * All column data in deleted rows will be removed, including any manual entries

  * Your spreadsheet must have a date column for this feature to work

  * Deleted data cannot be recovered, so consider backing up important historical data

## Managing auto-delete settings

You can modify or disable auto-delete at any time:

  1. Go to Extensions > Browse AI for Sheets > Experimental features

  2. Uncheck the auto-delete option to disable, or adjust settings as needed

## Troubleshooting

If rows aren't being deleted:

  * Verify your date column header matches exactly

  * Check that your sheet name is correct

  * Ensure your date column contains valid dates

  * Confirm you have the latest version of the add-on

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374638390/daf4fb12ed88b13e4a97a7f14553/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image800e.png?expires=1771547400&signature=245bc8adc2d43048b6ff1bd081e4ff61853967f56bc646010682a37ef0c532c8&req=dSMgEs99lYJWWfMW1HO4za%2Fb2uSoWSwZtwxWr3X2a6mEbg6fALtvLmQ%2FYZrQ%0AGQ2AsFBmj%2Fjje4VXNdU%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374638390/daf4fb12ed88b13e4a97a7f14553/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image800e.png?expires=1771547400&signature=245bc8adc2d43048b6ff1bd081e4ff61853967f56bc646010682a37ef0c532c8&req=dSMgEs99lYJWWfMW1HO4za%2Fb2uSoWSwZtwxWr3X2a6mEbg6fALtvLmQ%2FYZrQ%0AGQ2AsFBmj%2Fjje4VXNdU%3D%0A)

[Airtable](../collections/11657242-airtable.html)

How to enable Airtable integration

# How to enable Airtable integration

Connect your Browse AI robot to Airtable in minutes with our step-by-step
guide to enable automatic syncing of extracted web data.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 12 months ago

Table of contents

# Prerequisites

Before you begin, make sure you have:

  1. A trained Browse AI robot that's ready to extract data

  2. An Airtable account with access to create and modify bases

  3. Completed at least one successful test run of your robot

# Setting up the integration

## Open the integrations tab

Navigate to your dashboard, select your robot, and click the "Integrate" tab
at the top of the screen.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374644326/397bfb8892b734cf6d2231a7e67b/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image3e8c.png?expires=1771547400&signature=fe069e910757e6d68b0d1f7b3b16e66779211e697502abddfeed3b2c5f6b25f6&req=dSMgEs96mYJdX%2FMW1HO4zT3Y%2BsHqnO0eIEEWiTbwqtJ8I7%2BGckrH%2FZrJuT1V%0AgJptmkCGNM7eSmhcyVc%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374644326/397bfb8892b734cf6d2231a7e67b/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image3e8c.png?expires=1771547400&signature=fe069e910757e6d68b0d1f7b3b16e66779211e697502abddfeed3b2c5f6b25f6&req=dSMgEs96mYJdX%2FMW1HO4zT3Y%2BsHqnO0eIEEWiTbwqtJ8I7%2BGckrH%2FZrJuT1V%0AgJptmkCGNM7eSmhcyVc%3D%0A)

##  
Enable the Airtable integration

Connect and enable syncing to to Airtable by clicking the Airtable tile, then
select "Enable Syncing to Airtable."

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374644545/36ef36708f486568891c1008a371/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image8551.png?expires=1771547400&signature=de80ce0a5707145506f93211fba8ea68c82b404936b508c2cf004aa56aafd024&req=dSMgEs96mYRbXPMW1HO4zeZvc3goodi11pC0VL%2Bdgm2BHwxJa7Edp2xsoVzF%0AglXq39QRvb5CdFmrYlQ%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374644545/36ef36708f486568891c1008a371/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image8551.png?expires=1771547400&signature=de80ce0a5707145506f93211fba8ea68c82b404936b508c2cf004aa56aafd024&req=dSMgEs96mYRbXPMW1HO4zeZvc3goodi11pC0VL%2Bdgm2BHwxJa7Edp2xsoVzF%0AglXq39QRvb5CdFmrYlQ%3D%0A)

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374644756/a1db5991df2dea3e82d0b7ae56a4/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagef240.png?expires=1771547400&signature=d4b163a7cfa9019da63be4ecd339e62530dd14f9bc8aad1e651d960bdbf669f2&req=dSMgEs96mYZaX%2FMW1HO4zcTZfMaHHFxCPdUfONW5zlSM21nohQ%2FBbnmv%2FqWK%0AMOqsGSqIvRA1XDKBABQ%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374644756/a1db5991df2dea3e82d0b7ae56a4/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagef240.png?expires=1771547400&signature=d4b163a7cfa9019da63be4ecd339e62530dd14f9bc8aad1e651d960bdbf669f2&req=dSMgEs96mYZaX%2FMW1HO4zcTZfMaHHFxCPdUfONW5zlSM21nohQ%2FBbnmv%2FqWK%0AMOqsGSqIvRA1XDKBABQ%3D%0A)

## Authorize your account

Choose "Authorize New Account" under the "Choose Airtable Account" section and
follow the prompts to connect your Airtable account.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374645020/d1f2972e9b0eb0430eebd00b248f/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image13cd.png?expires=1771547400&signature=72254d83e0606485ff21d075ea9dad5e8acafff29b153fa13c25375cb74d9120&req=dSMgEs96mIFdWfMW1HO4zTmHizveKEB8TCYg1vqewI2OHaV8kxp9yikPObhA%0AURxwMaASh4mz3bdY5TY%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374645020/d1f2972e9b0eb0430eebd00b248f/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image13cd.png?expires=1771547400&signature=72254d83e0606485ff21d075ea9dad5e8acafff29b153fa13c25375cb74d9120&req=dSMgEs96mIFdWfMW1HO4zTmHizveKEB8TCYg1vqewI2OHaV8kxp9yikPObhA%0AURxwMaASh4mz3bdY5TY%3D%0A)

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374645211/d7a654fc258e3b82debd17894e5b/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imaged793.png?expires=1771547400&signature=3b6cfe92ee7f21f8fac0ca9f337dfafec394db1a9019125b04cd17c60b16e2b5&req=dSMgEs96mINeWPMW1HO4zQZnzznIfLFoiQcTxNl7o%2FbFM6FN%2BHk4gQooKF9V%0Ai9cZ6AF6qkdG5o4mvyg%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374645211/d7a654fc258e3b82debd17894e5b/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imaged793.png?expires=1771547400&signature=3b6cfe92ee7f21f8fac0ca9f337dfafec394db1a9019125b04cd17c60b16e2b5&req=dSMgEs96mINeWPMW1HO4zQZnzznIfLFoiQcTxNl7o%2FbFM6FN%2BHk4gQooKF9V%0Ai9cZ6AF6qkdG5o4mvyg%3D%0A)

If you've connected Airtable before, you can select an existing account.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374640547/2c5693993b21b4d71697a8b73f36/file-
Ztqc2ahEZ3.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-
Ztqc2ahEZ38d17.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=54167e4864bad55610048a41e68991b20ea69765dd3c8e955fdb51a7b41f065f&req=dSMgEs96nYRbXvMW1HO4zSmyUCSSvnT1jgP8qVO5HnDTAqr65eSKA3q%2BklCe%0AqdwuROz9ucDC4NEpNDw%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374640547/2c5693993b21b4d71697a8b73f36/file-
Ztqc2ahEZ3.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-
Ztqc2ahEZ38d17.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=54167e4864bad55610048a41e68991b20ea69765dd3c8e955fdb51a7b41f065f&req=dSMgEs96nYRbXvMW1HO4zSmyUCSSvnT1jgP8qVO5HnDTAqr65eSKA3q%2BklCe%0AqdwuROz9ucDC4NEpNDw%3D%0A)

## Configure your sync

  1. Select your target Airtable base

  2. Map your robot's data fields to Airtable columns

  3. Review and activate the integration

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374640564/3ae47db8841a8da790ef51afab0c/file-y5222qs4jD.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-y5222qs4jD8cf6.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=a620204127aec40e010be0bda83d9e15e5c56b5c58deeac0ea7bee089e279381&req=dSMgEs96nYRZXfMW1HO4zRfpHjlO26W%2BU6ok7MOIxuiFd5nEG0H6kMm1yFQn%0ABmEUDn7eRcdSdwokQOw%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374640564/3ae47db8841a8da790ef51afab0c/file-y5222qs4jD.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-y5222qs4jD8cf6.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=a620204127aec40e010be0bda83d9e15e5c56b5c58deeac0ea7bee089e279381&req=dSMgEs96nYRZXfMW1HO4zRfpHjlO26W%2BU6ok7MOIxuiFd5nEG0H6kMm1yFQn%0ABmEUDn7eRcdSdwokQOw%3D%0A)

## Test the connection and run your task

Navigate to the "Run Task" tab and initiate your designated task.

During this step, ensure that the extracted texts are correctly displayed in
your Airtable, creating a new tab for your data.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1389568524/5dff461e76799180fdfa75590506/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagedcc7.png?expires=1771547400&signature=2d236a06fd7491081864138897f8f56e4977245992e2c3b9d4be9f2e8f19f54c&req=dSMvH8x4lYRdXfMW1HO4zUVYvkDE%2Fnj0iNiTY83etcQIIoUx%2FGdffPv8PTqJ%0AYiGJ5L048Yc6WUrqn14%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1389568524/5dff461e76799180fdfa75590506/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagedcc7.png?expires=1771547400&signature=2d236a06fd7491081864138897f8f56e4977245992e2c3b9d4be9f2e8f19f54c&req=dSMvH8x4lYRdXfMW1HO4zUVYvkDE%2Fnj0iNiTY83etcQIIoUx%2FGdffPv8PTqJ%0AYiGJ5L048Yc6WUrqn14%3D%0A)

## Set up an automated continuous sync

With the setup complete, when you run your task, or it executes a monitoring
check, the newly captured data will be automatically update to your Airtable.

This automated process ensures that your data remains up-to-date and
consistently organized.

# Important technical details

Your Airtable integration needs specific settings to work properly:

  * Browse AI syncs data to multiline text fields, which have a 100,000-character limit. Data exceeding this limit won't sync successfully.

  * Keep all synced fields as multiline text type in Airtable. Changing field types (like switching from text to currency) will break the sync.

# Troubleshooting

If your data isn't syncing:

  * Verify that your robot is extracting data successfully

  * Check that your Airtable field types haven't been modified

  * Ensure your data doesn't exceed the character limit

  * Confirm you have proper permissions in your Airtable base
