How to integrate Browse AI with Pabbly Connect

# How to integrate Browse AI with Pabbly Connect

Connect Browse AI with Pabbly Connect to automate your data workflows - set up
triggers, actions, and data transformations in just a few steps.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 12 months ago

Table of contents

This guide shows you how to connect Browse AI with Pabbly Connect to automate
your web data extraction workflows. You'll learn how to set up triggers,
configure actions, and transform your data for use in other applications.

# Prerequisites

  * Active Browse AI account with at least one approved robot

  * Pabbly Connect account

# Setting up the connection

## Create a new workflow

  1. Sign in to your [Pabbly Connect dashboard](https://connect.pabbly.com/dashboard)

  2. Click **Create workflow**

  3. Name your workflow something descriptive (e.g., "Browse AI Product Monitoring") 

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374660493/645e811ef1ab9cadbae542b85857/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagec49d.png?expires=1771547400&signature=4aebf437d4c21e99d7700220d85ae2cc969e74fbd688a3484733ca66cc8f09a3&req=dSMgEs94nYVWWvMW1HO4zf3wiEK44Z5BJerM%2BTR1l9ilUhMEX5MRhhh1%2FtUd%0AgtnGf1cntwVcRlD1xjo%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374660493/645e811ef1ab9cadbae542b85857/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagec49d.png?expires=1771547400&signature=4aebf437d4c21e99d7700220d85ae2cc969e74fbd688a3484733ca66cc8f09a3&req=dSMgEs94nYVWWvMW1HO4zf3wiEK44Z5BJerM%2BTR1l9ilUhMEX5MRhhh1%2FtUd%0AgtnGf1cntwVcRlD1xjo%3D%0A)

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374660757/84943b33a69fcf69e5cd27a31d9b/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image6790.png?expires=1771547400&signature=4f41d14cc7adca121c38cf788dc9f74e30e575204bcb27503c401a2256492f5b&req=dSMgEs94nYZaXvMW1HO4zTYf%2FMa3jx8kFm7AZAlGxvjihDac1DJ7EGgIFmHD%0AYpeb2o3C%2BxAH%2BrYsIzk%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374660757/84943b33a69fcf69e5cd27a31d9b/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image6790.png?expires=1771547400&signature=4f41d14cc7adca121c38cf788dc9f74e30e575204bcb27503c401a2256492f5b&req=dSMgEs94nYZaXvMW1HO4zTYf%2FMa3jx8kFm7AZAlGxvjihDac1DJ7EGgIFmHD%0AYpeb2o3C%2BxAH%2BrYsIzk%3D%0A)

## Configure Browse AI trigger

  1. Select Browse AI as your trigger app

  2. Choose **Executed task**. 

Note: Each workflow starts with a trigger on which the workflow runs, and then
the data flows through different actions. Browse AI has an official trigger
called Executed Task and two official actions (Bulk Run Tasks, Execute a
Task).

Any other triggers and/or actions on the list are created by the Pabbly team
and are being deprecated.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374659351/6c1b57048172698b5194fcca13fe/file-
liOFlahOag.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-
liOFlahOag295f.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=7cc7d39514c2ac67a6d8c82ce0392dc7669bcb8ac6b0dad727eb3f07ef2238e3&req=dSMgEs97lIJaWPMW1HO4zVuFZUAU4K%2BStIjKfMGXATeRQz8EHRUYINGx1SJo%0ALQCwPUv6Zr%2F7p4tCDak%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374659351/6c1b57048172698b5194fcca13fe/file-
liOFlahOag.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-
liOFlahOag295f.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=7cc7d39514c2ac67a6d8c82ce0392dc7669bcb8ac6b0dad727eb3f07ef2238e3&req=dSMgEs97lIJaWPMW1HO4zVuFZUAU4K%2BStIjKfMGXATeRQz8EHRUYINGx1SJo%0ALQCwPUv6Zr%2F7p4tCDak%3D%0A)

## Authorize the connection

  1. Click **Add new connection**

  2. Log in to your Browse AI account to authorize the connection (you should see a success message)

  3. You'll see your list of robots in a dropdown. Choose the one you want to retrieve data from. 

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374659354/62dd322a0df5b42f120a5346c989/file-
erhv5LvI52.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-
erhv5LvI52a37e.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=91420651fe26089935c091f188110183603eb25079d149f54491ba28a375ac54&req=dSMgEs97lIJaXfMW1HO4zTvyEWMOm7dHDWhdK740%2Fgbdmqy9IZj105xGl%2Fnf%0A6XPGt2XloxXIcVXSHm4%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374659354/62dd322a0df5b42f120a5346c989/file-
erhv5LvI52.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-
erhv5LvI52a37e.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=91420651fe26089935c091f188110183603eb25079d149f54491ba28a375ac54&req=dSMgEs97lIJaXfMW1HO4zTvyEWMOm7dHDWhdK740%2Fgbdmqy9IZj105xGl%2Fnf%0A6XPGt2XloxXIcVXSHm4%3D%0A)

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374661270/d853e18f5153a4b3e1fb6f3eed39/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image50b9.png?expires=1771547400&signature=552715e0358a5acd3d32bd2f8b7035d574f43a02740fb0ab9f5122bc3c07f0c2&req=dSMgEs94nINYWfMW1HO4zU%2F55x9Nsnua99QOO4GPIn34itdCrygbf%2FZLxNZH%0AL9PZzoSx7bEFzkqZq78%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374661270/d853e18f5153a4b3e1fb6f3eed39/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image50b9.png?expires=1771547400&signature=552715e0358a5acd3d32bd2f8b7035d574f43a02740fb0ab9f5122bc3c07f0c2&req=dSMgEs94nINYWfMW1HO4zU%2F55x9Nsnua99QOO4GPIn34itdCrygbf%2FZLxNZH%0AL9PZzoSx7bEFzkqZq78%3D%0A)

# Configuring workflow settings

## Choose your Event Type

The Event Type you choose will be what triggers the workflow to execute.
Select one of these Event Type options:

  * Change detected in captured data: triggers when captured data changes 

  * Task execution is finished: triggers on any task completion (including if the task was successful, or with an error)

  * Task execution finished successfully: triggers only on successful tasks

  * Task execution failed: triggers only on failed tasks 

Once you've selected your Event Type, make sure to turn off **Simple
Response**. This is highly recommended to optimize your experience using the
integration.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374661500/d9bcc437f51f75a8990e715dfdfd/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imageeeec.png?expires=1771547400&signature=ee9305289b0bcc2b355e6b9696a2b3e6541550afce16a2888834898ea8c80572&req=dSMgEs94nIRfWfMW1HO4zXn5vZe5HDj2u%2FzxZs%2FQG9qQ4XZLelLkta3H7lQh%0A9fD%2BTRue5CxMbX5%2FWXY%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374661500/d9bcc437f51f75a8990e715dfdfd/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imageeeec.png?expires=1771547400&signature=ee9305289b0bcc2b355e6b9696a2b3e6541550afce16a2888834898ea8c80572&req=dSMgEs94nIRfWfMW1HO4zXn5vZe5HDj2u%2FzxZs%2FQG9qQ4XZLelLkta3H7lQh%0A9fD%2BTRue5CxMbX5%2FWXY%3D%0A)

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374661705/414e46d59edbb5d105206449720a/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image62c3.png?expires=1771547400&signature=2579b3c2f64a6096a5cf881c145bb0df0fce1522b0ea932ea493771a8230f443&req=dSMgEs94nIZfXPMW1HO4zQvMVnMbRwcQ2zDRT3DE8eNKe%2BtmXxJjCRr%2F2tNe%0AqUeaOebtt4kS72PXYdo%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374661705/414e46d59edbb5d105206449720a/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image62c3.png?expires=1771547400&signature=2579b3c2f64a6096a5cf881c145bb0df0fce1522b0ea932ea493771a8230f443&req=dSMgEs94nIZfXPMW1HO4zQvMVnMbRwcQ2zDRT3DE8eNKe%2BtmXxJjCRr%2F2tNe%0AqUeaOebtt4kS72PXYdo%3D%0A)

## Verify the integration

Verify the webhook connection in your Browse AI robot's **Integration** tab.

Select **Webhooks** , and you should see a webhook connected to your Pabbly
workflow. From now on, every time you run a task, you'll see your data in the
Pabbly workflow.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374661882/08007dd90d71b77aebda6024dcec/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagef034.png?expires=1771547400&signature=1c2e1498396b6f629a2a8cdb45d52411d5bc26b5c6ff8fdb8f13a15738f90b10&req=dSMgEs94nIlXW%2FMW1HO4zYk664eA3dwCth8iv2x5dspDHYhoSpjGnAbv%2BJ%2FA%0AhZJ1JsXA4DMmyuhuY2I%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374661882/08007dd90d71b77aebda6024dcec/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagef034.png?expires=1771547400&signature=1c2e1498396b6f629a2a8cdb45d52411d5bc26b5c6ff8fdb8f13a15738f90b10&req=dSMgEs94nIlXW%2FMW1HO4zYk664eA3dwCth8iv2x5dspDHYhoSpjGnAbv%2BJ%2FA%0AhZJ1JsXA4DMmyuhuY2I%3D%0A)

## Execute a test run

Go the **Run Task** tab for your robot and click on the **Run a Task** button
to send a response to Pabbly Connect. Go back to Pabbly Connect to and you
will see that a response has been received.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374659346/1ec685f155e4e7ffa72e25610eb4/file-7feFFKw4iX.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-7feFFKw4iX5d3e.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=dc1d5fe9f35ce5a0ed30eed9793e61208e2bdc41c49b0ec233b9d1d607b79e77&req=dSMgEs97lIJbX%2FMW1HO4zSHj%2FrT5kiiU%2B%2BDR1KCdciWUSpw0t4QSAig4lBQx%0AMvkVboX0MnNDlMWHo%2Fc%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374659346/1ec685f155e4e7ffa72e25610eb4/file-7feFFKw4iX.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-7feFFKw4iX5d3e.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=dc1d5fe9f35ce5a0ed30eed9793e61208e2bdc41c49b0ec233b9d1d607b79e77&req=dSMgEs97lIJbX%2FMW1HO4zSHj%2FrT5kiiU%2B%2BDR1KCdciWUSpw0t4QSAig4lBQx%0AMvkVboX0MnNDlMWHo%2Fc%3D%0A)

# Transforming scraped data with Pabbly Connect

The advanced data will give you packaged data, on which you can apply
iterators, extractors and more.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374662070/501e733afe864a9fa6547b10e643/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imaged37e.png?expires=1771547400&signature=c526164a40f3b403043795618c4bd73628f3e66d689c6360d2a481471e687ccf&req=dSMgEs94n4FYWfMW1HO4zVmnEcp7hi%2BHtk%2FE%2BHGcN2KUWbp30IB9X5bZSx5l%0AJdFDCHPyHXzSd%2Br4abU%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374662070/501e733afe864a9fa6547b10e643/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imaged37e.png?expires=1771547400&signature=c526164a40f3b403043795618c4bd73628f3e66d689c6360d2a481471e687ccf&req=dSMgEs94n4FYWfMW1HO4zVmnEcp7hi%2BHtk%2FE%2BHGcN2KUWbp30IB9X5bZSx5l%0AJdFDCHPyHXzSd%2Br4abU%3D%0A)

For transforming the JSON data into data that can be used in other apps:

  1. Select the **Data Transformer by Pabbly** Action. 

  2. Set the Action Event to **Line Itemizer**

  3. You have to choose the Line items array which was received from the Browse AI. You can search for it by starting to type **capture** in the search field. 

  4. In this case, **Task CapturedList PH Number 1** is the array we should select

  5. Once you click on **Save & Send Test Request**, you will see the values and labels organized as lie items.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374662318/345b4933bbaefe99b998316e1f9d/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imaged381.png?expires=1771547400&signature=5ac8e41d008e4113bb92f9014fcf7228b643f7b7f2434b9785e302ffd9642d0b&req=dSMgEs94n4JeUfMW1HO4zbXQwVQoJLqduNj67ANwZJaA6owEF8dhAKXf8Etq%0A%2FYTQBeh7%2F761TX1MtTM%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374662318/345b4933bbaefe99b998316e1f9d/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imaged381.png?expires=1771547400&signature=5ac8e41d008e4113bb92f9014fcf7228b643f7b7f2434b9785e302ffd9642d0b&req=dSMgEs94n4JeUfMW1HO4zbXQwVQoJLqduNj67ANwZJaA6owEF8dhAKXf8Etq%0A%2FYTQBeh7%2F761TX1MtTM%3D%0A)

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374662485/f50dfdf6e82704caecab41db936c/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagee072.png?expires=1771547400&signature=0a5129458db787d49f5eec16f0880dac695d72410d10c41aca6370c4cb43ab93&req=dSMgEs94n4VXXPMW1HO4zV%2FM8wFpzN0vN6CT7qwqp4a6uH5dYz0DvonWSTLQ%0AkkYloJ41CYU%2BK0ShH7Q%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374662485/f50dfdf6e82704caecab41db936c/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagee072.png?expires=1771547400&signature=0a5129458db787d49f5eec16f0880dac695d72410d10c41aca6370c4cb43ab93&req=dSMgEs94n4VXXPMW1HO4zV%2FM8wFpzN0vN6CT7qwqp4a6uH5dYz0DvonWSTLQ%0AkkYloJ41CYU%2BK0ShH7Q%3D%0A)

With that line item format you could send data to Google Sheets using the
**Add Multiple Rows** Action (for example).

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374662692/c599e174bab2d9247d81808e6fb9/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagefa43.png?expires=1771547400&signature=9354d28ec78608078e739e45a595ff8f0f8b44aaf5874e941d4fcd58e7af9189&req=dSMgEs94n4dWW%2FMW1HO4zbCsAXiBmQ6mA4VFDuLlMLA%2BLfij0mtIN6l3IrH%2F%0A25zEEWxCx5WAuoj2iiI%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1374662692/c599e174bab2d9247d81808e6fb9/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagefa43.png?expires=1771547400&signature=9354d28ec78608078e739e45a595ff8f0f8b44aaf5874e941d4fcd58e7af9189&req=dSMgEs94n4dWW%2FMW1HO4zbCsAXiBmQ6mA4VFDuLlMLA%2BLfij0mtIN6l3IrH%2F%0A25zEEWxCx5WAuoj2iiI%3D%0A)

**NOTE:** For other uses cases and Pabbly Connect apps you may have to use
their **Iterator** instead, to loop through each of the items that the Browse
AI trigger delivers. Learn more about that [in their help
docs.](https://www.pabbly.com/pabbly-connect-documentation-complete-
integration-
guide/#:~:text=App%E2%80%9D%20drop%2Ddown.-,6.%20Iterator,-This%20is%20used)

How to extract data across multiple pages (deep scraping)?

# How to extract data across multiple pages (deep scraping)?

Use workflows to connect multiple robots to scrape content across multiple
pages: one to gather lists of items, another to get details from subpage(s).

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over a month ago

Table of contents

When building a web scraper or monitors, you'll often encounter this scenario:

  * A list page which shows basic information (like product names and prices), 

  * A detail page that lives on separate pages that you access by clicking each item.

This is called deep scraping. Deep scraping is a way to extract data from
multiple linked pages on a website. Simply put, it's how you scrape an entire
website across multiple layers vs. a singular web page.

Depending on how the website you want to scrape or monitor is structured, you
can deep scrape by:

  * Category - category > sub category > page

  * Site search - search term > results > page

  * Navigation - navigate > scrape links > pages

  * Site map - scrape site map > pages

Popular use cases for deep scraping include:

  * E-commerce price and product monitoring to create a database of product and prices based on category or search terms. 

  * Scraping and monitoring directories for lead generation. 

  * Competitive monitoring to scrape entire websites.

# How do I deep scrape with Browse AI?

There are two ways you can set up deep scraping with Browse AI depending on
the data you want to scrape or monitor, and the structure of the website.

| What it does| When it's ideal  
---|---|---  
Bulk run| Upload a CSV of URLs or Input parameters to scrape up to 500,000
pages at once.|

  * Site search deep scraping
  * Single use static dataset

  
Workflow| Connect multiple robots to automatically automatically scrape all pages and sub pages. | 

  * Monitored or 'live' data
  * Category deep scraping

  
  
## Bulk run: How to deep scrape with bulk runs

Read our [Bulk Run Guide](10453933-bulk-run-how-to-use-bulk-runs-to-extract-
data-from-multiple-urls.html) here to learn how to set up a bulk run to scrape
and monitor up to 500,000 URLs or input parameters.

## Workflows: how to deep scrape using workflows

A basic workflow to deep scrape involves two main steps:

  * Robot A: a robot scrapes a list or category page to collect basic information and URLs.

  * Robot B: a robot that visits each URL to extract or scrape detailed information from individual pages.

Note that using workflows you can connect as many robots as you'd need
depending on the structure of the data you need to scrape.

## Step 1. Train Robot A to scrape a list or category page to get a list of
URLs

In this first step, you'll create a robot that scrapes or extracts a list of
URLs based on:

  1. Go to the category page URL. 

  2. Use Capture List to select repeating items (like products, job listings, or properties). Make sure to capture the URL field that links to the detail pages.

  3. Finish, approve and name this robot. 

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1394630107/818e40af7e4c888d749242e8d806/file-
HNTzk2sGWj.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-
HNTzk2sGWjd708.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=fd52d7242561b0770c379b4c07f1e1ed894e0fd0af8146b57ace390b5a8c6945&req=dSMuEs99nYBfXvMW1HO4zRX0K4wIrxjiRSG1Ix1oLBI%2FNDO3capLGs1nWTNb%0ArMdodTDsoCZ1kficVJ4%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1394630107/818e40af7e4c888d749242e8d806/file-
HNTzk2sGWj.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-
HNTzk2sGWjd708.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=fd52d7242561b0770c379b4c07f1e1ed894e0fd0af8146b57ace390b5a8c6945&req=dSMuEs99nYBfXvMW1HO4zRX0K4wIrxjiRSG1Ix1oLBI%2FNDO3capLGs1nWTNb%0ArMdodTDsoCZ1kficVJ4%3D%0A)

## Step 2: Train Robot B to extract, monitor or scrape details

  1. Create a second robot using one of the URLs for the details page.

  2. Train the robot to [scrape, structure and monitor](10496382-how-to-train-a-robot-to-scrape-or-monitor-data.html) the data you'd like.

  3. Finish, approve and name this robot.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1379514603/ce516ca30f3c81688dc2464e22ed/file-
cLSbUPka3N.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-
cLSbUPka3N26f3.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=b3b70d2e10bbe392139f14492bb9f306904319e2341075b8a28756fb8b29e39a&req=dSMgH8x%2FmYdfWvMW1HO4zUkwjnwVLJA6x1wOrNS7FwsEk3IX4nsVnJhupWNy%0AH7FLXugOhvhDIpq%2FOU8%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1379514603/ce516ca30f3c81688dc2464e22ed/file-
cLSbUPka3N.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-
cLSbUPka3N26f3.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=b3b70d2e10bbe392139f14492bb9f306904319e2341075b8a28756fb8b29e39a&req=dSMgH8x%2FmYdfWvMW1HO4zUkwjnwVLJA6x1wOrNS7FwsEk3IX4nsVnJhupWNy%0AH7FLXugOhvhDIpq%2FOU8%3D%0A)

## Step 3: Connect these two robots together using workflows

Automatically feed the URLs from Robot A into Robot B by creating a workflow.

  1. Go to Workflows.

  2. Set up a workflow that feeds data from Robot A to Robot B.

  3. Schedule the workflow to run automatically at your preferred frequency.

[Read our workflows guide here.](10459476-workflows-how-can-i-create-a-
workflow-connecting-two-robots.html)

# Common use cases for deep scraping

## E-commerce product monitoring

  * Robot A collects product information from category pages.

  * Robot B visits each product page to gather specifications, reviews, and availability.

## Real estate data

  * Robot A scans property listing pages for basic details.

  * Robot B visits individual property pages to collect specifications and amenities.

## Lead generation of business listings

  * Robot A processes directory pages to gather business listings.

  * Robot B visits each business profile to extract contact details and services.

# Best practices

  * Start by mapping out what the structure of the website and webpages looks like first. How many workflows/layers do you need?

  * Make sure to set up a monitor on one or both of your robots to keep this data up to date.

How to integrate your robot with Make.com

# How to integrate your robot with Make.com

Send and create automations from your Browse AI robot's extracted data using
Make.com's visual automation platform.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 12 months ago

Table of contents

In this article, we go through the steps to configure the Make.com
integration.

# Prerequisites

  * An approved robot

  * A Make.com account (any plan that supports webhooks)

  * Your Browse API key

# What is Make.com?

Make.com is a visual automation platform that connects different apps and
services to create automated workflows. It's helpful when you want to:

  * Connect Browse AI with tools that don't have direct integrations

  * Create custom workflows beyond our standard integrations

  * Build automated processes without coding

Think of Make.com as a visual way to connect different tools together - when
something happens in one tool (like data being extracted by your Browse AI
robot), it can trigger actions in other tools (like updating a spreadsheet or
sending a notification).

# Setting up the Make.com integration

## Initiate the integration

  1. Go to your robot and click on the **Integrate** tab. 

  2. Select **Make.com**

  3. Click **Integrate Browse AI with Make.com**

  4. Log into your Make.com account

  5. Click **Create a new scenario**

  6. Click the plus button to add your first module

  7. Search for and select **Browse AI**

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1384608447/b1a61fe1c60c97cb9b2a1bd59510/file-0eef8uHYRg.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-0eef8uHYRg7ac6.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=86c09134379c16350b9d4e486ee6427e210b4e753dd119bbeb8c0f89fd3b87ee&req=dSMvEs9%2BlYVbXvMW1HO4zbrYQq3tOVngOO4k5EZheu1MMeYNWpg2qjws2ZLP%0AKcI%2FxacQrLh48dFZOH4%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1384608447/b1a61fe1c60c97cb9b2a1bd59510/file-0eef8uHYRg.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-0eef8uHYRg7ac6.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=86c09134379c16350b9d4e486ee6427e210b4e753dd119bbeb8c0f89fd3b87ee&req=dSMvEs9%2BlYVbXvMW1HO4zbrYQq3tOVngOO4k5EZheu1MMeYNWpg2qjws2ZLP%0AKcI%2FxacQrLh48dFZOH4%3D%0A)

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1384608452/a8fff640b4b048bfd9dae4595d22/file-
Gy7BxYrTEG.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27file-

## Configure the Browse AI trigger

  1. Select your trigger type

  2. Name your Webhook (we recommend naming it based on what it does)

  3. If needed create a new connection by clicking **Create a connection** and**** authenticate with your Browse AI account

  4. Select your robot from the dropdown 

  5. Choose your trigger event 

     * Task finished (runs after any task completion)

     * Task finished successfully (runs only after successful tasks)

     * Task failed (runs when tasks fail)

     * Change detected in captured data (runs when monitored data changes 

  6. Select **Save**

  7. Go back to your robot dashboard and select **Integrate**

  8. If successful, you'll not see that under Webhooks it will say **configured**

## Set up your workflow

After setting up the webhook, you can choose what happens with the data after
being received in Make.com.

  * Add an Iterator module if processing multiple items

  * Configure your desired output actions

  * Set up any needed data transformations

  * Add error handling if needed

### Tutorial: Creating a Google Sheet database from scraped data

Let's say that you have a list of items you've scraped and want to structure
the data in a specific way into a Google Sheet.

#### How to set it up

  1. Use **Flow Control** to **select Iterator.**

  2. Leave the **Array** empty 

  3. Find and select **Google Sheets**

  4. Select **Add a Row**

Iterator is a special type of module that converts an array into a series of
bundles. Each array item will output as a separate bundle. You can learn more
[here](https://www.make.com/en/help/modules/iterator#:~:text=Iterator%20is%20a%20special%20type,output%20as%20a%20separate%20bundle.).

#### How to run it

  1. Go to the Run Task tab of your robot and click on **Run a Task**. 

  2. When that has finished, in your Make.com scenario click **Run once** to get sample data.

  3. After receiving the data, click on the Iterator. Click on **task** , then scroll down until you see **capturedLists** , which in our case, it is Search Results[ ].

  4. The data should now be in Google Sheets. After selecting the Spreadsheet name and Sheet name, you can choose the Values that were received from the Iterator, as shown in the below.

  5. For automating tasks, make sure to turn on the scheduling.

How to export data from Tables

# How to export data from Tables

Export your robot's data as a CSV or JSON for analysis, reporting, or
integration with other tools. Tables support multiple export formats with full
control over what data is included.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 2 months ago

Table of contents

# What data do you want to export?

To export data from Tables, you'll first want to navigate to and filter (as
needed) to configure the data.

### **Export all data**

  * Select the **Main tab**

    * Export as a CSV to get a ZIP file with all your data (includes Main.csv plus one CSV for each list tab)

    * Export as a JSON to get a single file of all of your data

  * Perfect for complete backups or comprehensive analysis

### **Export specific data**

  * Select any **list tab** to export the data as a single file

  * Ideal for focused analysis or sharing specific datasets

## Export filtered data

Any filters or toggles you apply will also be applied to the export file. This
includes:

  * [Custom filters](10594160-how-to-filter-your-data.html)

  * [Show/hide columns](10595195-how-to-show-hide-and-resize-table-columns.html)

  * [Latest data run vs. all historical data](13178214-working-with-historical-data.html)

💡 What you see in your Table is exactly what exports - so apply the filtures,
and views you need first to export the data you want. You can apply multiple
filters and views to configure the dataset you'd like to export.

# How to export data

Once you've determined and changed your view to show the data you'd like to
export you can export that data as a CSV, or JSON file.

  1. Click the **Export** button (top-right corner)

  2. Review the export summary.

  3. Choose your format (CSV or JSON) and click **Download**.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1890467651/fb1685818caca73e9f074bd08838/How%20to%20Export%20Data%20%25281%2529.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27How%2520to%2520Export%2520Data%2520%25281%25298af5.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=d13ad7bf13684e4904db5548f803e7168393aa3485d6d27c337e51a89684b666&req=dSguFs14modaWPMW1HO4zYTfTHVlJIURVn5J30syck1d%2BsVvzDk6jHIl3PUZ%0Akxl7RorZBO4ORa8waFg%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1890467651/fb1685818caca73e9f074bd08838/How%20to%20Export%20Data%20%25281%2529.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27How%2520to%2520Export%2520Data%2520%25281%25298af5.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=d13ad7bf13684e4904db5548f803e7168393aa3485d6d27c337e51a89684b666&req=dSguFs14modaWPMW1HO4zYTfTHVlJIURVn5J30syck1d%2BsVvzDk6jHIl3PUZ%0Akxl7RorZBO4ORa8waFg%3D%0A)

After clicking **Export Data** , you have two ways to access your files:

## **Direct download from Table**

  * The file will begin downloading immediately.

  * A record of the export also appears in your Table for later access.

## **Email download link**

  * Browse AI sends an email with a download link.

  * This link remains active for 24 hours.

  * Useful for sharing the export with team members. 

💡 Hovering over the question mark icon will tell you what was exported in each
export.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1890476036/19f0b09b34f7fd5238234be4f317/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image8b8f.png?expires=1771547400&signature=aca6da2ed2dc54ef68224fe62e2f5c3474c17f02c4c6ab525e0c0c1d292a4d43&req=dSguFs15m4FcX%2FMW1HO4zezf%2BGZSW4GJhLrMxbWen1Mh8hHqH9Rst%2FruDBoY%0AfMAshYxGl662V5daU0g%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1890476036/19f0b09b34f7fd5238234be4f317/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image8b8f.png?expires=1771547400&signature=aca6da2ed2dc54ef68224fe62e2f5c3474c17f02c4c6ab525e0c0c1d292a4d43&req=dSguFs15m4FcX%2FMW1HO4zezf%2BGZSW4GJhLrMxbWen1Mh8hHqH9Rst%2FruDBoY%0AfMAshYxGl662V5daU0g%3D%0A)

# Export to AWS S3

For automated workflows and large-scale operations, export directly to your
AWS S3 bucket.

  1. Click **Export** in your Table.

  2. Choose either:

     * Export as JSON to S3.

     * Export as CSV to S3.

  3. Click **Set up** to configure your S3 connection.

  4. Follow the setup guide to connect your bucket.

  5. Data exports directly to your cloud storage. 

📖 To learn more about [how to integrate your robot to AWS
S3](10715592-integrating-browse-ai-with-amazon-s3.html).

# Exporting captured screenshots

If you trained your robot to capture screenshots, they won't appear in your
regular data exports (CSV/JSON). Screenshots are stored separately from your
structured data.

  1. In your Table's Main tab, find the row for the extraction you want to export.

  2. Click the **Status** to open History

  3. Scroll to **Captured Screenshot** and right-click to **Save image as**.

💡 Unlike data exports, screenshots must be saved individually - there's no
bulk export option.

# Troubleshooting export issues

## Check for browser pop-up blocks.

  * Look for a pop-up blocker notification in your browser address bar.

  * Click **Allow** or **Always allow pop-ups from Browse AI**.

  * Try the download again.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1890494202/036ddf1e45ddc1e3896ea00c60e9/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagec38a.png?expires=1771547400&signature=aa43249ec12e33f54a37d524e98e81f8e18bd26996e3c9ea55723e68f01dc894&req=dSguFs13mYNfW%2FMW1HO4zeCo9XYlFTriaLJaVSqKokinIQhaZzIYndn7Z1R8%0ALi67uD7ML6tz7sTk3%2B0%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1890494202/036ddf1e45ddc1e3896ea00c60e9/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagec38a.png?expires=1771547400&signature=aa43249ec12e33f54a37d524e98e81f8e18bd26996e3c9ea55723e68f01dc894&req=dSguFs13mYNfW%2FMW1HO4zeCo9XYlFTriaLJaVSqKokinIQhaZzIYndn7Z1R8%0ALi67uD7ML6tz7sTk3%2B0%3D%0A)

## Other common issues

  * Large files may take longer to generate (wait a few moments).

  * Check your downloads folder as the file might have downloaded without notification.

  * Try using the email link if direct download continues to fail.

  * Clear your browser cache if problems persist.

How to filter your data

# How to filter your data

Filters help you quickly find exactly what you need, whether that's tracking
down failed extractions, or segmenting data based on extracted data or input
parameters.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 2 months ago

Table of contents

You can filter your Table data in two ways:

  1. Quick filtering to drill down on a specific extraction.

  2. Adding a custom filter.

💡 Applied filters are automatically applied to extracted data.

# Drill down on a single extraction

When your robot has multiple extractions, you can quickly filter to see data
from just one specific run:

  1. In your Main tab, hover over a row in your Table.

  2. Look for the eye icon that appears in the list column.

  3. Click the eye icon to filter the list tab to show only data from that specific task.

💡 This creates a Task ID filter that you can clear later by clicking the X on
the filter badge.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1387995752/68f49eb268202435d8eea5dd1f44/filter-
by-task.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27filter-by-
taskcf4a.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=72adc2395eee4b1b8a225014dae90a75abc88870a92fe7280831526790401ea9&req=dSMvEcB3mIZaW%2FMW1HO4zX147%2Bv7pZJlAQYSOtQY1H03UIH0FWM1SwlgxwkH%0ArQBggUgGDukdxYm3gIg%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1387995752/68f49eb268202435d8eea5dd1f44/filter-
by-task.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27filter-by-
taskcf4a.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=72adc2395eee4b1b8a225014dae90a75abc88870a92fe7280831526790401ea9&req=dSMvEcB3mIZaW%2FMW1HO4zX147%2Bv7pZJlAQYSOtQY1H03UIH0FWM1SwlgxwkH%0ArQBggUgGDukdxYm3gIg%3D%0A)

# Adding a custom filter

Click **Add Filter** above your Table to access all filtering options.

✅ Filter options will depend on the data you've extracted.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1387984663/3281b714203db4ebd71bcbacb471/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image4029.png?expires=1771547400&signature=7129ec32cdf4dddf22ba3c2a27ce8f7182ada1f0d36a9f32d376f1fdadbc39f9&req=dSMvEcB2mYdZWvMW1HO4zb4N3yBie8MyCVHjU%2FWDxuF5IQTFA0RUkednI8zC%0A6YRb1A5xzCPjXxhf0f0%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1387984663/3281b714203db4ebd71bcbacb471/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image4029.png?expires=1771547400&signature=7129ec32cdf4dddf22ba3c2a27ce8f7182ada1f0d36a9f32d376f1fdadbc39f9&req=dSMvEcB2mYdZWvMW1HO4zb4N3yBie8MyCVHjU%2FWDxuF5IQTFA0RUkednI8zC%0A6YRb1A5xzCPjXxhf0f0%3D%0A)

## Filter by Origin URL

Filter by the specific [Origin URL](10453064-what-is-the-origin-url.html).
This is useful when:

  * Analyze performance on particular sites.

  * Compare data across different sources.

  * Troubleshoot issues with specific URLs.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1387963290/78bb69bca620cfe966807052056d/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagec9d3.png?expires=1771547400&signature=9ed4ee39716ff965ea83a9e58bf1ca7c9a74f5adf3637c3ddbb9064fcbff5fac&req=dSMvEcB4noNWWfMW1HO4zfJeEakFWr%2F2ty1oG8jsdtz7PZsDTet5Xj7K7J5B%0AtjFbeoO1SWMhKaQ48IE%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1387963290/78bb69bca620cfe966807052056d/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagec9d3.png?expires=1771547400&signature=9ed4ee39716ff965ea83a9e58bf1ca7c9a74f5adf3637c3ddbb9064fcbff5fac&req=dSMvEcB4noNWWfMW1HO4zfJeEakFWr%2F2ty1oG8jsdtz7PZsDTet5Xj7K7J5B%0AtjFbeoO1SWMhKaQ48IE%3D%0A)

## Filter by Extract Date

Find data from specific time periods:

  * View yesterday's extractions

  * Analyze weekly trends

  * Find historical data from specific dates

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1888458680/e230f95f70628cf69f9b1f7191a5/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image2c26.png?expires=1771547400&signature=b3a08c18584bf3f49c3d4f504f1314e1cba65acf83ff2d568bba179882beabe7&req=dSgvHs17lYdXWfMW1HO4zRgKJApXpOzefFVlhtvoYkPCemIOkPyh02sFtOzN%0AofYdaiobo6dPbSCgGxc%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1888458680/e230f95f70628cf69f9b1f7191a5/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image2c26.png?expires=1771547400&signature=b3a08c18584bf3f49c3d4f504f1314e1cba65acf83ff2d568bba179882beabe7&req=dSgvHs17lYdXWfMW1HO4zRgKJApXpOzefFVlhtvoYkPCemIOkPyh02sFtOzN%0AofYdaiobo6dPbSCgGxc%3D%0A)

💡 If you just want to see the latest data extracted, uncheck **Show Historical
Data** at the top right.

## Filter by Status

Quickly identify successes and failures:

  * Successful - data is ready to be exported.

  * Failed - troubleshoot issues and re-run extractions.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1888476205/586c3051c921db4c8341edf8b425/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagecdaf.png?expires=1771547400&signature=5f871ed5156b3d12aca4d16ba3cf8c64cdef1ce16d7e896de2126e82be3303a7&req=dSgvHs15m4NfXPMW1HO4zaEaWYSj4VBzWNKjl%2BrAT3lbcZ6wMcRWrlFiXUHT%0APF%2Fl4Q7Pz1QoVloGeag%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1888476205/586c3051c921db4c8341edf8b425/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagecdaf.png?expires=1771547400&signature=5f871ed5156b3d12aca4d16ba3cf8c64cdef1ce16d7e896de2126e82be3303a7&req=dSgvHs15m4NfXPMW1HO4zaEaWYSj4VBzWNKjl%2BrAT3lbcZ6wMcRWrlFiXUHT%0APF%2Fl4Q7Pz1QoVloGeag%3D%0A)

💡 Filter by "Failed" status and click to re-run or use [bulk
actions](10453933-bulk-run-how-to-use-bulk-runs-to-extract-data-from-multiple-
urls.html) to re-run all failed extractions at once.

## Filter by Created by

Track how extractions were initiated:

  * User - manually triggered runs

  * Monitor - scheduled automatic extractions

  * API - webhook or integration triggers

  * System - optimization tasks (no credit usage)

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1888474855/96f862fdc0f11cf5650705719700/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image4b6e.png?expires=1771547400&signature=97e53fbdfa885ad934f8cd56ba16c1d97506c673bcb8570c482866f026c9f697&req=dSgvHs15mYlaXPMW1HO4zdYaSezDGSA3i4L03%2Bjws2mMbDX4eYnt69zt51Ty%0A9lop41kmIZ%2BEEMSQbVY%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1888474855/96f862fdc0f11cf5650705719700/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image4b6e.png?expires=1771547400&signature=97e53fbdfa885ad934f8cd56ba16c1d97506c673bcb8570c482866f026c9f697&req=dSgvHs15mYlaXPMW1HO4zdYaSezDGSA3i4L03%2Bjws2mMbDX4eYnt69zt51Ty%0A9lop41kmIZ%2BEEMSQbVY%3D%0A)

##  
[List Name] Limit filter

If your robot extracts lists (like products, properties, or articles), you can
filter by how many items you chose to extract:

  * Filter to see only your comprehensive data sets (higher limits).

  * Focus on smaller, more targeted extractions.

  * Compare different sampling sizes.

✅ This filter only appears if your robot extracts data from a list.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1387974433/64e8891fa0e7001733af49a4318b/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imageefae.png?expires=1771547400&signature=cc6121ddfee718ed892c697b0818f9f5d649cab32639161c9a4ac68f6366a647&req=dSMvEcB5mYVcWvMW1HO4zeGtYiyM8HgYHLr%2FG31gUsEsbOYWQeXf8xNixMBg%0A77sDn3W0zEVDT2dsVKI%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1387974433/64e8891fa0e7001733af49a4318b/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imageefae.png?expires=1771547400&signature=cc6121ddfee718ed892c697b0818f9f5d649cab32639161c9a4ac68f6366a647&req=dSMvEcB5mYVcWvMW1HO4zeGtYiyM8HgYHLr%2FG31gUsEsbOYWQeXf8xNixMBg%0A77sDn3W0zEVDT2dsVKI%3D%0A)

## Bulk Run name filter

Filtering by [Bulk Run](10453933-bulk-run-how-to-use-bulk-runs-to-extract-
data-from-multiple-urls.html) you can:

  * Isolate specific bulk operations.

  * Track progress of large extraction jobs.

✅ This filter only appears if you've executed bulk run(s).

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1388063699/277204562a94cf61004c15886863/CleanShot%202025-02-19%20at%2015.40.05.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27CleanShot%25202025-02-19%2520at%252015.40.055474.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=e3c0246fe1bc8e282688c66cb23d6fea25cc320d13c607ccc5654138d85b2bf4&req=dSMvHsl4nodWUPMW1HO4zffOD3nJtbRoZkmVSNqmwRfSz6CDPrSgUgEgxxLN%0AZICHRRxlyS4ZMr%2BeGBQ%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1388063699/277204562a94cf61004c15886863/CleanShot%202025-02-19%20at%2015.40.05.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27CleanShot%25202025-02-19%2520at%252015.40.055474.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=e3c0246fe1bc8e282688c66cb23d6fea25cc320d13c607ccc5654138d85b2bf4&req=dSMvHsl4nodWUPMW1HO4zffOD3nJtbRoZkmVSNqmwRfSz6CDPrSgUgEgxxLN%0AZICHRRxlyS4ZMr%2BeGBQ%3D%0A)

## Input parameter filtering

When your robot uses input parameters (like search terms), they appear as
filterable columns:

  * Filter by specific search keywords

  * Isolate results from particular form inputs

  * Compare different parameter combinations

Example: In an Amazon search robot, filter by Search Keyword = "laptop" to see
only laptop search results.

# Combining multiple filters

Apply multiple filters for custom views. Common combinations include:

  * Status = "Failed" \+ Extract Date = "Today" → Today's issues to fix

  * Origin URL = "example.com" \+ Created by = "Monitor" → Automated monitoring results for a specific site

  * Status = "Successful" \+ Show Historical Data unchecked → Latest clean data for export

# Managing your filters

## Active filters

  * All active filters appear as badges above your Table

  * Each badge shows the filter type and value

  * Click the **X** on any badge to remove that filter

## Clear all filters

  * Click **Clear all filters** to reset your view

  * Returns you to seeing all available data

# Working with historical data

The **Show Historical Data** toggle works alongside filters to control your
view.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1888490926/faba6310f7fdd8dbf696809d3d65/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagec02f.png?expires=1771547400&signature=9fb5a151c4191c7bba60fe7cbbe59cfde10996d3b5c0470ba7a3da2db3014032&req=dSgvHs13nYhdX%2FMW1HO4zbwDUOjJhuCiMybrz4JY%2BVXHFvPz6CxDC7OE%2FvM7%0AtkvAfADTxJ4M35kpr4Q%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1888490926/faba6310f7fdd8dbf696809d3d65/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagec02f.png?expires=1771547400&signature=9fb5a151c4191c7bba60fe7cbbe59cfde10996d3b5c0470ba7a3da2db3014032&req=dSgvHs13nYhdX%2FMW1HO4zbwDUOjJhuCiMybrz4JY%2BVXHFvPz6CxDC7OE%2FvM7%0AtkvAfADTxJ4M35kpr4Q%3D%0A)

## Understanding historical data

  * **☑ Show Historical Data** \- Displays ALL extractions, including multiple runs with identical parameters

  * **☐ Show Historical Data** \- Shows only the LATEST extraction for each unique parameter combination

## When to hide historical data

Perfect for:

  * Preparing clean exports with only current data

  * Monitoring dashboards showing latest status

  * Reducing clutter when you run the same extraction daily

**Example** : If you monitor prices daily, unchecking this shows only today's
prices instead of the entire price history.

## Combining with filters

Historical data toggle + filters = powerful data control:

  * Hide historical + Status filter = Latest successful data only

  * Hide historical + Date filter = Most recent data within a date range

  * Show historical + Origin URL filter = Complete history for specific pages

# How filters affect your exports

💡 Whatever you see in your filtered Table is exactly what exports.

  * Applied filters carry through to all export formats (CSV, JSON, AWS)

  * Hidden columns also exclude from exports

  * Historical data toggle affects what's included

## Export examples

**Scenario 1** : Export only successful extractions

  1. Filter by Status = "Successful"

  2. Click Export

  3. Result: CSV contains only successful runs

**Scenario 2** : Export latest data without duplicates

  1. Uncheck "Show Historical Data"

  2. Add any additional filters

  3. Click Export

  4. Result: Only the most recent unique data

**Scenario 3** : Export this week's monitoring results

  1. Filter by Created by = "Monitor"

  2. Filter by Extract Date = this week

  3. Click Export

  4. Result: Only automated extractions from this week

How to run your robot

# How to run your robot

Run your robots manually with single or Bulk Tasks, or automate with monitors,
workflows, integration platforms, and our REST API.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 11 months ago

Table of contents

Browse AI offers multiple ways to run your robots, from simple manual
operations to fully automated solutions. This flexibility allows you to choose
the best method based on your needs, whether you're running a one-time
extraction or setting up ongoing data monitoring.

# Manual running a task

## Running a single task

The most straightforward way to run your robot is through the **Run Task**
tab:

  1. Navigate to your robot's detail page.

  2. Select the **Run Task** tab.

  3. Click the **Run a Task** button in the bottom right corner.

  4. Your robot will execute and add the new data to your table.

This method is ideal for quick, one-off data extractions or when testing your
robot.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1388084252/92b4230f68c3d369b91ce4af8ece/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image34c1.png?expires=1771547400&signature=fcec45987c1872dec62aa3c17fe4a6e7f7d1120ac1ba63562f18474575cc0cf1&req=dSMvHsl2mYNaW%2FMW1HO4zb3oILnc1ayVERILwRHrYLOGIfnKvoN9%2FbPSIoqf%0AzzGRIkdtb298uxBiqz0%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1388084252/92b4230f68c3d369b91ce4af8ece/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image34c1.png?expires=1771547400&signature=fcec45987c1872dec62aa3c17fe4a6e7f7d1120ac1ba63562f18474575cc0cf1&req=dSMvHsl2mYNaW%2FMW1HO4zb3oILnc1ayVERILwRHrYLOGIfnKvoN9%2FbPSIoqf%0AzzGRIkdtb298uxBiqz0%3D%0A)

## Using bulk run for multiple URLs

When you need to extract data from multiple URLs at once, use the Bulk Run
feature:

**Method 1: From the Run Task tab**

  1. Navigate to your robot's detail page.

  2. Select the **Run Task** tab.

  3. Click the **Bulk Run Tasks** button in the bottom right corner.

  4. Upload a CSV file containing the URLs you want to scrape.

  5. Confirm to start the bulk operation.

**Method 2: From the robot 's table**

  1. Navigate to your robot's detail page.

  2. Go to the **Table** tab.

  3. Click the **Import CSV** button above the data table.

  4. In the sidebar that opens, click to upload your CSV file.

  5. Confirm to start the bulk operation.

Your CSV file should contain a column with URLs. Make sure the URLs are
formatted correctly and accessible to your robot. There is a sample CSV you
can download from this screen if you're unsure of the format.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1388084255/26bacc9bd2f8fa437408009e9c5a/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image6fce.png?expires=1771547400&signature=59bf5595b02f071a414eb814a6e6827a43491c0d6999c68139e1c650a85e5f72&req=dSMvHsl2mYNaXPMW1HO4za9M38bhDNgtpDRAc4%2F2qifSExAnvb0MA11lJzI0%0ApUgM7%2BKEz8D9jsc43Uo%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1388084255/26bacc9bd2f8fa437408009e9c5a/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image6fce.png?expires=1771547400&signature=59bf5595b02f071a414eb814a6e6827a43491c0d6999c68139e1c650a85e5f72&req=dSMvHsl2mYNaXPMW1HO4za9M38bhDNgtpDRAc4%2F2qifSExAnvb0MA11lJzI0%0ApUgM7%2BKEz8D9jsc43Uo%3D%0A)

#  
Automating tasks in Browse AI

## Using scheduled monitors

Set your robot to run automatically on a regular schedule:

  1. Navigate to your robot's detail page.

  2. Select the **Monitor** tab.

  3. Click **Create a Monitor.**

  4. Set your preferred schedule (hourly, daily, weekly, etc.).

  5. Configure any additional settings like notifications.

  6. Save your monitor.

Scheduled runs are perfect for ongoing data monitoring, price tracking, or any
situation where you need regular updates without manual intervention.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1388096276/db2278c3fca28f01a7af758bf830/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image9151.png?expires=1771547400&signature=c5c3c0cbb3b3b276e45fe93d4c560df5b679e34d3135b3fdf92aaeeaf3a41233&req=dSMvHsl3m4NYX%2FMW1HO4zQOt2W4FWvsIUNZiI0DeYPWwGRaI4L7MtEzr88yz%0AbEeV9LiSR%2B0ISPopjis%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1388096276/db2278c3fca28f01a7af758bf830/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image9151.png?expires=1771547400&signature=c5c3c0cbb3b3b276e45fe93d4c560df5b679e34d3135b3fdf92aaeeaf3a41233&req=dSMvHsl3m4NYX%2FMW1HO4zQOt2W4FWvsIUNZiI0DeYPWwGRaI4L7MtEzr88yz%0AbEeV9LiSR%2B0ISPopjis%3D%0A)

##  
Running through Workflows

Connect multiple robots together with Browse AI's Workflow feature:

  1. Create a primary robot (Robot A) to extract initial data.

  2. Create a secondary robot (Robot B) to trigger based on inputs from Robot A.

  3. Set up a workflow that automatically triggers Robot B after Robot A completes.

This approach is ideal for deep scraping scenarios where you need to extract
data across multiple linked pages.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1388084250/8a84714bee34de7b2127f1254a0c/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image0f1d.png?expires=1771547400&signature=554c90cdc1504c2040fab5559ec358be7c0e501d23f71748f03805656ae94f6d&req=dSMvHsl2mYNaWfMW1HO4zQOiJcVMFhSXseyiRaiz9%2BkHFUlKh4GYW3ANoSG3%0Am1gQz9KSqd7VUmqrtMo%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1388084250/8a84714bee34de7b2127f1254a0c/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image0f1d.png?expires=1771547400&signature=554c90cdc1504c2040fab5559ec358be7c0e501d23f71748f03805656ae94f6d&req=dSMvHsl2mYNaWfMW1HO4zQOiJcVMFhSXseyiRaiz9%2BkHFUlKh4GYW3ANoSG3%0Am1gQz9KSqd7VUmqrtMo%3D%0A)

# Automating tasks using an Integration

## Using automation platforms

Connect Browse AI with popular automation platforms:

  * Zapier: create Zaps that trigger robot runs based on events in other apps.

  * Make.com: build scenarios that include Browse AI robot runs.

  * Pabbly Connect: create automated workflows that trigger robot runs.

These integrations allow you to incorporate web data extraction into your
broader business processes and systems. Read our [integration
guide](10459396-integrations-how-to-send-extracted-data-to-other-tools-and-
apps.html) to learn more.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1388086898/7af769589894ad7549e837d3d078/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image673f.png?expires=1771547400&signature=f9d64d2f98d0f2680262ee2a84e420276785970c7250a755ac72319c41b2153f&req=dSMvHsl2m4lWUfMW1HO4zTyOpzKmSch7VoBZbTA%2FZGgODkeQX4ru%2Fl5AF5Mf%0A1%2BELATtszWDbU9ERnQQ%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1388086898/7af769589894ad7549e837d3d078/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image673f.png?expires=1771547400&signature=f9d64d2f98d0f2680262ee2a84e420276785970c7250a755ac72319c41b2153f&req=dSMvHsl2m4lWUfMW1HO4zTyOpzKmSch7VoBZbTA%2FZGgODkeQX4ru%2Fl5AF5Mf%0A1%2BELATtszWDbU9ERnQQ%3D%0A)

## Using the REST API

For developers and technical users, Browse AI offers a comprehensive REST API
that provides programmatic control over robots, tasks, monitors, webhooks, and
bulk runs:

  1. Authentication: all API requests require your secret API key for authentication.

  2. Running robots: use the API to run robots with custom parameters.

  3. Managing robots: retrieve, update, and manage your robots.

  4. Bulk operations: run up to 500,000 tasks at once with different parameters.

  5. Scheduling monitors: create and manage automated monitoring schedules.

  6. Real-time updates: configure webhooks to receive notifications when tasks complete.

The API gives you complete control over your data extraction processes and
integrates seamlessly with your existing systems and workflows.

[View the full API documentation](https://api.browse.ai/v2/docs)

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1388094586/120483f14a59d2d5ecda1284a53c/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image9681.png?expires=1771547400&signature=9ac1d249b11d1000606ac28e891d6e781b0ab8742b34c6977e8ac2442a97e658&req=dSMvHsl3mYRXX%2FMW1HO4zQmCmNz7tVNxFXLliPh%2FjPaY909k0YoGQL4M4Q4o%0AoxIFJAoPpTuxTMC9L%2B8%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1388094586/120483f14a59d2d5ecda1284a53c/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image9681.png?expires=1771547400&signature=9ac1d249b11d1000606ac28e891d6e781b0ab8742b34c6977e8ac2442a97e658&req=dSMvHsl3mYRXX%2FMW1HO4zQmCmNz7tVNxFXLliPh%2FjPaY909k0YoGQL4M4Q4o%0AoxIFJAoPpTuxTMC9L%2B8%3D%0A)

How to show, hide, and resize Table columns

# How to show, hide, and resize Table columns

Tables display all your extracted data, but you don't always need to see
everything at once. Customize your view to focus on what matters most, whether
you're analyzing specific data points or preparing clean exports.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 2 months ago

Table of contents

# How to show/hide columns

Control exactly which columns appear in your Table view.

  1. Look for the **columns button** in the top-right corner of your Table (shows number + "columns").

  2. Click to open the column selection panel.

  3. **Toggle off** any columns you want to hide.

  4. Your view updates instantly.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1543392181/f095dcb4d367051a17b66d6e0a97/CleanShot%202025-05-27%20at%2010.09.20.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27CleanShot%25202025-05-27%2520at%252010.09.206adb.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=c0f448ac0ba8382575a7fbda24d3cac205335900c98b2ffa5152634641886d71&req=dSUjFcp3n4BXWPMW1HO4zcrvV%2BYVHNzRfg40jy6TYqJLzcAnN7fsIPIbn7v%2B%0AVn1MEKwfm32qHd7ztZg%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1543392181/f095dcb4d367051a17b66d6e0a97/CleanShot%202025-05-27%20at%2010.09.20.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27CleanShot%25202025-05-27%2520at%252010.09.206adb.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=c0f448ac0ba8382575a7fbda24d3cac205335900c98b2ffa5152634641886d71&req=dSUjFcp3n4BXWPMW1HO4zcrvV%2BYVHNzRfg40jy6TYqJLzcAnN7fsIPIbn7v%2B%0AVn1MEKwfm32qHd7ztZg%3D%0A)

**💡** Hidden columns are excluded from exports. Only visible columns export to
CSV or JSON.

# How to show all columns

  1. Click the **columns button.**

  2. Select **Show All Columns** at the bottom of the panel

  3. All columns instantly reappear

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1543397595/4b6712152f1c261225c6cf047f42/CleanShot%202025-05-27%20at%2010.10.46.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27CleanShot%25202025-05-27%2520at%252010.10.46b00b.png?expires=1771547400&signature=997320fb1d211bfea4cc804a6bc5c0abca5c80a31573b259c15b7008677a6371&req=dSUjFcp3moRWXPMW1HO4zW0Q7tD8ifSWxZ41YYb6rinTzUoeDim5mseRoPhk%0A4zrv%2BKidYNs%2BgaIjY7M%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1543397595/4b6712152f1c261225c6cf047f42/CleanShot%202025-05-27%20at%2010.10.46.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27CleanShot%25202025-05-27%2520at%252010.10.46b00b.png?expires=1771547400&signature=997320fb1d211bfea4cc804a6bc5c0abca5c80a31573b259c15b7008677a6371&req=dSUjFcp3moRWXPMW1HO4zW0Q7tD8ifSWxZ41YYb6rinTzUoeDim5mseRoPhk%0A4zrv%2BKidYNs%2BgaIjY7M%3D%0A)

# Resizing columns

While you can't reorder columns, you can adjust their width to improve
readability.

  1. **Hover** over the column divider (cursor changes to resize icon).

  2. **Click and drag** left or right to adjust width.

  3. Release when you've reached your desired size.

## View full cell contents

For cells with more content than can be displayed:

  1. **Double-click** any cell.

  2. A popup displays the complete contents.

  3. Perfect for long URLs, descriptions, or nested data.

# Creating task-specific views

Combine column management with [filters](10594160-how-to-filter-your-
data.html) for powerful custom views.

## Analysis view

  1. Hide all metadata columns (IDs, timestamps)

  2. Show only data columns you're analyzing

  3. Apply status filter for "Successful" only

  4. Export this clean dataset

## Troubleshooting view

  1. Show all columns

  2. Filter by "Failed" status

  3. Focus on error-related fields

  4. Widen status and URL columns

## Export preparation view

  1. Hide internal reference columns

  2. Show only client-facing data

  3. Apply date range filters

  4. Verify column selection matches export needs  
​

[Managing your data view](../collections/17371279-managing-your-data-
view.html)

Can data be deleted from a Table?

# Can data be deleted from a Table?

No, data cannot be deleted from Tables. Tables serve as a permanent historical
record of all data your robot has extracted.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 2 months ago

Table of contents

# Why Tables preserve all data

## Complete audit trail

Every extraction is permanently recorded, providing:

  * Proof of when data was captured.

  * Evidence of historical values.

  * Verification of changes over time.

  * Compliance documentation.

## Historical analysis

Permanent records enable:

  * Price tracking over months or years

  * Inventory trend analysis

  * Content change monitoring

  * Competitive intelligence archives

## Data integrity

Non-deletable data ensures:

  * No accidental loss of valuable information

  * Consistent historical records

  * Reliable data for reporting

  * Protection against user errors

# Common scenarios and solutions

## "I have test data cluttering my Table"

_Solution: Use filters to hide test runs_

  * Filter by date to exclude testing periods

  * Filter by status to show only successful runs

  * Use "Show Historical Data" toggle to see only latest results

## "I extracted the wrong data"

_Solution: Run your robot again with correct settings_

  * New extractions don't replace old ones

  * Filter by most recent date to see only new data

  * Retrain your robot if needed for different data points

## "I have duplicate extractions"

_Solution: Use the historical data toggle_

  * Uncheck "Show Historical Data" to see only the latest version

  * Each unique URL/parameter combination shows once

  * Perfect for monitoring scenarios with daily runs

## "I need to comply with data deletion requests"

_Solution: Control access and exports_

  * Remove user access to workspaces with sensitive data

  * Export and maintain records externally where you can delete

  * Contact Browse AI support for compliance-related concerns

## Think of Tables as append-only databases

Unlike spreadsheets where you can edit cells, Tables work more like:

  * Transaction logs - every action is recorded.

  * Version control - all versions preserved.

  * Audit systems - complete trail maintained.

# Features that help manage permanent data

## Filtering tools

  * Hide irrelevant data without deleting

  * Create custom views for different needs

  * Export only what you want to see

## Historical data toggle

  * Control whether you see all versions

  * Reduce clutter from repeated extractions

  * Focus on current data when needed

## Column visibility

  * Hide columns you don't need

  * Excluded columns don't appear in exports

  * Create cleaner views without deletion

## Calculated columns

  * Add status flags to mark obsolete data

  * Create "ignore" markers for filtering

  * Enhance data without modifying originals

## What you CAN modify

While you can't delete data, you can:

  * ✅ Hide rows using [filters](10594160-how-to-filter-your-data.html)

  * ✅ [Hide columns](10595195-how-to-show-hide-and-resize-table-columns.html) from view and exports

  * ✅ Add [calculated columns](https://calcu/) for new data

  * ✅ [Export](10593816-how-to-export-data-from-tables.html) subsets of your data

* * *

Can I add custom tabs to a Table?

# Can I add custom tabs to a Table?

No, tabs are automatically generated based on what your robot extracts and
cannot be manually added. However, you can control your Table structure by
modifying what your robot captures.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 2 months ago

Table of contents

No, it's not currently possible to manually add new tabs to your robot's
Table. Tabs are automatically generated based on your robot's extraction
settings and cannot be customized after the robot has been trained.

Tables automatically generate tab(s) based on how the robot was trained:

  * Main tab - always present, shows extraction summaries.

  * List tabs - One for each list your robot extracts (Products, Results, etc.).

# Getting the tab structure you want

## Option 1: Retrain your robot

Modify what lists your robot extracts:

  1. Go to Settings > Danger Zone > Retrain robot

  2. Add or modify the lists you're capturing

  3. Save and run - new tabs appear automatically

📖 You can learn more about retraining your robot [here](10518674-how-to-
retrain-or-edit-a-robot.html).

## Option 2: Use multiple robots

Create separate robots for different data structures:

  * Each robot has its own Table with unique tabs

  * Ideal for completely different data types

  * Maintain clean, focused data organization

## Option 3: Export and customize

Export your data to reorganize as needed:

  * Use CSV/JSON exports

  * Create custom views in Excel or Google Sheets

  * Build exactly the structure you need

💡 Map out the structure you want before training to get the tabs you want from
the start.

* * *

Import CSV for bulk data extraction

# Import CSV for bulk data extraction

Import a CSV file to automatically extract data from thousands of URLs or
input parameters. This feature supports up to 50,000 rows and is perfect for
batch processing, deep scraping workflows, and running with different
parameters.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 2 months ago

Table of contents

✅ You will need an [approved robot](10518718-how-to-approve-a-robot.html) in
order to automatically extract data from a list of input parameters, or URLs.

💡 Need to process more than 50,000 URLs? Use the dedicated [Bulk Run
feature](10453933-bulk-run-how-to-use-bulk-runs-to-extract-data-from-multiple-
urls.html) which supports up to 500,000 URLs.

In **Tables** you can upload a list of input parameters or URLs to
automatically extract data from thousands of web pages.

For example:

  * Scraping large numbers of product detail pages.

  * Extracting data from multiple search results.

  * Performing deep scraping across many pages.

  * Batch processing with different parameters.

# How to import a CSV into Tables

## Format your CSV file for upload

Your CSV must have column headers matching your robot's input parameters
exactly.

This could include:

  * originUrl column containing full URLs (including https://)

  * Additional columns for any other parameters your robot needs

Make sure to include:

  * Include a header row with column names.

  * Make sure URLs include the full address (including http:// or https://).

  * Avoid special characters in your CSV.

  * Ensure the file is properly encoded (UTF-8 recommended).

💡 You can download a sample file directly from **Tables** if you're unsure of
the correct format. Go to **Tables** , and select **Import CSV.** From there
you can download a sample CSV to use as a template.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1894480825/6af69c2c424d0543ac4c2b048c1a/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image9edd.png?expires=1771547400&signature=346cf17ffe648d8650b142e062fc0ce7924e346d93e8cfe74c9c7b6efab01107&req=dSguEs12nYldXPMW1HO4zX0BhN0pHieYrJ4RSCq8FjvshtjmUmo9iXaDdQzu%0AZvueZ9orL2AMQXCeLWg%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1894480825/6af69c2c424d0543ac4c2b048c1a/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image9edd.png?expires=1771547400&signature=346cf17ffe648d8650b142e062fc0ce7924e346d93e8cfe74c9c7b6efab01107&req=dSguEs12nYldXPMW1HO4zX0BhN0pHieYrJ4RSCq8FjvshtjmUmo9iXaDdQzu%0AZvueZ9orL2AMQXCeLWg%3D%0A)

## Import your file to run your bulk task

  1. In your robot's **Table** , click **Import CSV**.

  2. Click **Click to upload** in the sidebar and select your CSV file.

  3. Verify the column mapping is correct and click **Start Bulk Run**.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1894485450/60a34a57586c8e597b43f15e3f1f/CleanShot%25252B2025-02-12%25252Bat%25252B17_17_39.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27CleanShot%25252B2025-02-12%25252Bat%25252B17_17_396e34.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=c56e4149f7ca7cd2f66b3c161c2cbe224b8a3890290494a4010accc75dc3cd06&req=dSguEs12mIVaWfMW1HO4zTpJa32XcHsLFqvkdsnY0lR24gwcHgFdwGnfVPd7%0A9AuITZXODsELoupqutc%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1894485450/60a34a57586c8e597b43f15e3f1f/CleanShot%25252B2025-02-12%25252Bat%25252B17_17_39.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27CleanShot%25252B2025-02-12%25252Bat%25252B17_17_396e34.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=c56e4149f7ca7cd2f66b3c161c2cbe224b8a3890290494a4010accc75dc3cd06&req=dSguEs12mIVaWfMW1HO4zTpJa32XcHsLFqvkdsnY0lR24gwcHgFdwGnfVPd7%0A9AuITZXODsELoupqutc%3D%0A)

## Review your Bulk Run progress and results

Your imported tasks will:

  * The run will appear in your **Table** with a unique Bulk Run identifier.

  * Tasks will process sequentially.

  * You can filter your **Table** by the _Bulk Run name_ to see only these results.

  * Progress indicators will show how many tasks are complete vs. pending.

💡 Depending on the complexity of your robot and the number of tasks you've
triggered, it may take a few minutes for your robot to process the full list
of tasks.

# Deep scraping with CSV import

A common workflow is using one robot's output as another robot's input. For
example extracting a list of product details from a category page.

  * Robot A extracts product URLs from category pages.

  * Robot B is trained to extract data from a single product page.

To create a dataset of all product pages from the category pages you would:

  1. Export Robot A's URLs as CSV.

  2. Import to Robot B to automatically extract details from all pages.

💡 You can also do this in the product using our
[workflows](10518596-workflows-how-to-connect-robots-together-to-scrape-
across-multiple-pages.html) feature.

## **How to deep scrape with Tables**

✅ To use this feature, you need to have a trained robot for:

  * Robot A: trained to extract a list of URLs or input parameters

  * Robot B: trained to extract details on a page using a URL or input parameter.

  1. Export URLs from Robot A's Table

  2. Rename columns to match Robot B's parameters

  3. Keep only the columns Robot B needs

  4. Import to Robot B (max 50,000 rows)

# Common use cases

## E-commerce product monitoring

Import URLs of multiple product pages to extract pricing, features, and
availability across competitors.

## Real estate property data

After scraping property listings from search results, use Bulk Run to get
detailed information about each property.

## Content aggregation

Import URLs of articles or blog posts to extract and compile content from
multiple sources.

## Lead generation

Scrape contact details from a list of company pages or professional profiles.

[Optimization and best practices](../collections/11458089-optimization-and-
best-practices.html)

Best practices and tips for web scraping, data extraction, and monitoring
websites

# Best practices and tips for web scraping, data extraction, and monitoring
websites

Best practices for training reliable web scraping robots in Browse AI. This
guide covers everything from initial setup through testing and optimization.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 3 months ago

Table of contents

# Planning your data extraction strategy

You want to know what success looks like before you start training. Ask
yourself:

  1. Is the data I need on one page, or across multiple pages?

  2. What 'fields' do I want to extract from each page, how do I want it structured?

  3. How many pages and/or rows of data am I extracting?

  4. Do I need this data on an ongoing basis?

  5. How am I using this data once it's extracted?

# Site structure: Single page vs. multi-level extraction

**Site structure**| **Extraction type**  
---|---  
All data is on one page type| Single-page extraction  
Data is spread across multiple pages| Multi-level extraction  
  
## Single page extraction

If all your data is on one page type (search results, directory listing),
you'll want to:

  1. [Train a robot](10496382-how-to-train-a-robot-to-scrape-or-monitor-data.html) to extract data from one of those pages.

  2. Use that robot to extract from multiple URLs with the same page by either:

    1. [Uploading a list of URLs](10453933-bulk-run-how-to-use-bulk-runs-to-extract-data-from-multiple-urls.html).

    2. [Adding a new row in tables](10595023-is-it-possible-to-manually-add-data-to-a-robot-s-table.html).

    3. Changing the [Origin URL](https://orig/).

## Multi-level extraction (deep scraping)

If the data you need to extract is across multiple page types, this is called
deep scraping. For example:

  * E-commerce (product list → product details)

  * Real estate (listing → property details)

  * Job boards (search results → full descriptions)

You'll need to train robots for each page type, and connect them together
using workflows.

  1. [Train a robot](10496382-how-to-train-a-robot-to-scrape-or-monitor-data.html) to extract data from each of the page 'types'.

  2. Connect these robots together using [workflows](10553465-how-to-extract-data-across-multiple-pages-deep-scraping.html).

Example: You're looking to extract e-commerce product data based on a search
term and the data you need is on the search results page, and the product
pages.

  1. Train a robot to extract:

    1. Robot A: a product list based on a search term. 

    2. Robot B: product details on a specific page. 

  2. Connect Robot A to Robot B using workflows to automatically scrape the product detail pages that appear as a result for Robot A. 

📖 Read our guide on [how to extract different data structures and types across
multiple web page](10553465-how-to-extract-data-across-multiple-pages-deep-
scraping.html).

💡 You can use workflows to chain unlimited robots together - ex: Robot A >
Robot B > Robot C > Robot D.

# Data structure: Extracting different data types

Depending on what kind of data you want to extract, you'll want to think about
how you're going to train your robot.

## How the data on the page is structured

The way data appears on the page determines which extraction method to use.

| **Capture List**| **Capture Text**  
---|---|---  
Data displayed as| • Repeating cards or rows  
• Search results grids  
• Product galleries  
• Directory listings  
• Comment threads  
• Table rows  
• Any pattern that repeats| • Individual values  
• Standalone elements  
• Summary information  
• Page headers/totals  
• Single data points  
• Unique page sections  
Best to use when| You see the same type of information repeated multiple times
in a consistent pattern (like multiple products, each with name, price,
image)| You need specific individual elements that appear once on the page, or
when building a custom data structure from scattered elements.  
Creates| Structured table with multiple rows - one row per item in the
pattern.| Structured table where each selection becomes a column.  
Example| A search page with 20 products → Creates 20 rows of data| A product
detail page → Creates 1 row with your selected fields as columns  
Key advantage| Automatically scales across all similar items and handles
pagination.| Complete control over what to extract and how to structure it.  
  
📖 Learn more about when to extract data [as a list vs. just
text](10477709-capture-text-from-a-list-vs-just-text.html).

## Form fields, and search parameters

If the data you're extracting is triggered based on forms fields or search
parameters you'll need to configure these as [input
parameters](https://input/):

  1. During training, naturally fill out the form or search box. 

  2. After the robot is approved, these will be converted into input parameters.

  3. Use that robot to extract from input parameters by either:

    1. [Uploading a list of input parameters](10453933-bulk-run-how-to-use-bulk-runs-to-extract-data-from-multiple-urls.html).

    2. [Adding a new row in tables](10595023-is-it-possible-to-manually-add-data-to-a-robot-s-table.html).

    3. Changing the [input parameter](https://input/) in the robot dashboard.

💡 Any text that you train a robot to enter as part of the training process is
captured as an input parameter.

## Dynamic content

Anything a human can see on a web page, you can train a robot to capture. This
includes:

  * [Infinite scroll and lazy loading](12882972-how-to-handle-infinite-scroll-and-lazy-loading.html)

  * [Drop downs and interactive elements](12883179-extracting-data-from-dropdowns-and-interactive-elements.html)

  * [Pop ups and modals](12883336-handling-popups-and-modal-windows.html)

  * [Data that appears on click](12883397-extracting-data-that-appears-on-click.html)

  * [Javascript rendered content](12883448-working-with-javascript-rendered-content.html)

  * [Pagination that requires multiple clicks](10875387-how-to-handle-pagination-that-has-multiple-clicks-to-load-more-items.html)

  * [Location based data](12271191-does-browse-ai-offer-location-based-extraction-for-region-dynamic-content.html)

# Volume of data: simple extractions, bulk runs, and service options

If you're looking to extract from a few pages, you can train your robot to
extract data from other pages by either [adding a new row in
tables](10595023-is-it-possible-to-manually-add-data-to-a-robot-s-table.html),
or changing the [input parameter](https://input/) in the robot dashboard.

## Bulk extraction and web scraping

You can run a robot on thousands of pages simultaneously with [Bulk
Runs](10453933-bulk-run-how-to-use-bulk-runs-to-extract-data-from-multiple-
urls.html).

  1. Go to the approved robot and select **Bulk run tasks**. 

  2. Upload a list of input parameters. 

💡 Input parameters depend on how you trained your robot. They always include
the Origin URL, and depending on the data can include search query terms or
other text inputs.

## Services and solutions

We also offer dedicated service and solutions for large scale data extraction.
[Talk to our sales team](https://www.browse.ai/service) to learn more.

# Data frequency: Single use or Ongoing

If you only have a single use for the data, it's best to export or integrate
the data as needed once you create your robot(s).

If however any (or all) of these apply to you, you'll want to set up a
monitor:

  * Have an ongoing use for this data. 

  * Want to keep this data up to date. 

  * Want to maintain a historical database of this data. 

  * Want to be alerted when this data changes. 

## Setting up monitoring

You can set up monitors to check for changes to website content on a schedule.

  1. Go to your robot dashboard and click **Monitor**. 

  2. Create a new monitor and configure your frequency and alert settings. 

💡 Once created, your robot will check for content changes based on the
schedule you selected. It will also notify you when things change, and what
has changed based on your settings.

# Using the data you've extracted

You can export, connect, or integrate data from a robot depending on your
internal tools, apps, and workflows.

## Exporting the data

  * [You can manually export as either a CSV or JSON file](10593816-how-to-export-data-from-tables.html)

  * [Export the data to an AWS S3 bucket](12305998-how-to-set-up-scheduled-exports-for-aws-s3.html)

  * [Set up scheduled exports to an AWS S3 bucket](12305998-how-to-set-up-scheduled-exports-for-aws-s3.html)

  * [Sync the data to a Google Sheet](10535898-how-to-sync-scraped-data-with-google-sheets.html)

  * [Sync the data to an Airtable](10537018-how-to-enable-airtable-integration.html)

## **Integrating the data**

  * Create workflows and automations with [Zapier](10536328-how-to-connect-browse-ai-with-zapier.html), [Make](10573987-how-to-integrate-your-robot-with-make-com.html), and [Pabbly](10537045-how-to-integrate-browse-ai-with-pabbly-connect.html)

  * [Turn the data into an API](12683249-api-guide-getting-started.html)

  * [Get sent the data via webhook](12683642-webhooks-set-up-guide.html)

How to download the results of a Robot's task (non-Tables method)

# How to download the results of a Robot's task (non-Tables method)

Download your robot's scraped data directly from the History tab in CSV or
JSON format, with options to include or exclude previous changes.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 12 months ago

Table of contents

Browse AI offers two ways to download your scraped data: directly from the
Task History tab (covered here) or through the Tables interface.

The History tab method is quick and focused on individual task results.

# Downloading from the Task History tab

Follow these steps to download data from a specific robot run:

  1. Navigate to your robot in the Browse AI dashboard

  2. Go to your robot's History tab.

  3. Click into the task that has the data you'd like to download.

  4. Select if you want to export historical data using the **Highlight changes since last check**.

    1. Toggle ON: includes highlighting of what changed since the previous run (useful for monitoring).

    1. Toggle OFF: Downloads only the current data without any change indicators.

  5. Click the **Download** button to the right of the list name.

  6. Select whether to download a CSV or JSON file.

    1. CSV (for spreadsheet applications like Excel).

    1. JSON (for developers or advanced data processing). 

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1396531773/59d7eacea31d4c5bb7bc0366a84c/export-
csv-history.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27export-csv-
historyc675.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=8714ed8b9155a0f5153be52bf6b49b8ac989e1bc6495ed23a6938c59c3f10451&req=dSMuEMx9nIZYWvMW1HO4ze1kULAMBCmsjZjiSMcTWVMf2z5c7uICkMoY8xgE%0A6X8B3zwuTnoNTiIuMB8%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1396531773/59d7eacea31d4c5bb7bc0366a84c/export-
csv-history.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27export-csv-
historyc675.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=8714ed8b9155a0f5153be52bf6b49b8ac989e1bc6495ed23a6938c59c3f10451&req=dSMuEMx9nIZYWvMW1HO4ze1kULAMBCmsjZjiSMcTWVMf2z5c7uICkMoY8xgE%0A6X8B3zwuTnoNTiIuMB8%3D%0A)

## When to use the History tab download method

This approach is ideal when you:

  * Need data from one specific robot run.

  * Want to quickly grab results from a recent task.

  * Are troubleshooting or reviewing a particular extraction.

  * Need to compare specific versions of data.

## File format options explained

Choose the format that best suits your needs:

  * **CSV (Comma Separated Values)** :

    * Works with Excel, Google Sheets, and other spreadsheet applications.

    * Easy to view and manipulate data.

    * Best for analysis and reporting.

  * **JSON (JavaScript Object Notation)** :

    * Preserves the structure of complex data.

    * Ideal for developers and integrations.

    * Better for preserving data types.

# Alternative: Downloading using Tables

For more comprehensive data, you can also use the Tables interface:

  1. Go to your robot's **Tables** tab.

  2. Select the tab containing the data you want to download.

  3. Click the **Export** button.

  4. Choose your preferred format (CSV or JSON).

  5. Apply any filters if needed before exporting.

The Tables method offers additional advantages:

  * Filter data before exporting.

  * Export historical data across multiple runs.

  * Select specific data subsets.

  * View and organize your data more extensively.

For detailed instructions on using Tables for downloads, see our guide: [How
to export data from a robot's Table](10593816-how-to-export-data-from-
tables.html).
