Integrating Browse AI with Amazon S3

# Integrating Browse AI with Amazon S3

Securely export your scraped data to your own AWS S3 bucket (no coding
required) with CloudFormation templates.

![Kimber
avatar](../../../static.intercomassets.com/avatars/8022949/square_128/hello-1740080738.png)

Written by Kimber

Updated over 5 months ago

Table of contents

Setting up Browse AI to export data to your S3 bucket is straightforward with
our CloudFormation template approach. This guide walks you through creating
the necessary permissions while maintaining security best practices.

## Why connect Browse AI to S3?

This integration allows you to:

  * Export data extracted by your robots to your own storage

  * Maintain control over your extracted datasets

  * Integrate Browse AI data with your existing AWS workflows

  * Keep a secure backup of all your extracted data

## What you'll need

  * An **Amazon Web Services (AWS) account** with permissions to create CloudFormation stacks and IAM roles.

  * The **name of your S3 bucket** where you want Browse AI to export data.

  * _Optional_ : A specific **folder path** within your bucket if you want to restrict access. For example, if your bucket is named `my-data`, and the folder is named `browse-ai-exports`, you'd use `browse-ai-exports`.  
​

# **How to set up the Amazon S3 integration**

## Step 1. Access AWS Management Console

  1. Login to the **[AWS Management Console](https://aws.amazon.com/console/)**.

  2. Search for **CloudFormation** in the top search and select it. 

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1511148093/0f1e896685f8aa8390b371b4d69a/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imaged54f.png?expires=1771547400&signature=8ba4bba9fa20c710a2635466f4462b0c2f968f18fd1fefbea0ca9b3205d14a5b&req=dSUmF8h6lYFWWvMW1HO4zcm0H7psVfgTa8xrJTy6XNXl7hOgo0Eo9tcIg%2FJV%0AXE9OHH2kl%2FaWrF7tYVM%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1511148093/0f1e896685f8aa8390b371b4d69a/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imaged54f.png?expires=1771547400&signature=8ba4bba9fa20c710a2635466f4462b0c2f968f18fd1fefbea0ca9b3205d14a5b&req=dSUmF8h6lYFWWvMW1HO4zcm0H7psVfgTa8xrJTy6XNXl7hOgo0Eo9tcIg%2FJV%0AXE9OHH2kl%2FaWrF7tYVM%3D%0A)

**What is CloudFormation?** CloudFormation helps automate the setup process
for AWS resources.​

## Step 2. Create a new stack

  1. Click "Create stack".

  2. Select "With new resources (standard)"**** only**** if prompted.

  3. Under "Prerequisite - Prepare template," choose "Choose an existing template."

  4. For "Template source," select "Amazon S3 URL."

  5. Paste the url below in the text field and click "Next".

    
    
    https://browse-ai-integration.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/s3-integration.yaml

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1511150674/221964acdf5855a5b7deab099344/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagedcd0.png?expires=1771547400&signature=e6885fa959e85e7d8022b91b140ba9e54a39e0d899d7dc8c50068891ed0b9ca3&req=dSUmF8h7nYdYXfMW1HO4zW2PCb1%2BjUzE8p4WINPLbA0lI6kUmQBe%2FlqHzkoX%0AGJKYwRd5gHcIoXUm%2BJA%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1511150674/221964acdf5855a5b7deab099344/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagedcd0.png?expires=1771547400&signature=e6885fa959e85e7d8022b91b140ba9e54a39e0d899d7dc8c50068891ed0b9ca3&req=dSUmF8h7nYdYXfMW1HO4zW2PCb1%2BjUzE8p4WINPLbA0lI6kUmQBe%2FlqHzkoX%0AGJKYwRd5gHcIoXUm%2BJA%3D%0A)

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1511151540/1c612b9aab54616005e9ff4ef05b/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image79ea.png?expires=1771547400&signature=b4cfe9ceed897ff686be4469565b327ca54486eb39099a660cd3b78417cc915e&req=dSUmF8h7nIRbWfMW1HO4zVSlZfXfLsWVGoKltzvb6MKF09l6nTXgtFPX%2F2sQ%0A8FIejZnPc%2BXiOmoM1Rg%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1511151540/1c612b9aab54616005e9ff4ef05b/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image79ea.png?expires=1771547400&signature=b4cfe9ceed897ff686be4469565b327ca54486eb39099a660cd3b78417cc915e&req=dSUmF8h7nIRbWfMW1HO4zVSlZfXfLsWVGoKltzvb6MKF09l6nTXgtFPX%2F2sQ%0A8FIejZnPc%2BXiOmoM1Rg%3D%0A)

## Step 3. Configure stack details

  1. Stack name: enter a descriptive name (ex: "BrowseAI-S3-Access").

  2. BucketName: Enter your exact S3 bucket name.

  3. ExternalId: Generate a random 20-character string using letters and numbers. This serves as an additional security verification.

  4. BucketPathName (optional): Enter a folder name if you want to restrict Browse AI's access to a specific folder. Leave blank for full bucket access.

  5. Click "Next."

## Step 4: Configure stack options

  1. The default options are typically sufficient, but you can add tags if needed.

  2. Click "Next."

  3. Review your settings carefully.

  4. Check the box acknowledging that CloudFormation might create IAM resources.

  5. Click "Submit" to deploy the stack.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1511156848/1a1bed0424c18b9750fe2cca286d/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imageca2b.png?expires=1771547400&signature=b6702e6ec80741075970a6ecb4146354d6c42f29bcd52dd6e377fc73bebd9a91&req=dSUmF8h7m4lbUfMW1HO4zZ3YvThYNJmUC%2FSOnLhX%2FEARXQLnwEyYGDJwBSG2%0ArYkAHUuhT0OrTulPsXw%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1511156848/1a1bed0424c18b9750fe2cca286d/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imageca2b.png?expires=1771547400&signature=b6702e6ec80741075970a6ecb4146354d6c42f29bcd52dd6e377fc73bebd9a91&req=dSUmF8h7m4lbUfMW1HO4zZ3YvThYNJmUC%2FSOnLhX%2FEARXQLnwEyYGDJwBSG2%0ArYkAHUuhT0OrTulPsXw%3D%0A)

## Step 5: Retrieve the IAM Role ARN

  1. After stack creation completes, go to the "Stack info" tab.

  2. Find the "S3AccessRole" in the list and click its Physical ID link.

  3. In the IAM console that opens, find and copy the Role ARN from the Summary section. It will look similar to:

    
    
    arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/BrowseAI-S3-Access-S3AccessRole-ABCDEFGH1234

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1511157224/28fd6caad577e8f9506c09613d7c/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image86ca.png?expires=1771547400&signature=b16b8808d96faa00a9d758f127914888df401342638c52fd116779a603de3692&req=dSUmF8h7moNdXfMW1HO4za2GDcAZpk5pkrcZUyq5Tz3m7u%2BOwPmswtpde1g2%0Akhi5%2FJvAo8jsgD5h%2BLk%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1511157224/28fd6caad577e8f9506c09613d7c/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image86ca.png?expires=1771547400&signature=b16b8808d96faa00a9d758f127914888df401342638c52fd116779a603de3692&req=dSUmF8h7moNdXfMW1HO4za2GDcAZpk5pkrcZUyq5Tz3m7u%2BOwPmswtpde1g2%0Akhi5%2FJvAo8jsgD5h%2BLk%3D%0A)

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1511158546/6752b5efb3621ef4bcbaf2ceb88e/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagec2b9.png?expires=1771547400&signature=707fec89157106f2e508d2282d7f765fc7cf5f2c06a70ff24cd4d68e49c16409&req=dSUmF8h7lYRbX%2FMW1HO4zYBNgeIJSRjO4%2B%2Fvlqq1EWDis4lO0nDHz%2Bg7kdC5%0AqdF%2FW9%2FJKzsGBryf9cc%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1511158546/6752b5efb3621ef4bcbaf2ceb88e/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagec2b9.png?expires=1771547400&signature=707fec89157106f2e508d2282d7f765fc7cf5f2c06a70ff24cd4d68e49c16409&req=dSUmF8h7lYRbX%2FMW1HO4zYBNgeIJSRjO4%2B%2Fvlqq1EWDis4lO0nDHz%2Bg7kdC5%0AqdF%2FW9%2FJKzsGBryf9cc%3D%0A)

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1511158978/e226cdae18f45334f298e72d37bf/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imageaf74.png?expires=1771547400&signature=0c990b92af76c8bac496a8b365dd3fc2536389c17c99bd52c8f06db9d159dd28&req=dSUmF8h7lYhYUfMW1HO4zZaSMVOSsAHboPzuk%2BhgEIOnB7aYh5pzS%2BudlBHz%0AFu0YKTpQyfckfz6nrug%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1511158978/e226cdae18f45334f298e72d37bf/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imageaf74.png?expires=1771547400&signature=0c990b92af76c8bac496a8b365dd3fc2536389c17c99bd52c8f06db9d159dd28&req=dSUmF8h7lYhYUfMW1HO4zZaSMVOSsAHboPzuk%2BhgEIOnB7aYh5pzS%2BudlBHz%0AFu0YKTpQyfckfz6nrug%3D%0A)

## Step 6: Connect to Browse AI

  1. In your Browse AI dashboard, select your robot and go to the "Integrate" tab.

  2. Select "AWS" from the integration options.

  3. Choose "\+ Add new S3 bucket" from the dropdown menu.

  4. Complete the form with these details:

    1. **AWS Region** : Your S3 bucket's region (e.g., us-east-1)

    2. **Role ARN** : Paste the ARN you copied

    3. **External ID** : Enter the same 20-character string you created earlier

    4. **Bucket Name** : Your S3 bucket name

    5. **Bucket Path** : The folder path you specified (if any)

  5. Click "Add" to complete the connection.

After the steps above are complete, when you attempt to export Tables, you
will have an option to send the exported files directly to your AWS S3 bucket.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1511160863/5497d441f75f28c931dd38f27f49/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagea8b1.png?expires=1771547400&signature=17548c1c8670bd1ee36af79bd5db35f52c8a74f5e15c51f87f9e20e24e09c689&req=dSUmF8h4nYlZWvMW1HO4zT%2B0Ed7OzFxjAZyEP%2FHEQg4%2FV31WpI%2BeOLE6Rj8I%0AMsblE6ycGyO%2BzKNgSwA%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1511160863/5497d441f75f28c931dd38f27f49/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagea8b1.png?expires=1771547400&signature=17548c1c8670bd1ee36af79bd5db35f52c8a74f5e15c51f87f9e20e24e09c689&req=dSUmF8h4nYlZWvMW1HO4zT%2B0Ed7OzFxjAZyEP%2FHEQg4%2FV31WpI%2BeOLE6Rj8I%0AMsblE6ycGyO%2BzKNgSwA%3D%0A)

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1511161434/002a90a23585b768a480306de445/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image9d91.png?expires=1771547400&signature=f67883cf254c036994636236d210ee38fdeb5e994c097b53b04ac506016b66de&req=dSUmF8h4nIVcXfMW1HO4zQKk8YSEMWGAzoRRzil420c32b%2F20kUgrfXOInu%2B%0A69XIQnnqB5MB5pc08IU%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1511161434/002a90a23585b768a480306de445/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image9d91.png?expires=1771547400&signature=f67883cf254c036994636236d210ee38fdeb5e994c097b53b04ac506016b66de&req=dSUmF8h4nIVcXfMW1HO4zQKk8YSEMWGAzoRRzil420c32b%2F20kUgrfXOInu%2B%0A69XIQnnqB5MB5pc08IU%3D%0A)

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1511162178/7d1e9b4940638f9b2b1aedd830f0/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagea6e5.png?expires=1771547400&signature=2fbf283be81233bb2fd7dcfe9677fbf3711705836d86f6860b6a290a8cd8c5ba&req=dSUmF8h4n4BYUfMW1HO4zVqxGgvl4dSD%2BQnjJ86UQKnlYKNJTRlf4gbGCQsu%0AvyEwwtK2BP9pIgBLsfw%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1511162178/7d1e9b4940638f9b2b1aedd830f0/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagea6e5.png?expires=1771547400&signature=2fbf283be81233bb2fd7dcfe9677fbf3711705836d86f6860b6a290a8cd8c5ba&req=dSUmF8h4n4BYUfMW1HO4zVqxGgvl4dSD%2BQnjJ86UQKnlYKNJTRlf4gbGCQsu%0AvyEwwtK2BP9pIgBLsfw%3D%0A)

# File naming convention

Once your AWS S3 integration is configured, when you export data from Tables,
you can choose to send it directly to your bucket. This section describes
where those files will be stored and what they will be named.

Every Table export has a unique ID. For every export, a directory will be
created under your bucket and optionally, under the folder path you chose
during setup. This directory will be named `export_{ISO 8601
timestamp}_{unique export ID}`.

Within this folder, there will be at least one file per exported tab. If the
file size is not large (for example, smaller than 100mb), the files will be
named `{tab name}.{format; csv or json}`. If the file size is large, it will
be chunked into multiple files with the naming convention of `{tab
name}.part{n}.{format; csv or json}`.

  * Example 1 (combined file with small size): `export_20250415T175912Z_32ffd473-4476-42bc-96bb-1857e4fa4add/Main.json`

  * Example 2 (combined file with large size): `export_20250415T175912Z_32ffd473-4476-42bc-96bb-1857e4fa4add/Main.part1.json` `export_20250415T175912Z_32ffd473-4476-42bc-96bb-1857e4fa4add/Main.part2.json` ...

Alternatively, if you choose to export a separate file per record, the files
will be named `{unique record ID}.json`.

  * Example 3 (separate files per record): `export_20250415T175912Z_32ffd473-4476-42bc-96bb-1857e4fa4add/0005541b-d61d-42d7-ba24-17600e0068e5.json`

# Security details

This setup uses AWS best practices to keep your data secure:

  * Browse AI receives only the minimum permissions needed to export data to your bucket.

  * The External ID provides an additional security layer, preventing unauthorized access.

  * You can restrict access to a specific folder within your bucket.

  * All permissions can be revoked at any time by deleting the CloudFormation stack.

# Troubleshooting

## **Stack creation fails** :

  * Verify you have sufficient IAM permissions in your AWS account.

  * Check that the bucket name is exactly correct.

  * Ensure the bucket exists in your AWS account.

## **Can 't find the IAM Role ARN**:

  * Check the "Resources" tab in your CloudFormation stack.

  * If the stack shows "CREATE_COMPLETE" but no resources appear, try refreshing the page.

## **Browse AI connection fails** :

  * Double-check that the External ID matches exactly.

  * Verify the bucket name and region are correct.

  * Ensure the IAM role hasn't been modified manually after creation.

# Next steps

After connecting your S3 bucket, you can:

  * Configure your robot to export its extracted data to your bucket.

  * Set up automated workflows in AWS to process the exported data.

  * Create backups or data transformations using AWS services. 

Need more help? Check out [AWS
documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/Welcome.html)
or reach out to our support team.

How to see what changed in a captured screenshot

# How to see what changed in a captured screenshot

Click on your image and use the slider to compare versions. Use the Highlight
Changes toggle to make it more clear.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 11 months ago

If you've got a robot that's been trained to capture a screenshot, and you'd
like to see what has changed since the previous screenshot, here are the
steps:

  * Click on the robot from your dashboard

  * Click on the **History** tab for your robot

  * Find the appropriate task from the list

  * Check your screenshot to see if it says "Changed"  
​

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1423524325/bbd48f49f05e91f6a2d39793a56c/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image4c00.png?expires=1771547400&signature=3e8a24315d33e9ac93e42e790933bfb8bbf2d8f371c1b9cdd532ed1c52d5aaa0&req=dSQlFcx8mYJdXPMW1HO4zYh7AUx6Vjfiv%2BTQTwKUJGurp%2FormatQIUiAbQdp%0AmrXV%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1423524325/bbd48f49f05e91f6a2d39793a56c/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image4c00.png?expires=1771547400&signature=3e8a24315d33e9ac93e42e790933bfb8bbf2d8f371c1b9cdd532ed1c52d5aaa0&req=dSQlFcx8mYJdXPMW1HO4zYh7AUx6Vjfiv%2BTQTwKUJGurp%2FormatQIUiAbQdp%0AmrXV%0A)

  * Click on your image to view a larger version

  * Use the slider to see the previous screenshot compared to this most recent  
​

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1423527703/be4174822d45244f611aa26c7633/changed-
screenshot.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27changed-
screenshot508f.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=282ab779835f6b1155d72c2cc6b36078b3850e833bec2a487a26fdbf8b95073d&req=dSQlFcx8moZfWvMW1HO4zQxZ0Cqxq%2F%2BcoKqOp1S5nQ7cYQVj102WI%2BRQ8gms%0Ax6%2FCPTKj6xTQASfV0WI%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1423527703/be4174822d45244f611aa26c7633/changed-
screenshot.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27changed-
screenshot508f.gif?expires=1771547400&signature=282ab779835f6b1155d72c2cc6b36078b3850e833bec2a487a26fdbf8b95073d&req=dSQlFcx8moZfWvMW1HO4zQxZ0Cqxq%2F%2BcoKqOp1S5nQ7cYQVj102WI%2BRQ8gms%0Ax6%2FCPTKj6xTQASfV0WI%3D%0A)

**Note:** There is a Highlight Changes toggle that you can use in order to get
more visibility into what the differences are between the two versions.

Using a captured screenshot in a Zapier action step

# Using a captured screenshot in a Zapier action step

When you've trained your robot to capture a screenshot, it's possible to pass
off that image file to action steps in your Zap.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 11 months ago

Some examples of what you might do with a screenshot:

  * Upload it to an app like Google Drive, Dropbox, or OneDrive

  * Send it to ChatGPT for analysis

  * Deliver it to a shared Slack channel that your team monitors

The screenshots will have a URL that begins with `https://prod-browseai-
captured-data` and the label will have a name that contains **Captured
Screenshots.**

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1434934208/4d14c78fd00cfacab8ecf5a46748/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image0cf6.png?expires=1771547400&signature=fd4b12593ccf989b38518a8779e50c8060ab5f17d0539631cfc55919fa1d088e&req=dSQkEsB9mYNfUfMW1HO4zW%2BX%2FVV0IFobqWKNFa1EKF2JN3C%2BbWETtHi%2BhDus%0AOq4qnWPN6qT53SY%2B1xQ%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1434934208/4d14c78fd00cfacab8ecf5a46748/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image0cf6.png?expires=1771547400&signature=fd4b12593ccf989b38518a8779e50c8060ab5f17d0539631cfc55919fa1d088e&req=dSQkEsB9mYNfUfMW1HO4zW%2BX%2FVV0IFobqWKNFa1EKF2JN3C%2BbWETtHi%2BhDus%0AOq4qnWPN6qT53SY%2B1xQ%3D%0A)

If there are multiple screenshots captured by your robot, there will be one
field per screenshot captured. Again, the URL will begin with `https://prod-
browseai-captured-data`.

In the screenshot below, notice that there are two file objects.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1434923292/fb1bedb1dd2244d98e20b8eb0601/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image7028.png?expires=1771547400&signature=1db38c967682705a86a49d1a8e41ae207941fcd0f51ae045b65ca4e4cc03ebfc&req=dSQkEsB8noNWW%2FMW1HO4zXPB95JgVzO99at0moClLJl3axZ6y9FqpxIXM%2FP8%0A9WR%2FHDR5pQ6jeqa9USU%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1434923292/fb1bedb1dd2244d98e20b8eb0601/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image7028.png?expires=1771547400&signature=1db38c967682705a86a49d1a8e41ae207941fcd0f51ae045b65ca4e4cc03ebfc&req=dSQkEsB8noNWW%2FMW1HO4zXPB95JgVzO99at0moClLJl3axZ6y9FqpxIXM%2FP8%0A9WR%2FHDR5pQ6jeqa9USU%3D%0A)

The names _" Screenshot 1"_ and _" Screenshot 2"_ will be replaced with
whatever you choose during the training of your robot.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1434926720/e48ec036ca765df3b0998ef008d4/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image75fb.png?expires=1771547400&signature=d5c803318f3b512f038ec6b3bf680bd1559e9209d33b609e72df0854ee2071e6&req=dSQkEsB8m4ZdWfMW1HO4zVNbVORjNo1KdcENR%2F5dpP3tVCU9v36fJ8FpWw2O%0ADaey81Xt0DVqEADMpVg%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1434926720/e48ec036ca765df3b0998ef008d4/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image75fb.png?expires=1771547400&signature=d5c803318f3b512f038ec6b3bf680bd1559e9209d33b609e72df0854ee2071e6&req=dSQkEsB8m4ZdWfMW1HO4zVNbVORjNo1KdcENR%2F5dpP3tVCU9v36fJ8FpWw2O%0ADaey81Xt0DVqEADMpVg%3D%0A)

**Note:** If your robot is capturing multiple screenshots, it's possible
you'll need to add an action step per screenshot in your app. In this example,
one Google Drive action uploads the first screenshot, and the next one uploads
the second screenshot.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1434932892/04bdcbb1f8aa4bc9b35ae03ba7fe/zapier-
multiple-screenshots.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27zapier-multiple-

How to handle pagination that has multiple clicks to load more items

# How to handle pagination that has multiple clicks to load more items

Browse AI can handle these complex pagination patterns by recording your
complete interaction sequence.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 3 months ago

Table of contents

Some websites require additional clicks or interactions before their standard
pagination becomes visible. This guide covers complex pagination scenarios
that go beyond the standard pagination settings.

# When to use this guide

This guide provides you an overview of how to manage pagination on a page
where your web page only shows pagination after you click an initial
element(s).

**Scenario**| **Solution**| **Example**  
---|---|---  
Regular "Load more" buttons| 📄 [Standard pagination
settings](10509840-capture-text-how-to-set-up-pagination-when-extracting-data-
from-a-list.html)| Social media feeds, product lists  
Infinite scroll| 📄 [Standard pagination settings](10509840-capture-text-how-
to-set-up-pagination-when-extracting-data-from-a-list.html)| Twitter,
Instagram feeds  
Next/Previous/Page numbers| 📄 [Standard pagination settings](10509840-capture-
text-how-to-set-up-pagination-when-extracting-data-from-a-list.html)| Search
results, blog posts  
Different pages for each section| [🔧 Use Workflows](10518596-workflows-how-to-
connect-robots-together-to-scrape-across-multiple-pages.html)| Category →
Products → Details  
Multiple URLs to paginate through| [🔧 Use Workflows](10518596-workflows-how-
to-connect-robots-together-to-scrape-across-multiple-pages.html)| Search
results with different filters  
Deep scraping (list → detail pages)| [🔧 Use Workflows](10518596-workflows-how-
to-connect-robots-together-to-scrape-across-multiple-pages.html)| Product
listings → Individual products  
Pagination hidden on same page| ⭐ **This guide**|  "Show all" reveals "Load
more" below  
Controls need enabling first| ⭐ **This guide**|  Toggle view to reveal
pagination  
JavaScript reveals pagination| ⭐ **This guide**|  Click to load content and
pagination  
  
# How to handle hidden pagination on a page

In this scenario, your website only shows pagination after you click an
initial element on a web page.

  1. Train your robot on the initial page

  2. Interact with the page to display the full list (click, scroll, select)

  3. Select Capture List on the displayed data

  4. Configure pagination settings (now visible):

     * Choose "Load more items" as type

     * Set your item count

     * Robot will handle the rest

### Example

A product results page shows 10 items with a "Show all 400 items" button. Only
after clicking this does the "Load more" pagination appear at the bottom.

Train your robot to click the reveal button first, then train the robot to
capture as a list and configure the pagination options:

  1. Start on the initial results page

  2. Click the reveal button ("Show all items", "View all results", "Expand list")

  3. Wait for the pagination controls to appear

  4. Now select Capture List on your data

  5. Configure pagination settings (now visible):

     * Choose "Load more items" as type

     * Set your item count

     * Robot will handle the rest

The robot learns: Reveal click → Then pagination

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1839156892/32a8c6adfc2aa7896862fca49f09/2-step-
pagination.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%272-step-
pagination5e20.gif?expires=1771546500&signature=7d635b5f82b40240bb9458f533419d71fec45e250f08c6864d4eaff41f187b06&req=dSgkH8h7m4lWW%2FMW1HO4zenDA%2BzV3IxgDOYMxpXM0qjEAARFe4aMPORWdxy5%0AqVndkEreDVY9GrGk6i8%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1839156892/32a8c6adfc2aa7896862fca49f09/2-step-
pagination.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%272-step-
pagination5e20.gif?expires=1771546500&signature=7d635b5f82b40240bb9458f533419d71fec45e250f08c6864d4eaff41f187b06&req=dSgkH8h7m4lWW%2FMW1HO4zenDA%2BzV3IxgDOYMxpXM0qjEAARFe4aMPORWdxy5%0AqVndkEreDVY9GrGk6i8%3D%0A)

## Common single-page reveal patterns

## Expandable sections with pagination

**Scenario** : FAQ page where each section expands and has "Show more" within
it

  1. Click to expand the section

  2. Initial items show within the expanded area

  3. "Load more" appears within that section

  4. Configure Capture List with pagination

  5. All happens without leaving the page

## Toggle views that enable pagination

**Scenario** : List/Grid toggle where only List view has pagination

  1. Click "List view" toggle

  2. Page reformats to list (same URL)

  3. Pagination controls now visible

  4. Set up Capture List with pagination

## JavaScript-revealed pagination

**Scenario** : "Show results" button that uses JavaScript to reveal both data
and pagination

  1. Click "Show results"

  2. JavaScript loads content below

  3. "Load more" button appears at bottom

  4. No page navigation occurs

  5. Configure extraction with pagination

* * *

Capture screenshots: How to train your robot to capture screenshots

# Capture screenshots: How to train your robot to capture screenshots

You can train a robot to scrape or monitor screenshots (full or partial) on
any web page.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 3 months ago

Table of contents

Screenshots help you monitor for visual changes on a web page. You can train a
robot to capture:

  * Full page screenshot.

  * Only the visible part of the page.

  * Specific areas or sections of a page. 

Once you approve your robot, you can monitor for visual changes. Screenshots
work best when you need to:

  * Monitor for visual changes.

  * Track layout modifications or design updates.

  * Capture images, charts, or graphics that appear on pages.

  * Document how a page looked at a specific time.

💡 You can train a single robot to capture a screenshot, capture '[Just
text](12703713-capture-text-how-to-extract-and-structure-specific-data-from-a-
page-using-just-text.html)', and extract data '[From a List](12703688-capture-
text-how-to-extract-data-from-a-list.html)' on a web page.

## **Step 1: Start training your robot**

To start training your robot, all you'll need is the URL you'd like to scrape
or monitor.

  1. From your Browse AI dashboard, click "Build New Robot".

  2. Select either:

    1. Extract structure data - if you'd like to scrape data from a web page

    2. Monitor site changes - if you want to create a web monitor.

  3. Enter the Origin URL you would like to scrape or monitor.

  4. Click Start Training Robot.

  5. Select Use Robot Studio and wait for your web page to load. 

## Step 2: Train your robot to capture a screenshot

  1. In the right sidebar, select 'Capture screenshot'

  2. Choose between:

    1. Selections: select a specific part of the page you'd like to screenshot. 

    2. Entire page: takes a screenshot of the entire page. 

    3. Visible part: takes a screenshot of the visible part of the page only. 

  3. Name your screenshot. 

  4. Click 'Finish' to finish recording your robot if you've captured all of the data you need, or keep capturing text or screenshots.

  5. Name your robot to run it, review the data and approve it. 

  6. From this screen you'll be able to access and download the screenshot you've captured.

### How to capture a 'selection' of the page

To capture a selection, move your mouse to the part of the page you'd like to
capture. You can capture, and name multiple selections to create a structured
visual output.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1837347126/c9499433a1c6c5cfd661f04427b5/CaptureSelection.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27CaptureSelectiond9b0.gif?expires=1771546500&signature=8a00439d188382c4eeabdeb1b708a9dbb08e4e9e2861d17b2eeec43adbbd78af&req=dSgkEcp6moBdX%2FMW1HO4zflZ8F8R%2BdX0MaojFhZRhPTpsxsyCjpdhyG3V49C%0AAfyTqJd%2FhH0tOvkWy4Y%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1837347126/c9499433a1c6c5cfd661f04427b5/CaptureSelection.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27CaptureSelectiond9b0.gif?expires=1771546500&signature=8a00439d188382c4eeabdeb1b708a9dbb08e4e9e2861d17b2eeec43adbbd78af&req=dSgkEcp6moBdX%2FMW1HO4zflZ8F8R%2BdX0MaojFhZRhPTpsxsyCjpdhyG3V49C%0AAfyTqJd%2FhH0tOvkWy4Y%3D%0A)

### How to capture the 'entire page'

When capturing the entire page, simply choose that option then give the
screenshot a name.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1837347099/cd5b00205eab2f9f41b05b19a79b/CapturePage.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27CapturePage1363.gif?expires=1771546500&signature=b774e6249df84c7b94cac3c5e4855fee0630a6da6f6015c7852456183a6cf8ef&req=dSgkEcp6moFWUPMW1HO4zQSVtcmuL1%2FP2MOr0JkM3umkWjsu2p%2F0O10qQ9mj%0AGNS3vDBrxmVmvajgFfE%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1837347099/cd5b00205eab2f9f41b05b19a79b/CapturePage.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27CapturePage1363.gif?expires=1771546500&signature=b774e6249df84c7b94cac3c5e4855fee0630a6da6f6015c7852456183a6cf8ef&req=dSgkEcp6moFWUPMW1HO4zQSVtcmuL1%2FP2MOr0JkM3umkWjsu2p%2F0O10qQ9mj%0AGNS3vDBrxmVmvajgFfE%3D%0A)

### How to capture the 'visible part' only

When capturing the entire visible part, choose that option then give the
screenshot a name. The robot will automatically capture the part of the page
that's visible.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1837347101/75b82c2639ae5687ecfb2df538b9/CaptureVisible.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27CaptureVisible8d62.gif?expires=1771546500&signature=ce2e7bceeb4e38c1456e1c6f920b3b1c41642bc25731a7e3669347f48e95a1b5&req=dSgkEcp6moBfWPMW1HO4zbeP2%2Fo123%2FxbWNmI7Wvi6k15Mv2fKH7y%2FNQJ%2FBF%0Ay1av4T3wY582J5nfONw%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1837347101/75b82c2639ae5687ecfb2df538b9/CaptureVisible.gif_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27CaptureVisible8d62.gif?expires=1771546500&signature=ce2e7bceeb4e38c1456e1c6f920b3b1c41642bc25731a7e3669347f48e95a1b5&req=dSgkEcp6moBfWPMW1HO4zbeP2%2Fo123%2FxbWNmI7Wvi6k15Mv2fKH7y%2FNQJ%2FBF%0Ay1av4T3wY582J5nfONw%3D%0A)

## How do I monitor this web page for visual changes?

Once you approve your robot you can [add a monitor](10453923-how-to-use-
monitors-to-automate-data-extraction.html) to automatically check for visual
changes.

  1. Select your robot in the Dashboard and click Monitor.

  2. Select Create New Monitor and configure:

    1. How often you'd like the robot to check for changes (minute, hour, week, month).

    2. The size of change (any change, small change, medium change, major change, gigantic change). 

    3. Where you'd like to be notified of these changes. 

## How do I view or download historical screenshots I've captured?

Once your robot is approved, you can access the captured screenshots.

  1. Select your robot in the Dashboard.

  2. Click the History tab.

  3. Click into the specific task.

  4. From here you'll be able to see all of the screenshots you've trained your robot to capture.

[arcade id="kzgymX4Q9uKHwGmd1lD5" title="How to capture and monitor
screenshots" padding-bottom="61.5339%"]

Displaying images directly in your Google Sheets

# Displaying images directly in your Google Sheets

Turning scraped image URLs into visuals

![Kimber
avatar](../../../static.intercomassets.com/avatars/8022949/square_128/hello-1740080738.png)

Written by Kimber

Updated over 5 months ago

Table of contents

This guide will walk you through the process of displaying the images directly
within your spreadsheets.

# Why?

This can help transform your list of image URLs into a visually rich dataset
by displaying the actual pictures right inside your Google Sheet.

# How to display images from URLs in Google Sheets

## Step 1: Build your robot to extract the image URLs from your target
website.

See: **[How to build a robot using Robot Studio](10496382-how-to-train-a-
robot-to-scrape-or-monitor-data.html)**

  * During robot building, capture the image URL of the images you want to capture. Here's an example of a task result:

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1514669644/bb9daff2a570f4dd346cdeeed1e5/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imageb925.png?expires=1771547400&signature=e01a14dacda1a75a582af296debe879b3848ad4d9bc74bcf4e61e5b573138666&req=dSUmEs94lIdbXfMW1HO4zeIfDGT6hqJiSUG3E1gXD%2FOIolu1Gt0LF5PWyodf%0AKCIA%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1514669644/bb9daff2a570f4dd346cdeeed1e5/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imageb925.png?expires=1771547400&signature=e01a14dacda1a75a582af296debe879b3848ad4d9bc74bcf4e61e5b573138666&req=dSUmEs94lIdbXfMW1HO4zeIfDGT6hqJiSUG3E1gXD%2FOIolu1Gt0LF5PWyodf%0AKCIA%0A)

## Step 2: Integrate the robot with Google Sheets to automatically populate
your spreadsheet with the scraped data

**See:[How to sync robot data with Google Sheets](10535898-how-to-sync-
scraped-data-with-google-sheets.html)**

  * If you see the menu tabs (including Integrate tab) being greyed out, make sure to [approve your robot](10518718-how-to-approve-a-robot.html) to enable the rest of the menu. 

  * Note: Your robot don't retroactively append all previously extracted data the moment you integrate your robot to Google Sheets, so you will need to run another task for the syncing to happen after integrating your robot.

## Step 3: Run a task to sync your data

  * After integrating your robot <-> Google Sheets, run a task by clicking on the purple Run task button, found on the Run Task tab:

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1514687688/834c96aabfb1a7e9bb744780f3a4/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagebad8.png?expires=1771547400&signature=7683241baf02fd812922e597454954ce181bc77b95d3debe8894197327c5603b&req=dSUmEs92modXUfMW1HO4zRlWehKJ76LWftMlnh83qlmqjHjl4Gxi0VPjbxSH%0A%2Ft2v%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1514687688/834c96aabfb1a7e9bb744780f3a4/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imagebad8.png?expires=1771547400&signature=7683241baf02fd812922e597454954ce181bc77b95d3debe8894197327c5603b&req=dSUmEs92modXUfMW1HO4zRlWehKJ76LWftMlnh83qlmqjHjl4Gxi0VPjbxSH%0A%2Ft2v%0A)

## Step 4: Locate the column containing the image URLs

  * Open your Google Sheet and identify the column containing the scraped image URLs. Here's an example: 

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1514710476/11595f68910ef39e7d53ef48dfe6/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imageafe0.png?expires=1771547400&signature=102ebbaf5780b22ccd5ed8ae074a8acbab2caf17b398f73fc62525fc8fa44a10&req=dSUmEs5%2FnYVYX%2FMW1HO4zSLT4pVlgWxi5yow6Ydd3RlQKXjhCP7XdvL6mSFU%0AWLBu%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1514710476/11595f68910ef39e7d53ef48dfe6/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27imageafe0.png?expires=1771547400&signature=102ebbaf5780b22ccd5ed8ae074a8acbab2caf17b398f73fc62525fc8fa44a10&req=dSUmEs5%2FnYVYX%2FMW1HO4zSLT4pVlgWxi5yow6Ydd3RlQKXjhCP7XdvL6mSFU%0AWLBu%0A)

## Step 5: Adding a formula using the `IMAGE` function

You can either:

  1. #### Display all images in a blank column

     * Create a new (blank) column wherever you want your images to be displayed in, and put a header name. 

     * For instance, in this screenshot, the blank column was made right beside the product name: 

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1514722278/1b32d6eda304d12c2057b47f9d44/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image3345.png?expires=1771547400&signature=99356074c4a1235e5b7802f239aa359c26d3a2922e94cae687b992494c10957e&req=dSUmEs58n4NYUfMW1HO4zbUK3wl7Gxffd6QF6cL1taYzJd8BUeY6SuzQvVcw%0AYqYG%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1514722278/1b32d6eda304d12c2057b47f9d44/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image3345.png?expires=1771547400&signature=99356074c4a1235e5b7802f239aa359c26d3a2922e94cae687b992494c10957e&req=dSUmEs58n4NYUfMW1HO4zbUK3wl7Gxffd6QF6cL1taYzJd8BUeY6SuzQvVcw%0AYqYG%0A)

     * In the first cell of the new column (e.g., the cell directly below the header), enter the following formula:

`=ARRAYFORMULA(IF(O2:O<>"", IMAGE(O2:O), ))`

       * `ARRAYFORMULA` allows the formula to apply to the entire column.

       * `IF(O2:O<>"", ... , )` checks if the corresponding range of cells in the image URL column is not empty.

       * `IMAGE(O2:O)` fetches and displays the image from the URL in the corresponding range of cells.

       * `O2:O` is the range of cells containing your image URLs.

     * Make sure to adjust the corresponding cell range accordingly.​

  2. #### Display images on specific cells 

     * If you only want to display images from specific cells, you can use the `IMAGE` function directly.

     * In the cell where you want to display the image, enter:   
​`=IMAGE(O2)`

     * Replace `O2` with the cell containing the image URL you want to display.

     * You can repeat this for other specific cells.  
​

## Step 6: Allow access when prompted by Google Sheet

  * After you enter the formula (whether for a whole column or a single cell), Google Sheets will need permission to get the images from the internet using the URLs. You'll see a prompt asking you to allow this connection. This is normal and required for the images to show up:

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1514733622/74cc8f6712c80704bae0a65ac7d5/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image29d7.png?expires=1771547400&signature=409e559c9a214330782e716cfaf0b5bdb1f73d8f974e948d14df926d52264e3d&req=dSUmEs59noddW%2FMW1HO4zfxKLFyTdB3eOKgBmvERWH3Dt53ldwjNiVaweXks%0AJpTl%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1514733622/74cc8f6712c80704bae0a65ac7d5/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image29d7.png?expires=1771547400&signature=409e559c9a214330782e716cfaf0b5bdb1f73d8f974e948d14df926d52264e3d&req=dSUmEs59noddW%2FMW1HO4zfxKLFyTdB3eOKgBmvERWH3Dt53ldwjNiVaweXks%0AJpTl%0A)

## Step 7: Image displaying in your spreadsheet

  * After allowing access, you should start to see the images loading into your previously empty column. Here are some samples:

    * Displaying all images: 

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1514743100/62a53309ec58b18328b0792dd734/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image1d28.png?expires=1771547400&signature=b6eece02674ce36bb0a78140ea5e36cfca06882b61573b3cf486a6d73db119d5&req=dSUmEs56noBfWfMW1HO4zdb%2BsHgEqQtpBsdAWmc4KT9dCGp66%2FYf3VPP1rb1%0AhdFx%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1514743100/62a53309ec58b18328b0792dd734/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image1d28.png?expires=1771547400&signature=b6eece02674ce36bb0a78140ea5e36cfca06882b61573b3cf486a6d73db119d5&req=dSUmEs56noBfWfMW1HO4zdb%2BsHgEqQtpBsdAWmc4KT9dCGp66%2FYf3VPP1rb1%0AhdFx%0A)

    * Displaying specific images: 

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1514746658/7428c7fdbf25095c3031371c62e3/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image9437.png?expires=1771547400&signature=36c0c314b5acb9912059fe102ec249a987041d1751e30d5f9e681ed89d67b6a1&req=dSUmEs56m4daUfMW1HO4zaEARqLYAhkMXwfVpM8yCRu0V3dleg392ox%2F4d27%0AxEHn%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1514746658/7428c7fdbf25095c3031371c62e3/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image9437.png?expires=1771547400&signature=36c0c314b5acb9912059fe102ec249a987041d1751e30d5f9e681ed89d67b6a1&req=dSUmEs56m4daUfMW1HO4zaEARqLYAhkMXwfVpM8yCRu0V3dleg392ox%2F4d27%0AxEHn%0A)

* * *

# Troubleshooting common issues

### **Image not loading** :

This might happen if the URL is broken, misspelled, or no longer points to a
valid image. Check the URL in a web browser to ensure it works.

### Error displayed instead of image:

This likely means the URL is invalid or the `IMAGE` function encountered a
problem fetching it. Verify the URL to make sure it works.

### Images are too big or too small:

Google Sheets tries to fit images within the cell. You can manually adjust the
height of the rows and the width of the columns to change how the images are
displayed.

# Things to consider

  * Depending on the size and number of images, it might take a few moments for them to load.

  * Google Sheets might resize images to fit the cell. You can adjust the row height and column width to better display the images.

  * Make sure that the scraped URLs are valid and point to accessible images. If a URL is broken, the formulas will display an error.

  * Displaying a large number of images can affect the performance of your Google Sheet.

  * Be mindful of the source of the images and ensure they are from trusted websites.

Need more help? Feel free to reach out to our support team.

How to extract data on a page that has CAPTCHAs

# How to extract data on a page that has CAPTCHAs

When your robots encounter CAPTCHAs during extraction, Browse AI can solve
supported verification types automatically.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 3 months ago

Table of contents

Browse AI offers support for handling common CAPTCHA systems including:

  * **reCAPTCHA** : Google's widely implemented security verification system.

  * **hCAPTCHA** : An alternative CAPTCHA service growing in popularity.

☎️ This feature needs to be enabled by our support team and is only available
for paid plans. To enable, reach out to support:

  * Request the "Solve CAPTCHA" be added.

  * Specify which robots need this feature.

  * Detail the type and details of the CAPTCHA.

## Identifying CAPTCHA types on your target site

Before setting up extraction, check what type of CAPTCHA you're dealing with:

**CAPTCHA type**| **What you 'll see**| **Key identifiers**| **Browse AI
Support**  
---|---|---|---  
reCAPTCHA v2| Checkbox "I'm not a robot"| Google branding, checkbox interface|
✅ Supported  
reCAPTCHA v3| No visible challenge| Runs invisibly in background| ✅ Supported  
reCAPTCHA Enterprise| Similar to v2 or invisible| Google branding when
visible| ✅ Supported  
hCAPTCHA| Checkbox "I am human"| Blue/purple branding, privacy messaging| ✅
Supported  
FunCaptcha| Rotating images/puzzles| Game-like challenges| ❌ Not supported  
Text CAPTCHAs| Distorted text to type| Wavy or obscured letters/numbers| ❌ Not
supported  
Math CAPTCHAs| Simple math problem| "What is 5 + 3?"| ❌ Not supported  
Custom CAPTCHAs| Varies widely| Site-specific puzzles or questions| ❌ Not
supported  
  
## Quick identification methods

**Visual check:**

  1. Look at the CAPTCHA box corners for branding

  2. Note the challenge type (checkbox, images, invisible)

  3. Check for "reCAPTCHA" or "hCAPTCHA" text

**Technical check (view page source):**

  * `google.com/recaptcha` or `gstatic.com/recaptcha` → reCAPTCHA ✅

  * `hcaptcha.com` → hCAPTCHA ✅

  * `funcaptcha` or `arkoselabs` → FunCaptcha ❌

  * Custom JavaScript validation → Custom CAPTCHA ❌

## How captcha handling works

Our CAPTCHA handling capabilities are specifically designed for **production
environments** where data extraction takes place. This means:

  * CAPTCHA solving is available during robot execution in robot workers.

  * The feature is not currently available during the robot creation process in Robot Studio.

  * This implementation prioritizes solving CAPTCHAs where it matters most - during actual data collection.

## Enabling captcha solving capabilities

To utilize CAPTCHA solving features for your robots:

  1. Contact Browse AI Support to request the "Solve CAPTCHA" feature be enabled for your specific robots.

  2. Our team will configure your robots with the appropriate CAPTCHA handling capabilities.

  3. Once enabled, your robots will be able to navigate through supported CAPTCHA challenges during execution.

Understanding subscription changes

# Understanding subscription changes

We've made some updates to simplify how subscription upgrades or downgrades
are handled.

![Kimber
avatar](../../../static.intercomassets.com/avatars/8022949/square_128/hello-1740080738.png)

Written by Kimber

Updated over 7 months ago

Our goal is to make the process more straightforward and ensure you get the
most value from your subscription. Previously, upgrading or downgrading
involved complex proration calculations based on remaining time and unused
credits. This process has been updated to be much clearer.

Now, when you decide to change your Browse AI subscription, here's exactly
what happens:

  * You will be charged the _full price_ of your new plan right away, regardless of whether you upgrade or downgrade. 

  * Crucially, any credits you had remaining from your previous plan are _not_ lost. These remaining credits are added to the full allowance of credits included in your _new_ plan for the current billing cycle.

    * This means for your initial period on the new plan, you will have a combined, larger pool of credits available.

  * Your billing date will reset to the date you changed your subscription plan. Your next renewal date will be exactly one month or one year (depending on your billing frequency) from this new date.

  * At the beginning of your second billing cycle on the new plan (i.e., on your first renewal date after upgrading or downgrading), your credit balance will reset to the standard monthly or annual credit allocation provided by your new plan.

To illustrate this with a couple of examples...

When upgrading:

  * Suppose you are on the Personal monthly plan (which includes 2,000 credits per month) and have 500 credits remaining.

  * You decide to upgrade to the Professional plan (which includes 5,000 credits per month).

  * Upon upgrading, you are immediately charged the full price for the Professional plan.

  * Your remaining 500 credits from the Personal plan are added to the 5,000 credits of the Professional plan.

  * For your first billing cycle on the Professional plan, you will have a total of 5,500 credits available (5,000 new credits + 500 carried-over credits).

  * Your billing date is now the date of your upgrade. 

  * When your subscription next renews (original billing cycle + one month later), your credit balance will reset to the standard Professional plan amount of 5,000 credits.

When downgrading:

  * Suppose you are on the Professional monthly plan (which includes 10,000 credits) and you are halfway through your billing cycle, with the whole 10,000 credits still unused.

  * You decide to downgrade to the Personal plan (which includes 2,000 credits per month).

  * Upon downgrading, you are immediately charged the full price for your new Personal plan.

  * Your remaining 10,000 credits from the Professional plan are added to the 2,000 credits of the Personal plan. 

  * For your first billing cycle on the Personal plan, you will have a total of 12,000 credits available (2,000 new credits + 10,000 carried-over credits). 

  * Your billing date is now the date of your downgrade. 

  * When your subscription next renews (original billing cycle + one month later), your credit balance will reset to the standard Personal plan amount of 2,000 credits.

If you have questions about this process or anything else regarding your
subscription, please reach out to our support team – we're here to help!

* * *

How do plans and pricing work?

# How do plans and pricing work?

Learn about websites, robots, users, credits and more!

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 8 months ago

Table of contents

If you're looking at our pricing page and wondering which plan is right for
you, or are unclear about some of the included features, this article will
help.

This article is about **current** pricing and does cover legacy plans. You
will know if you're on a legacy plan by checking your Billing page and looking
for the word "Legacy" in the plan name.

# Browse AI plans

There are 3 paid plans available, and also the free plan:

  * Free

  * Personal

  * Professional

  * Premium

## Free

* * *

 _Great for getting started. For a single website, create as many robots as
you want. With 50 credits you can extract 500 rows of data. Invite other users
to your workspace to try it out. Access to all platform features (monitors,
workflows, integrations, etc)._

  * **Credits:** 50 monthly 

  * **Robots:** unlimited

  * **Websites:** 2

  * **Users:** 3

## Personal

* * *

 _The Personal plan gives you unlimited robots with a simple, straightforward
pricing structure. You 'll get 5 websites included and can collaborate with up
to 3 team members. With annual billing, you'll save 20% and get all your
credits upfront so you can extract all the data you need from day one._

### Annual 12k credits: $19 per month (billed annually at $228)

  * **Credits:** 12,000 annually, all credits upfront

  * **Robots:** unlimited

  * **Websites:** 5

  * **Users:** 3

### Annual 24k credits: $38 per month (billed annually at $456)

  * **Credits:** 24,000 annually, all credits upfront

  * **Robots:** unlimited

  * **Websites:** 5

  * **Users:** 3

**Add-ons**  
On annual plans, additional websites are $4/month, paid annually. Extra
credits are $0.024 per credit (minimum top-up is 1,000 credits).

### Monthly 2k credits: $48 per month

  * **Credits:** 2,000 monthly

  * **Robots:** unlimited

  * **Websites:** 5

  * **Users:** 3

**Add-ons**  
On the monthly plan, additional websites are $5/month, paid annually. Extra
credits are $0.030 per credit (minimum top-up is 1,000 credits).

## Professional

* * *

 _Our Professional plan is perfect for your specific project. You 'll get
unlimited robots, 10 websites, and 10 team members included. Plus, you'll have
priority email support to ensure your project stays on track. The monthly
option gives you flexibility, and you can always add more websites or credits
as needed._

### Annual 60k credits: $69 per month (billed annually at $828)

  * **Credits:** 60,000 annually, all credits upfront

  * **Robots:** unlimited

  * **Websites:** 10

  * **Users:** 10

### Annual 120k credits: $129 per month (billed annually at $1,548)

  * **Credits:** 120,000 annually, all credits upfront

  * **Robots:** unlimited

  * **Websites:** 10

  * **Users:** 10

### Annual 240k credits: $239 per month (billed annually at $2,868)

  * **Credits:** 240,000 annually, all credits upfront

  * **Robots:** unlimited

  * **Websites:** 10

  * **Users:** 10

### Annual 360k credits: $319 per month (billed annually at $3,828)

  * **Credits:** 360,000 annually, all credits upfront

  * **Robots:** unlimited

  * **Websites:** 10

  * **Users:** 10

**Add-ons**  
On annual plans, additional websites at $2.40/month paid annually. Extra
credits are $0.017 to $0.013 per credit (minimum top-up is 1,000 credits).

### Monthly 5k credits: $87 per month

  * **Credits:** 5,000 credits monthly

  * **Robots:** unlimited

  * **Websites:** 10

  * **Users:** 10

### Monthly 10k credits: $162 per month

  * **Credits:** 10,000 credits monthly

  * **Robots:** unlimited

  * **Websites:** 10

  * **Users:** 10

### Monthly 20k credits: $299 per month

  * **Credits:** 20,000 credits monthly

  * **Robots:** unlimited

  * **Websites:** 10

  * **Users:** 10

### Monthly 30k credits: $399 per month

  * **Credits:** 30,000 credits monthly

  * **Robots:** unlimited

  * **Websites:** 10

  * **Users:** 10

**Add-ons**  
On monthly plans, additional websites are $3/month, paid annually. Extra
credits are $0.022 to $0.017 per credit (minimum top-up is 1,000 credits).

## Premium

* * *

**The Premium plan is only available with an annual contract.**

_Our Premium plan is designed for businesses that need a fully managed
solution. You 'll get a dedicated account manager, custom limits for websites
and users, and full service onboarding. We'll handle the data pipeline setup,
maintenance, and even custom data transformations. This ensures you get
reliable, accurate data without the operational stress._

### Annual 600k+ credits: Starting at $500 per month, billed annually

  * **Credits:** 600,000+ annually, all credits upfront

  * **Robots:** unlimited

  * **Websites:** custom

  * **Users:** custom

We work with you to create a customized solution including:

  * Fully managed onboarding and set up

  * Data transformations

  * Exclusive scale discounts

  * Data management

  * Dedicated Account Manager

# FAQs

## Q. What does it mean when a plan says X number of websites?

This means that for the allotted number of websites on your plan, you can
build an unlimited number of robots. The personal plan, for example, gives you
5 websites. That means different websites, not different pages on the same
website.

### Examples

**Each of the bullet points below represent a _single_ website**

All of these live on the same core domain, and are _subpages_ on that one
website.

  * website.com, website.com/products, and website.com/pricing

  * site.net, site.net/properties/ny, site.net/agents/la, site.net/buyers

  * store.co/products, store.co/sales, store.co/events, store.co/sellers

Across **all** the bullet points, this would be 3 different websites:
website.com, site.net, and store.co.

**Each of the bullet points ALSO below represent _a single_ websites**

The pages are ultimately _subdomains_ , but they share the same core domain.

  * company.io/pricing, help.company.io, jobs.company.io, press.company.io

  * us.store.com, ca.store.com, uk.store.com, mx.store.com

  * california.homes.co, texas.homes.co, newyork.homes.co

Across **all** the bullet points, this would be 3 different websites:
company.io, store.com, and homes.co.

**Note:** If you're scraping similar pages on a single website (this includes
subdomains of a site) you likely only need to create a single robot since the
structure/layout are the same. [Learn more](10478637-do-i-need-to-train-a-
robot-for-each-web-page.html).

## Q. How do add-on websites work?

You can add more websites to your plan, rather than upgrade the entire plan.
This provides convenience and flexibility.

  * **Free:** add-on websites N/A

  * **Personal:** On annual plans, additional websites are $4/month, paid annually. On monthly plans, additional websites are $5/month, paid annually. 

  * **Professional:** On annual plans, additional websites at $2.40/month paid annually. On monthly plans, additional websites are $3/month, paid annually.

  * **Premium:** Users on this plan can customize this.

## Q. How do add-on credits work?

You can add more credits (these are one-time and not recurring) to your plan,
rather than upgrade the entire plan. This provides convenience and
flexibility.

  * **Free:** add-on credits N/A

  * **Personal:** On annual plans, extra credits are $0.024 per credit. On monthly plans, extra credits are $0.030 per credit (minimum top-up is 1,000 credits).

  * **Professional:** On annual plans, extra credits are $0.022 to $0.017 per credit. On monthly plans, extra credits are $0.030 per credit (minimum top-up is 1,000 credits).

  * **Premium:** Users on this plan can customize this.

## Q. How do unlimited robots relate to the number of websites?

As the word "unlimited" suggests, there is no limit to the number of robots
you can train on a single website. If you want to scrape 100 different pages
on competitor.com then you only need a single website.

As mentioned above, if you want to scrape competitor.com, help.competitor.com
and blog.competitor.com that would still be considered just **one website**.

## Q. Can I delete all the robots from one website, and then open up a "slot"
to scrape a different website?

Yes, that's how it works. If your plan gives you 5 websites to scrape, and you
delete all of the robots for one of those websites, you will regain that
"slot" and be able to scrape a new website in its place.

**Note:** Doing so will permanently delete the robots and if you want to re-
scrape that particular website, you will have to re-train a robot(s).

## Q. Why would I add users to a workspace, and what can they do?

Inviting someone to a workspace means they can collaborate with you.

Workspace roles determine what actions members can take:

### Owner:

  * Full control over workspace settings

  * Manage members and permissions

  * Transfer ownership

  * Access all robots and API settings

### Admin:

  * Manage workspace settings

  * Add or remove members

  * Configure robots and API settings

  * Cannot transfer ownership

### Member:

  * Build and run robots

  * Access shared workspace data

  * Cannot modify workspace settings

  * Cannot add new members 

**Note:** On the Free and Personal plans you can invite 2 extra users, for a
total of 3 users (including you, as the workspace owner). On the Professional
plan, you can invite 9 extra users, for a total of 10 users.

[Learn more about workspaces.](10488330-how-to-use-workspaces.html)

## Q. For annual plans, is there a monthly limit on credits I can use?

No. For annual plans, you get all of the credits upfront. You can use them
however you see fit during the 12 months of your subscription.

### Examples

With the Annual Professional plan with 60k credits, you could:

  * Use 50,000 credits in the first week, no credits for the next 6 months, then the remaining 10,000 credits in a single day.

  * Use 3,000 in the first month, 12,000 two months later, then 5,000/month for the next 9 months.

  * Use 5,000 credits per month for the first 6 months, then 10,000 credits per month for the next 3 months, and 0 credits in the remaining 3 months.

You get the idea. Unlike monthly plans, where the limit for the plan above
(for example) would be 5,000 credits per month, annual plans give you the
entire year's worth of credits for you to "spend" as you'd like.

What are options for managed web data extraction services?

# What are options for managed web data extraction services?

Browse AI offers web scraping and monitoring services for custom and scaled
implementations.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 5 months ago

Table of contents

When your business needs reliable web data but lacks the time or technical
resources to manage extraction yourself, Browse AI offers managed services
that handle everything from robot creation to data delivery.

## What are Browse AI's managed services?

Browse AI's managed services provide fully managed web data scraping and
monitoring where our team takes care of all aspects of your data needs.
Instead of building and maintaining robots yourself, our experts design,
build, monitor, and manage your entire data workflow.

With managed services, our team:

  * Designs and builds custom web scrapers for your specific requirements.

  * Manages the entire web scraping process from setup to delivery.

  * Monitors and maintains robots to ensure reliable data collection.

  * Offers custom data post processing.

  * Integrates the scraped data with your existing systems.

  * Provides ongoing technical support.

## Who uses Browse AI managed services?

Managed services work best for businesses that:

  * Depend on web data for critical daily operations

  * Need complex data from multiple sources or workflows

  * Require custom data transformation or formatting

  * Want to scale extraction without managing infrastructure

  * Lack technical resources to handle web scraping internally

  * Need guaranteed reliability and uptime for their data

Common use cases include:

  * E-commerce companies tracking competitor pricing across thousands of products

  * Real estate firms monitoring property listings from multiple platforms

  * Financial services aggregating data from various sources

  * Marketing agencies collecting performance data for client reporting

  * Recruitment firms tracking job postings across multiple boards

## What's included in Browse AI managed services?

### Custom robot development

Our team analyzes your requirements and builds extraction robots tailored to
your exact needs. This includes complex multi-step workflows, handling dynamic
content, and extracting data that standard tools can't access.

### Complete extraction management

We handle all aspects of running your robots including scheduling, monitoring,
error handling, and recovery. Your data extraction runs automatically without
any manual intervention needed from your team.

### Data quality assurance

We build your web scraping solution on our AI-powered platform which
automatically detects and adapts to website changes. We also execute and
design custom workflows to ensure your data is accurate.

### Data transformation and delivery

Raw web data often needs processing before it's useful. We clean, format,
structure, and enrich your data according to your specifications, then deliver
it exactly how you need it.

### Custom integration

Our team connects your extracted data directly with your existing tools and
systems including:

  * Databases and data warehouses

  * Business intelligence platforms

  * CRM and ERP systems

  * Custom APIs

  * Cloud storage services

  * Spreadsheets and reporting tools

### Dedicated support

Managed services include priority support with dedicated account management.
Our team works directly with you to ensure your data extraction meets your
business needs.

## How are managed services different from self-service?

### Self-service Browse AI

With our self-service plans (Free, Personal, and Professional), you:

  * Build and train your own robots using our point-and-click interface

  * Manage robot scheduling and monitoring yourself

  * Handle any maintenance when websites change

  * Export data using our standard integrations

  * Access email support based on your plan level

### Managed services (Premium plan)

With managed services, Browse AI's team:

  * Builds custom robots based on your requirements

  * Handles all technical aspects of extraction

  * Maintains robots proactively as websites change

  * Transforms data to your exact specifications

  * Provides custom integration with your systems

  * Offers dedicated support and account management

## What types of projects need managed services?

### High-volume extraction

When you need to extract data from thousands of URLs daily or monitor large
numbers of items continuously, managed services ensure reliable delivery at
scale.

### Complex workflows

Projects requiring multiple extraction steps, or handling of dynamic content.

### Business-critical data

When extracted data directly impacts your operations, revenue, or customer
service, managed services provide the reliability and support you need.

### Custom requirements

If you need specific data formatting, complex transformation logic, or
integration with systems, our team can build custom solutions.

## How does pricing work?

Browse AI managed services are available through our Premium plan, which
starts at $500 per month.

Pricing depends on:

  * Volume of data extracted

  * Complexity of extraction requirements

  * Number of sources and robots needed

  * Level of customization required

  * Integration requirements

[Contact our sales team](https://www.browse.ai/service) for a custom quote
based on your specific needs.

## Getting started with managed services

### 1\. Initial consultation

Schedule a call with our team to discuss your data needs, including sources,
volume, frequency, and how you'll use the data.

### 2\. Requirements analysis

We analyze your requirements and provide a detailed proposal including
timeline, deliverables, and pricing.

### 3\. Robot development

Our team builds and tests your custom extraction robots, ensuring they capture
exactly the data you need.

### 4\. Testing and validation

You review initial data samples to confirm accuracy and format before full
production begins.

### 5\. Production launch

Your robots begin running on schedule, delivering clean, formatted data to
your systems.

### 6\. Ongoing optimization

We continuously monitor performance, handle any issues, and optimize
extraction as your needs evolve.

## When to choose managed services vs. self-service

Choose **managed services** when you:

  * Need guaranteed reliability for business-critical data

  * Require complex extraction logic or custom workflows

  * Want to completely outsource web scraping operations

  * Need custom data transformation or system integration

  * Lack technical resources to manage extraction

Choose **self-service** when you:

  * Have simple extraction needs from standard websites

  * Want direct control over robot configuration

  * Have technical team members who can manage robots

  * Need occasional data rather than continuous extraction

  * Are working with a limited budget

* * *

Does Browse AI support websites that change frequently?

# Does Browse AI support websites that change frequently?

Yes, Browse AI includes AI-powered change detection and adaptation features
designed to help maintain data extraction when websites update their layouts
or structure.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 5 months ago

Table of contents

**Browse AI 's is an AI scraper that is designed to adapt and maintain data
acuracy when websites change. This includes:**

  * **AI-powered change monitoring** \- Detects website structure changes

  * **Automatic retries** \- Built-in retry logic for failed extractions

  * **Change notifications** \- Alerts when robots need attention

  * **Human behavior simulation** \- Mimics natural browsing patterns

Browse AI works with many dynamic sites like e-commerce platforms, news
websites, and business directories, though success varies by website
complexity and security measures.

## How does Browse AI handle website changes?

**Browse AI 's system includes several features to help with changing
websites:**

  1. **Change monitoring** \- The platform monitors for website structure changes

  2. **Automatic retries** \- Built-in retry mechanisms for temporary failures

  3. **Rate limiting and scaling** \- Intelligent request management

  4. **Proxy management** \- Built-in proxy rotation

  5. **Alert detection** \- Notifications when issues occur

**When websites change:**

  * You may receive failure notifications if robots stop working properly

  * The system provides success rates for each robot in your usage reports

  * Browse AI offers guidance for improving robot performance

  * Some changes may require retraining robots or creating new ones

**Important note:** While Browse AI includes AI-powered features to help with
website changes, significant structural changes may still require manual
intervention or robot adjustments.

## What should I expect when my target website changes?

**When Browse AI detects potential issues:**

  1. **Failure notifications** \- You'll receive alerts about robot failures

  2. **Performance reports** \- Success rates are tracked in your usage reports

  3. **Guidance provided** \- Browse AI suggests improvements for robot performance

  4. **Support available** \- Contact support for help with persistent issues

**Types of notifications you may receive:**

  * Individual failure notifications (default for new users)

  * Daily, weekly, or monthly failure digests

  * Batched notifications if you have high failure volumes (12+ per month)

  * Success rate summaries in usage reports

**Response options:**

  * Review failure notifications for specific issues

  * Use provided links to improve robot performance

  * Retrain robots if necessary

  * Contact support for complex issues

## How can I improve Browse AI's performance on changing websites?

**Best practices for dynamic website extraction:**

**During robot setup:**

  * Use Browse AI's recommended datasets feature during creation

  * Select multiple example elements when training robots

  * Test robots thoroughly before deploying monitoring

  * Avoid interacting with cookie banners during training when possible

**Ongoing monitoring:**

  * Set appropriate monitoring frequencies based on your needs

  * Review failure notifications promptly when they occur

  * Check usage reports regularly to monitor success rates

  * Use session cookies for logged-in sites when possible

**When issues occur:**

  * Review the specific failure messages for guidance

  * Consider retraining robots if websites have changed significantly

  * Use the "Update Session Cookies" feature for login-required sites

  * Contact support for persistent or complex issues

**Integration setup:**

  * Connect to Google Sheets, Airtable, or other tools for automatic updates

  * Set up webhook notifications for real-time alerts

  * Use API integration for custom workflows

  * Configure Zapier or other automation tools for complex workflows

## What are Browse AI's current limitations with changing websites?

**CAPTCHA handling:**

  * Supports standard ReCaptcha and hCaptcha

  * Does **not** support custom CAPTCHAs

  * May require manual intervention for complex security challenges

**High-security websites:**

  * Some sites have advanced bot detection that Browse AI cannot bypass

  * Particularly challenging for sites requiring complex authentication

  * May not work with all 2FA/MFA implementations

**Technical constraints:**

  * Cookie consent banners can cause failures if they appear differently than during training

  * Some websites reject cookies from different IP addresses

  * Complex JavaScript applications may require specific handling

  * Frequent A/B testing can cause inconsistent behavior

**When manual intervention may be needed:**

  * Major website redesigns or structural changes

  * New security measures or bot detection systems

  * Changes to login processes or authentication requirements

  * Custom or unusual website implementations

## What support is available when robots stop working?

**Automatic features:**

  * **Failure notifications** keep you informed of issues

  * **Usage reports** show success rates and performance trends

  * **Retry logic** handles temporary failures automatically

  * **Guidance links** in notifications help improve performance

**Self-service options:**

  * **Robot retraining** tools for updating existing robots

  * **Documentation** for troubleshooting common issues

  * **Settings adjustments** for authentication and monitoring

  * **Bulk operations** for managing multiple robots

**Support channels:**

  * **Email support** for all users (basic support on free plans)

  * **Priority email support** for Professional plan users

  * **Live chat support** available for higher-tier plans

  * **Detailed help articles** for common scenarios

**Professional services:**

  * **Managed services** available for complex extraction needs

  * **Custom robot development** for challenging websites

  * **Dedicated support** for business-critical applications

  * **Custom integrations** and data transformation services

**Important:** Success rates and robot performance can vary significantly
based on website complexity, security measures, and change frequency. While
Browse AI includes AI-powered features to help with adaptation, some scenarios
may require manual adjustments or alternative approaches.

* * *

Does Browse AI offer location based extraction for region dynamic content?

# Does Browse AI offer location based extraction for region dynamic content?

Yes, Browse AI includes geolocation capabilities that allow you to extract
region-specific content as it appears to users in different countries.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 3 months ago

Table of contents

Browse AI automatically manages geographic access through:

  * IP rotation: Uses IP addresses from the country where you created the robot

  * Proxy management: Built-in proxy rotation for reliable access

  * Regional routing: Accesses region-specific versions of websites

  * Automatic handling: No manual proxy configuration needed

💡 Robots use IP addresses from your country automatically. Once you configure
and approve your robot, you can customize the geo-location of your robot in
your robot's settings.

This is particularly useful for e-commerce price monitoring, region-specific
content extraction, and market research across different geographic markets.

# How do I customize the geolocation of a robot?

You can configure the location for your robot through the robot settings.

  1. [Train ](10496382-how-to-train-a-robot-to-scrape-or-monitor-data.html)and [approve your robot](https://approv/).

  2. Go to **Settings** , and scroll down to **Advanced Options**.

  3. Select the location from the drop-down menu and click **Save settings.**

Your robot will now run its tasks and monitors to capture and check for data
based on that location.

💡 If you need to run tasks and monitors for multiple locations, it's best to
have different robots for each country.

[![](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1846940801/d034fedb45d8d6719dd1b1e27769/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image6620.png?expires=1771546500&signature=f0ec9c054df5ca3f8759d0d2647fa8fcddcc4adac8f0459fed12758990654db5&req=dSgjEMB6nYlfWPMW1HO4zYOOS7vUjpKtjEMhVClGDPQUBk3H%2Be5flZXUpTjY%0Ax28bHGhw0gkGaSvFstQ%3D%0A)](../../../downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/bcyfauog/1846940801/d034fedb45d8d6719dd1b1e27769/image.png_%3b%20filename_%3dUTF-8%27%27image6620.png?expires=1771546500&signature=f0ec9c054df5ca3f8759d0d2647fa8fcddcc4adac8f0459fed12758990654db5&req=dSgjEMB6nYlfWPMW1HO4zYOOS7vUjpKtjEMhVClGDPQUBk3H%2Be5flZXUpTjY%0Ax28bHGhw0gkGaSvFstQ%3D%0A)

# Geographic coverage

Coverage level| Availability| Details  
---|---|---  
**Your country**|  All plans| Automatic, no setup  
**Other countries**|  Premium plans| Select from dropdown  
**City-level**|  Premium plans| Contact support  
**Restricted regions**|  Not available| Technical/legal limits  
  
☎️ You can configure robots to extract data by country. If you require
stricter regional based data extractions (city, state, province), [talk to our
sales team](https://www.browse.ai/service) to learn more about our Premium
options.

# What you can extract by region

## E-commerce and pricing

  * Regional price differences

  * Local currency pricing

  * Product availability by country

  * Shipping costs and options

  * Regional promotions and deals

## Localized content

  * Country-specific news

  * Regional search results

  * Local business listings

  * Language-specific content

  * Geographic compliance notices

## Market research data

  * Competitive pricing by market

  * Regional product catalogs

  * Local service availability

  * Market-specific strategies

  * Regional customer reviews

## Common use cases

**Use case**| **What to extract**| **Why it matters**  
---|---|---  
Price monitoring| Product prices across countries| Identify arbitrage
opportunities  
Market analysis| Regional product availability| Understand market differences  
Competitive research| Local competitor offerings| Track regional strategies  
Compliance checking| Regional legal notices| Ensure proper disclosures  
Content localization| Marketing messages by region| Study localization
approaches  
  
* * *

How to set up scheduled exports for AWS S3

# How to set up scheduled exports for AWS S3

Scheduled exports automatically send your robot's data to AWS S3 on a regular
schedule, eliminating the need for manual data exports after each robot run.

M

Written by Melissa Shires

Updated over 2 months ago

Table of contents

# Prerequisites

Before setting up scheduled exports, ensure you have:

  * An approved robot that has extracted data.

  * [AWS S3 integration already configured for your robot.](10715592-integrating-browse-ai-with-amazon-s3.html)

🌱 This feature is still in Beta. To unlock this feature for your account,
please contact [[email protected]](../../cdn-cgi/l/email-
protection.html#87f4f2f7f7e8f5f3c7e5f5e8f0f4e2a9e6ee).

# How to create a scheduled export

## **Step 1: Create scheduled export**

  1. Navigate to your robot's dashboard.

  2. Click the Tables tab.

  3. Find your configured AWS S3 integration.

  4. Click the three-dot menu (⋯) next to the Export button.

  5. Select "Create scheduled export" from the dropdown.

## **Step 2: Configure your export schedule**

In the "Create scheduled export" modal, set up your preferences:

### **Name your export**

  * Enter a descriptive name to easily identify this scheduled export.

### **Set export frequency**

  * Export once every: Enter the interval number (1, 2, 3, etc.).

  * Select the time unit from the dropdown (Day, Week, or Month).

### **Choose export days**

  * Select which days of the week to run your exports.

  * Click "Deselect All" to clear selections and choose specific days.

  * For daily exports, select all relevant weekdays.

### **Set export timing**

  * Check "Anytime" for flexible scheduling.

  * Or uncheck to specify an exact time and select your timezone.

### **Advanced scheduling (optional)**

  * Click "Switch to advanced scheduling (RRULE)" for complex scheduling patterns.

  * Use RRULE format for irregular schedules or specific date requirements.

## **Step 3: Save your scheduled export**

  1. Review your settings.

  2. Click **Save scheduling** to activate the scheduled export.

# Managing your scheduled exports

## **View active exports**

Your scheduled exports appear in the "Scheduled Exports" section below your
integration options. This section shows:

  * Export names and current schedules.

  * Status of each scheduled export.

## **Modify export settings**

To change your AWS S3 configuration:

  1. Click "Configure integration" below the scheduled exports.

  2. Update your S3 bucket details, file naming, or other export preferences.

# Export scheduling best practices

## **Align with your data collection**

  * Schedule exports to run after your robot completes its monitoring tasks.

  * Allow sufficient time between data collection and export for processing.

## **Consider your workflow needs**

  * Daily exports: ideal for time-sensitive data and regular monitoring.

  * Weekly exports: suitable for periodic analysis and reporting.

  * Monthly exports: perfect for archival purposes and long-term trends.

## **Optimize for efficiency**

  * Set exports during off-peak hours to avoid potential delays.

  * Use specific timing rather than "Anytime" for predictable data delivery.

  * Consider your team's timezone when scheduling exports.

# Troubleshooting scheduled exports

## **Export not running**

  * Verify your robot is actively monitoring and collecting data.

  * Check that your AWS S3 integration is properly configured.

  * Ensure your AWS credentials have proper write permissions.

## **Missing or incomplete data**

  * Confirm your robot's monitoring schedule allows data collection before export time.

  * Check if your robot has sufficient credits for regular operations.

  * Verify your robot is successfully extracting data during scheduled runs.

## **AWS S3 connection issues**

  * Validate your S3 bucket permissions and access keys.

  * Ensure your S3 bucket exists and is accessible.

  * Check that your AWS region settings are correct.

* * *
